title: Assistant Manager, Corporate Communications
raw_description:
Job Summary:

This role supports the Corporate Communications team in the areas of publicity, media relations, digital and social media content development. He/She will also assist in analytics reporting as well as support in crisis and issues communications.


Job Description:

  • Media relations – Draft and issue news releases, facilitate media queries and interviews, media pitching, organise media briefings and events, maintain media databases and distribution lists
  • Digital communications – Manage and enhance existing digital and social media channels, and develop new ones where appropriate
  • Content development – Develop print, visual and multimedia content (copy, videos, graphics and presentations) across various platforms
  • Corporate marketing – Manage production of corporate collaterals, corporate advertising and branding materials, as well as coordinate corporate award submissions
  • Corporate events – Plan and manage corporate activities and communication initiatives
  • Crisis and issues communications – Support the development of holding statements, Q&As and response materials
  • Reporting and analytics – Track and analyse media coverage, sentiment, communications impact and social media traction; support the development of corporate and financial reporting materials such as financial results commentaries, quarterly business updates and presentations


Job Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in communications or a related discipline
  • Minimum 3 years of relevant experience in corporate communications and public relations, with exposure to a broad range of communications functions
  • Strong writing and content development skills across media, digital, print and social platforms, with the ability to adapt style and tone (corporate, strategic, creative)
  • Strong project management and organisational skills; able to coordinate across various departments, manage vendors and liaise with external stakeholders
  • Meticulous and detail-oriented

Requirements

City Developments Limited (CDL) is committed to fostering an inclusive culture that respects the diversity of its employees and stakeholders. As a signatory of the Employers Pledge for Fair Employment with TAFEP since 2008, CDL’s recruitment process adheres to strict guidelines on non-discrimination and fairness, regardless of gender, ethnicity, religion, or age.

title: Vet Nurse
raw_description:
🐾 We’re Hiring: Veterinary Nurse
Location: Holland Village
Employment Type: Part / Full-Time


About Us
At OmniVet, we pride ourselves on providing compassionate, high-quality veterinary care for pets and outstanding support for their owners. Our team is close-knit, supportive, and committed to delivering excellent service — and we’re looking for a Veterinary Nurse to join us.


Position Overview
The Veterinary Nurse plays a vital role in assisting veterinarians, supporting patients, and ensuring smooth day-to-day operations in the clinic. You’ll be hands-on with patient care, help during procedures, and be a reassuring presence for both pets and their owners.


Key Responsibilities

  • Assist veterinarians during consultations, surgeries, and treatments
  • Administer medications, vaccinations, and prescribed treatments
  • Monitor patients pre- and post-surgery
  • Perform diagnostic tests such as bloodwork and urine analysis
  • Educate pet owners on preventive care and post-treatment instructions
  • Maintain cleanliness and organization of the clinic
  • Ensure accurate and up-to-date medical records

Requirements

  • Diploma or certification in Veterinary Nursing (or equivalent)
  • Prior experience in a veterinary clinic is preferred but not essential — fresh graduates welcome
  • Strong teamwork and communication skills
  • Compassion, patience, and attention to detail
  • Willingness to work weekends or evenings on rotation

What We Offer

  • Competitive salary and staff benefits
  • On-the-job training and continuous learning opportunities
  • Supportive and collaborative work culture
  • Staff discounts on pet care services and products

How to Apply
Email your resume and cover letter to OmniVet.hr@gmail.com with the subject line "Application – Veterinary Nurse".

Join our team and help us provide the best care possible for pets and their families! 🐶🐱

title: Retail Sales Associate
raw_description:
Retail Associate

Home & Décor Superstore (Wholly owned by King Koil Singapore)

Please join our team if you are a service orientated and hardworking sales associate with furniture retail experience. We also welcome those who are keen but without related experience or fresh from schools. Every of our King Koil Ashley Furniture Homestore carry a full range of furniture and a complete King Koil Mattress Gallery.


Job Responsibilities:

  • Serves customers by helping them select products
  • Drive sales through engagement of customers, suggestive selling and sharing product knowledge.
  • Responds to customers’ questions ·
  • Able to share store promotion mechanics and merchandise with customers
  • Maintain and upkeep of showroom display items in proper order, assist with receiving and stocking merchandise.
  • Able to use the computerized Point of Sales system to close orders.
  • Training will be provided in both products and operations.

Job Requirements & Skills:

  • Fresh graduate/ Minimum Higher Secondary education or ITE and a fast leaner can apply.
  • Those with related furniture retail sales experience will be an advantage.
  • Bilingual in English & Mandarin, good listening and possess good people skills
  • A good team player with positive attitude
  • Must be result oriented and able to work independently

Additional Information:

  • Remuneration – Basic salary package with commission.
  • Full time job- 6 working days a week with 6 off days per month, required to work on Public holidays and weekend.
  • Working hour: 11am to 8pm.
  • On job Training and uniform will be provided
  • Medical benefits / insurance

title: Electrical Engineer
raw_description:
Reporting to: Project Manager

Package: Dependent upon experience

Working Place: Tuas / Pasir Panjang / Jurong port terminal


Purpose of the Job

Responsible for warranty support, additional projects and services. Collaborates with sales, service, engineering, and technical support to ensure projects are completed successfully. To perform commissioning activities and accept electrical systems into service completing pre and post checks, developing test schedules and Quality Control records for container handling equipment. Testing and tuning of equipment, load shedding and network control systems. Conduct initial drive runs. Plan stepwise commissioning details according to the overall procedure and instruct commissioning crews and or subcontractor for the necessary action.


Job Content and Context

1. Implementation of tasks assigned by Project Manager

2. Coordinates closely with Project Manager by communicating project status and success metrics.

3. Maintain follow up with the customer to provide additional information or to resolve problems.

4. Assist with the commissioning of new equipment into service. Ensure that equipment is operating according to specifications.

5. Obtain and adhere to testing and commissioning plan for container handling equipment.

6. Obtain commissioning plans to integrate and verify Drive, PLC and control system and sub-systems in accordance with compliance matrices.

7. Adhere to accepted Drive and PLC, automation system standards design. Seek operational data and operations-based feedback, provide comprehensive and useful documentation and assures the availability of adequate spares for efficient warranty support.

8. Supervising the technicians to carry out the job according to the work procedure.

9. Train and mentor technicians in the Drive and PLC, automation and control system to assure systems and equipment perform to expectations.

10. Repair and resets/adjustments are carried out, mindful of effects on, or unnecessary loss of, other equipment.

11. Diagnoses Drive and PLC, control system fault at 24 hours on-call basis.

12. Provide and maintain detailed Electrical, Drive and PLC, control system, automation system documentation on new design and modifications including engineering change notices to assure documentation is available when needed.

13. Occupational Health and Safety standards, statutory requirements, relevant codes of practice, manufacturers’ specifications, environmental requirements and enterprise procedures are identified and monitored throughout the work procedure.


Performance Standards

1. Targets set by the Project Manager are achieved within the timescales agreed.

2. Applies knowledge and skills when Establishing plans, Describing consequences and completing tasks, Identifying improvements, Applying safety precautions relevant to the task, Assessing operational capability of equipment used and work processes selected

3. Follows ZPMC and regulatory requirements in all commissioning, testing, diagnostic and repair operations

4. Shows evidence of application of relevant workplace procedures including: Job procedures and work instructions, Relevant guidelines relating to the use of equipment, Quality assurance procedures / policy, Reporting of unsafe or damaged equipment

5. Takes action promptly- accidents and incidents reported following ZPMC procedures

6. Completes work systematically with attention to detail and without damage of goods, equipment or personnel

7. Is capable of adapting to differing work conditions and circumstances (E.g. Climatic, light or dark, etc.)


Must have:

1. Good to have Diploma and above in Electrical/Electronic Engineering.

2. Fresh graduates are welcome.

3. Communicating effectively with others

4. Team player, confident and pro-active

5. Articulate and focused

6. Enthusiastic and flexible

7. Excellent written and oral communication skills

8. Must be able to work at elevated heights and outdoor working environment

9. 5.5days work per week

10. Own transportation

title: Chef de Partie (Baker)
raw_description:
We are seeking a motivated Junior Baker to support our central kitchen team. This role involves assisting with the preparation, baking, and finishing of breads, pastries, and other bakery products while learning professional baking techniques.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in preparing and baking a variety of bakery items.
  • Measure, mix, and handle ingredients under supervision.
  • Operate and clean baking equipment safely.
  • Maintain cleanliness and follow food safety standards.
  • Support senior bakers and follow daily production schedules.

Requirements

  • Some experience in a bakery, kitchen, or culinary training preferred (fresh graduates welcome).
  • Willingness to learn and follow instructions.
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced, team environment.
  • Flexible with early morning or weekend shifts.
  • Passion for baking and attention to detail.

Perks

  • Birthday Leave
  • 5 day work week
  • Staff Discount

title: Software Quality Assurance & Testing Analyst [Automation & Manual Testing; SIT; UAT; PROD] (Banking Wealth Management Application)
raw_description:
Project: Wealth Management System Implementation - Bank Sector Client.

You will play a crucial role in driving testing efforts for the programme. This includes defining, developing, documenting, executing and reporting on test approaches and plans; collaborating closely with project teams as well as other relevant stakeholders such as end users, subject matter experts, technical teams etc.


Job Description

Your role will require close collaboration across multiple areas within the organization including but not limited to:

1. Project Management Office (PMO)

2. Information Technology (IT)

3. Product Development & Strategy (PD&S)

4. Business Units / Divisions (BU/Ds)

5. Operations (Ops)

6. Risk Management

8. Other relevant units as needed


You will need to develop expertise in understanding the business processes and functions supported by the new system as well as conduct detailed reviews of user requirements and specifications. You will also need to develop and execute test plans, procedures and scenarios to ensure that the system meets all requirements and works seamlessly with other systems.


Responsibilities:

• Define, develop, document, execute and report on test approaches and plans.

• Collaborate closely with project teams as well as other relevant stakeholders such as end users, subject matter experts, technical teams etc.

• Develop and maintain comprehensive test plans for each release, which cover functional and non-functional requirements.

• Design and implement automated and manual test scripts for both short-term and long-term needs.

• Conduct unit, integration and performance tests using various tools and frameworks.

• Provide regular status updates on testing progress and issues.

• Identify and track software defects and work with developers to resolve them.

• Ensure compliance with quality standards and best practices throughout the testing process.

• Support user acceptance testing (UAT) activities.

• Execute ad hoc queries and perform exploratory testing as needed.



Qualifications:

• Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field.

• Minimum 3 years of experience in IT testing methodologies, tools and techniques.

• Experience in designing and implementing test automation solutions.

• Familiarity with Agile development methodologies.

• Good communication skills and ability to interact effectively with cross-functional teams.

• Ability to analyze complex problems and propose innovative solutions.

• Excellent attention to detail and strong organizational skills.



Good To Have:

- Automation Software Testing: TOSCA; Selenium; Appium

- Jira; Confluence: AS400 Test Data Preparation; AS400 Host Data; SQL Query

- Domain Knowledge: Unit Trusts; Equities; Forex; Wealth Portfolio Management; Insurance; CRM MS365 Dynamics; Sibel CRM Application

title: Sales Engineer (Valves/ Pumps)
raw_description:
Responsibilities:

  • In charge of generating new leads
  • Responsible in providing professional services to the existing and new clients through consultative selling, cross-selling or up-selling the company’s products and/or services.
  • In charge of establishing and maintaining strong and lasting relationships with clients by regularly updating them with company’s new products and introducing innovative solutions
  • In charge of providing continuing pre-sales and post-sales support in managing and meeting their expectations.
  • In charge of developing and customising relevant materials and presentations that explain key product features and viable technical solutions to benefit clients and prospects.
  • In charge of maintaining a high degree of technical knowledge to effectively engage in business discussions with clients across industries, and to co-create feasible solutions to address any of their engineering concerns throughout the selling process.
  • Responsible in managing accounts effectively throughout the sales cycle
  • In charge of preparing costing, tenders, proposals and quotations to current and new clients.
  • Provide weekly report updates of sales, pipelines, leads, opportunities and sales activities

Requirements:

  • Min Diploma in Engineering
  • Min 3 years of working experience in doing sales in Refineries/Petrochemical Industry
  • Possess own transport will be advantage

To submit your application, please apply online to apply@rn-care.com or call +(65) 6513 5697 for private and confidential discussion.

Your interest will be treated with the strictest of confidence. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified or contacted.

title: Corporate Development Executive
raw_description:
Role Description

The Corporate Development Executive will play a critical role in driving Unicomp Group’s growth agenda through strategy definition, corporate development, transaction execution, and post-merger integration. Reporting directly to the VP of Corporate Development (Singapore-based), this role will partner closely with C-suite leaders, global and regional growth teams, and cross-functional stakeholders to identify, evaluate, and execute strategic M&A opportunities across China and international markets.


The successful candidate will be responsible for shaping business strategy, coordinating end-to-end M&A processes, and ensuring seamless integration of acquired businesses to maximize long-term value creation.


Key Responsibilities

  • Be part of the full M&A cycle: strategy formulation, deal sourcing, evaluation, execution, and integration.
  • Partner with C-suite business leaders to define strategic priorities, conduct market studies, and assess industry trends.
  • Identify and develop a strong pipeline of M&A opportunities in alignment with group growth objectives.
  • Prepare and present investment cases, proposals, and recommendations to the Steering and Investment Committees for approval.
  • Collaborate with internal business units and external advisors (banks, consultants, law firms) to manage due diligence and deal execution.
  • Act as a key contributor on cross-functional projects, providing strategic insights and actionable recommendations to senior management.
  • Coordinate post-merger integration efforts to ensure operational, cultural, and financial synergies are realized globally.

Job Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree or higher in Finance, Commerce, Engineering, or another quantitative discipline
  • Proven track record in M&A and corporate development, with hands-on experience across the deal lifecycle.
  • Background in investment banking, Big 4 transaction advisory, consulting, or corporate M&A roles preferred.
  • Strong business acumen, with the ability to translate strategy into actionable opportunities.
  • Excellent stakeholder management skills, with experience working directly with senior executives and external partners.
  • Strong communication and presentation skills, with fluency in English and Chinese (both written and spoken).
  • Highly motivated, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced environment.
  • We welcome applications from fresh graduates who are eager to learn and grow with us.

title: QA Engineer
raw_description:
Job Descriptions

1. Quality Control & Inspection:

  • Perform detailed QC buy-off inspections of precision-engineered parts, assemblies, or systems at various stages of production, utilizing industry-standard inspection techniques (e.g., CMM, visual inspection, functional testing).
  • Track non-conformance reports filed during the buy-off process and develop and implement preventive action plans to mitigate recurring defects.

2. Process Development

  • Design and implement quality assurance protocols, checklists, and testing procedures specific to precision engineering workflows.
  • Establish, update, and ensure adherence to quality documentation, including inspection reports and non-conformance records.
  • Coordinate and oversee the First Article Inspection (FAI) process for new manufacturing processes or parts, ensuring compliance with industry and customer standards.

3. Collaboration & Problem Solving

  • Work closely with design engineers to review technical drawings, specifications, and tolerances, providing feedback to enhance manufacturability and quality.
  • Drive cross-functional initiatives to enhance long-term quality and reliability, performing root cause analysis and implementing corrective actions.
  • Lead the tracking and investigation of quality-related returns and customer complaints using the 8D methodology, coordinating problem-solving efforts and implementing long-term preventive measures.

4. Compliance & Standards

  • Ensure all products meet industry standards (e.g., ISO 9001, ASME, or other relevant certifications) and customer requirements.
  • Participate in internal and external audits, ensuring adherence to regulatory and supplier quality standards.

5. Continuous Improvement

  • Analyze quality data and trends from issue tracking systems (Project Issue Log) to identify opportunities for process optimization and defect reduction. Classify issues into relevant disciplines such as quality, operational, or design-related concerns.
  • Host post-mortem discussions after project closures, documenting lessons learned and integrating preventive action plans into future workflows.

6. Documentation & Reporting

  • Maintain accurate records of inspections, test results, and quality metrics using digital Quality Management Systems (QMS).
  • Prepare detailed reports for management and clients on quality performance, compliance status, and improvement initiatives, providing actionable insights to drive quality excellence."

7. Any other Task Assigned by Direct reporting manager or Stage BU head.


Requirements

  • Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Precision Engineering, Quality Management, or a related field from a recognized institution.
  • Preferably 3-5 years of experience in quality control or quality assurance within precision engineering or manufacturing, with hands-on expertise in inspection techniques (e.g. CMM, visual inspection, functional testing).

Skill

  • Proficiency in interpreting technical drawings, specifications, and tolerances.
  • Experience with Quality Management Systems (QMS) and issue tracking tools (e.g., Project Issue Log).
  • Familiarity with First Article Inspection (FAI), root cause analysis (e.g., 8D methodology), and non-conformance reporting

Personal Attributes

  • Detail-Oriented: Exceptional attention to detail to ensure precision in inspections, documentation, and compliance with stringent quality standards.
  • Analytical Thinker: Strong problem-solving skills with a methodical approach to conducting root cause analysis and implementing effective corrective actions.
  • Collaborative Team Player: Ability to work seamlessly with cross-functional teams, including design engineers and production staff, fostering a cooperative and solution-driven environment.
  • Proactive and Initiative-Driven: A self-starter who anticipates quality issues, develops preventive measures, and drives continuous improvement without needing constant supervision.
  • Adaptable: Flexible and resilient, able to handle evolving priorities and additional tasks assigned by management in a fast-paced manufacturing setting.
  • Communicative: Clear and concise in conveying quality findings, preparing reports, and presenting insights to stakeholders at all levels.
  • Results-Focused: Committed to achieving high-quality outcomes, meeting deadlines, and ensuring customer satisfaction through reliable processes.
  • Ethical and Integrity-Driven: Upholds the highest standards of professionalism and accountability, ensuring compliance with industry regulations and organizational policies.
  • Calm Under Pressure: Maintains composure and sound judgment during audits, customer complaints, or production challenges, ensuring effective resolution.

title: Helpdesk Support Engineer
raw_description:
Position Overview

We are seeking a dedicated IT Support Specialist to join our team and ensure seamless technology operations across our organisation. This role combines technical expertise with excellent customer service skills to support end-users and maintain critical IT infrastructure across multiple locations on Sentosa Island.


Key Responsibilities


Infrastructure & Network Support

  • Maintain and support end-user computing devices (EUCDs) and network infrastructure, including LAN and WiFi systems.
  • Oversee back-end server operations to ensure optimal performance and reliability across all locations.

Security Management

  • Administer and manage anti-virus solutions, specifically Trend Micro and Apex One Office Scan systems.
  • Ensure all IT systems remain current with security patches for Windows, Intel drivers, and applications to maintain organisational security standards.

Service Desk Operations

  • Provide comprehensive helpdesk support through multiple channels including email, IT hotline, walk-in requests, and both onsite and remote assistance.
  • Maintain detailed logs of all incidents and support requests whilst ensuring timely resolution through our ITSM platform.

Technical Support & Deployment

  • Manage front-end client systems running Windows 11 and other operating systems, including imaging, deployment, and troubleshooting.
  • Provide technical assistance for hardware and software setup, configuration, and problem resolution.

Asset & Vendor Management

  • Maintain accurate IT asset management records, including asset tagging and proper disposal of equipment.
  • Coordinate with vendors for system setup, repairs, and maintenance activities whilst logging all cases appropriately.

Documentation & Reporting

  • Create clear technical documentation and user manuals with step-by-step instructions.
  • Maintain work-in-progress reports and ensure all activities are properly documented for knowledge sharing and compliance purposes.

Additional Support

  • Configure and support online video conferencing systems to facilitate effective communication across the organisation.
  • Undertake other duties as assigned by the IT Supervisor to support overall departmental objectives.

Required Qualifications

  • Diploma or degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field
  • Minimum 2-3 years of experience in IT support or helpdesk roles
  • Strong knowledge of Windows operating systems, particularly Windows 11
  • Experience with network troubleshooting (LAN/WiFi)
  • Familiarity with ITSM platforms and ticketing systems
  • Knowledge of anti-virus management systems (Trend Micro preferred)
  • Understanding of hardware and software troubleshooting methodologies

Preferred Qualifications

  • Professional certifications (CompTIA A+, Network+, or Microsoft certifications)
  • Experience with server administration
  • Knowledge of video conferencing systems
  • Asset management system experience
  • Previous experience in government or public sector environments

Key Competencies

  • Excellent problem-solving and analytical skills
  • Strong communication and interpersonal abilities
  • Customer service orientation with focus on user satisfaction
  • Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities
  • Attention to detail and commitment to documentation
  • Willingness to travel between multiple office locations
  • Adaptability to changing technology environments

raw_title: GRIT: Group Legal and Compliance, Transformation
title: GRIT: Group Legal and Compliance, Transformation
raw_description:
Why Join


Be at the heart of change within OCBC’s Group Legal & Compliance division. As a Transformation Programme Associate, you’ll help build a more efficient and forward-thinking legal and compliance function. You'll gain hands-on experience with cutting-edge technologies and contribute directly to impactful initiatives, shaping the future of risk management at Singapore’s longest-established bank.


How you succeed


You’ll thrive by proactively seeking opportunities to improve processes. Embrace a fast-paced environment and quickly learn new technologies. Success lies in your ability to collaborate effectively with diverse stakeholders, delivering on projects with meticulous attention to detail. A curious mindset and a willingness to experiment will be key in driving innovation.


What you do


You’ll be involved in a wide range of activities, including:

  • Process Mapping & Analysis: Document and analyse legal and compliance workflows, identifying areas for improvement.
  • Project Support: Track action items, maintain project documentation, and coordinate with cross-functional teams.
  • Tech Exploration: Build prototypes using tools like Power Automate, Power Apps, and Gen AI to demonstrate potential solutions.
  • Meeting & Communication: Schedule meetings, prepare materials, and assist with stakeholder communications.
  • Stakeholder Engagement: Support change management activities and facilitate buy-in for new processes.
  • Continuous Learning: Stay updated on industry trends and participate in relevant training programs.

Who you work with


Group Legal & Compliance takes care of integrity and ensures compliance with all rules and regulations, developing the policies & procedures that manage and mitigate risk. In an evolving world where we’re actively driving digital transformation, it’s no easy task. But we thrive on the challenge because we’re future-ready and focused on driving change.


Who you are

  • A recent graduate from a Polytechnic or University with a diploma or degree in the relevant field.
  • Possess a Business, Information Systems, or a related discipline.
  • Demonstrate a proactive approach and a strong work ethic.
  • Exhibit a keen interest in emerging technologies and process improvement.
  • Showcase strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Possess excellent communication and interpersonal abilities.

Learning Objectives


This role will provide you with:

  • Hands-on experience with process mapping, analysis, and improvement techniques.
  • Practical skills in project management and execution.
  • Exposure to cutting-edge technologies like Power Automate, Power Apps, and Gen AI.
  • Opportunities to collaborate with stakeholders at all levels.
  • A deeper understanding of legal and compliance operations within a leading financial institution.

Who we are


As Singapore’s longest established bank, we have been dedicated to enabling individuals and businesses to achieve their aspirations since 1932. How? By taking the time to truly understand people. From there, we provide support, services, solutions, and career paths that meet their individual needs and desires.

Today, we’re on a journey of transformation. Leveraging technology and creativity to become a future-ready learning organisation. But for all that change, our strategic ambition is consistently clear and bold, which is to be Asia’s leading financial services partner for a sustainable future.

We invite you to build the bank of the future. Innovate the way we deliver financial services. Work in friendly, supportive teams. Build lasting value in your community. Help people grow their assets, business, and investments. Take your learning as far as you can. Or simply enjoy a vibrant, future-ready career. Your Opportunity Starts Here.


What we offer


Competitive base salary. A suite of holistic, flexible benefits to suit every lifestyle. Community initiatives. Industry-leading learning and professional development opportunities. Equal opportunity. Fair employment. Selection based on ability and fit with our culture and values. Your wellbeing, growth and aspirations are every bit as cared for as the needs of our customers.

title: Digital Marketing Executive Internship
raw_description:
Do you have a passion for helping businesses transform? If yes, then this role is for you!

​

We are looking for talents who are passionate about the consulting and digital marketing space to join our exciting, 9-year-old, award-winning consulting and digital marketing agency. As a Digital Marketing Executive intern in a multi-cultural agency, you will be exposed to various client projects ranging from B2C and B2B businesses across Singapore and the Asia Pacific region.

​

Apart from working with clients from a wide spectrum of industries, he or she will have the opportunity to work closely alongside the Founder and team to expand the practice and support businesses as they embark on a transformational journey to navigate the complexities and uncertainties of their business environment. From strategic consulting to 360° digital marketing and training services, our team is dedicated to offering performance-driven and customer-centric solutions to help our clients achieve their desired business outcomes.

​

For more information, visit us at https://www.vktransformation.com/


What You Will Learn

The Digital Marketing Executive intern will experience exponential learning growth if he or she can thrive in the fast-paced and experimental nature of an innovative firm. There will be opportunities for him or her to work on different projects and enjoy the excitement of analytical problem-solving, critical thinking & innovation across the Asia Pacific region.


​Duties & Responsibilities

  • Work closely with the team to develop digital marketing strategies and plans for the firm and projects. This includes online marketplaces, social media, content management, and online lead-generation strategies.
  • Conduct market research and benchmarks to provide insights into the market conditions, consumer behaviour, customer insights, customer segmentation, pricing, and competition landscape for target markets for a variety of clients across multiple industries to uncover new opportunities.
  • Design & execute social media strategy for VK Transformation and client projects with the support of the Seniors.
  • Develop brand awareness or promote a single product, service or company.
  • Execute digital marketing campaigns across relevant platforms.
  • Work closely with the seniors to develop and execute digital marketing strategies and plans for the firm and projects. This includes online marketplace management, social media, content management and online lead generation strategies.

The successful candidate should possess the following qualifications and qualities:

  • Proven digital marketing internship experience, undergoing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in business, Marketing, Economics (majoring in Marketing) or any other relevant discipline.
  • Singaporeans or Singapore PRs are encouraged to apply.
  • Experience in B2B or B2C marketing, planning and customer engagement is preferred.​
  • High proficiency in Word, Excel and PowerPoint.
  • Strong research, analytical and quantitative abilities with excellent written and verbal communication skills, preferably the ability to develop and deliver consulting presentations. Fluency in English is mandatory.
  • Possesses a strong interest in social media across Google, LinkedIn, Meta, TikTok platforms is preferred.
  • Comfortable working with a wide spectrum of analytical tools eg. Google Analytics, Adwords, Facebook Business Manager, Shopee, Amazon, Lazada etc. with the ability to analyse digital data and draw actionable insights.
  • Strong passion for working in a startup business environment.
  • Strong team player, open-minded, enthusiastic and eager to learn.

The coverage of the job description is highly dependent on the time spent with the company.

​

Please kindly send your CV to info@vktransformation.com. Your interest will be treated with strict confidence.

title: Junior Wealth Management Banker - Financial Advisory (Singapore Bank Client - Perm Hire)
raw_description:
What we are looking for (Bank Sector Client - Singapore):

- Perm Hire Directly With Local Bank in Singapore

- Education Requirements: Diploma / Degree Graduates without relevant work experience/certifications in wealth management
- Open to candidates without relevant experience as there will be training provided

About the Department:

The Retail banking function provides Personal and Business Banking customers with financial solutions tailored to their needs. We help them manage their money wisely and meet their financial goals through an extensive range of products and services. These include deposit, insurance, secured and unsecured loans, investment and wealth advisory services. Personal Financial Services: We design and provide products and services that empower our customers to enjoy greater banking convenience while they extract more value from their money. In particular, we are focused on promoting and developing healthy saving and investment practices across generations.

We are looking for driven individuals who...

  • Enjoys front line sales
  • Are motivated by rewards & recognition
  • Desire fast track career progression

With a structured training program provided, you will...

  • Create distinctive banking experiences for our clients
  • Provide expert financial advice on our clients' wealth journey
  • Leverage on our Bank's network to meet and achieve sales targets

Job Responsibilities

As a Personal Wealth Executive within the Personal Banking business, you will be responsible for rendering financial advisory to BANK'S mass-market customers. You are expected to achieve individual sales target while ensuring accurate and timely completion of the entire sales and compliance process.

  • Provide prudent financial advisory and investment solutions to meet the financial planning needs and requirements of BANK's clients
  • Develop and deepen relationships with clients who have been assigned to you
  • Ensure proper advisory procedures and documentation while adhering to the Bank’s internal policies, business processes and regulatory requirements (i.e. FAA and other related statutory Acts)

Job Requirements

  • A recognised university degree
  • Results-oriented, dynamic and self-driven
  • Competent in engaging and managing internal and external clients
  • Proven ability to achieve sales target
  • Resourceful and able to work with minimal supervision

==========================================

About us: D L Resources Pte Ltd is a leading provider of IT Professional Services & Banking outsourced staffing solutions, serving a diverse portfolio of clients across various industries including Financial Services Institutions, Banks & MNCs.

==========================================

Interested candidates may reach out directly to our recruiters (Edwin: +65 8 8 3 3 0 1 9 2 | EA License No: 24C2333 | EA Personnel No: R24123520)

title: Audit Associate
raw_description:
Job Description & Requirements


Job Highlights

· Friendly and caring colleagues and managers

· Proximity to MRT stations

· Good starting salary with annual increment and bonus

· Good benefits and working environment

· Good career progression

· 5 days work week

· Friendly culture, understanding management, open concept office

· Opportunities for quality learning and experience

· Offers challenging career and great career opportunities ahead

· Opportunities to travel overseas for work after traveling restrictions are relaxed


Responsibilities

· Perform statutory and special audits (fieldwork, documentation of results)

· Prepare audited financial statements in accordance with SFRS

· Prepare tax computations in accordance with the Singapore Income Tax Act

· Assist to prepare an audit plan for an external audit

· Support Senior or Manager in audit planning, fieldwork, and audit reporting; prepare reports and communicate findings and recommendations to clients

· Perform internal control reviews and special purpose auditing projects

· Effectively communicate any audit issues in a timely and organised manner

· Assist in completing any other assigned tasks

· Undertake specific stages of audit work under supervision by Audit Senior and Manager

· Assist to evaluate the audit evidence obtained and perform audit procedures to meet the objectives of external audit engagements

· Assist Seniors or Manager in the planning, execution and finalization of all areas of audit for SMEs and listed entities


Requirements

· Partial ACCA or degree holder.

· 1 to 2 years' relevant experience is preferred for audit associate

· Diploma, Advanced/Higher/Graduate Diploma

· Minimum of Diploma in Accountancy and working towards ACCA or Bachelor's Degree in Accountancy or related field

· Proficiency in Microsoft Office software programs such as MS Word, MS Excel and Office 365

· Professionalism, dependability, integrity and trustworthiness combined with a cooperative attitude

· Highly motivated self-starter with ability to multi-task and complete assignments within time constraints and deadlines

Interested candidates are invited to apply online


Only shortlisted candidates will be notified. Thank you for applying.

title: Veterinary Nurse
raw_description:
Paws N Claws Veterinary Surgery is an established Fear-Free concept clinic, now expanding and recruiting nurses for both locations

We pride ourselves in providing excellent veterinary care with desirous working environment, touching the lives of both our patients and clients. We see a range of animals from small furry pocket pets to avians


Passionate about working up cases in-house and are well equipped with a wide range of equipment to help us, such as, digital radiography, ultrasonography, iM3 GS dental unit, in-house IDEXX biochemistry and haematology machines, surgery room with isoflurane-anaesthetic machine and many more.

Providing a separate consult room for cats to lower stress and have an enthusiastic and supportive team of FT nurses and several PT members. We are lucky to have a hospital close by should we need any specialist advice or referrals.


Veterinary Nurses/Technicians

We are on the look for experienced and dedicated nurses to join us. You would be working as part of an experienced team. This is an exciting opportunity to fully utilise and develop your skills and gain extensive knowledge of specialist in-patient care.

You would be expected to carry out the following duties but not limited to:

  • Triaging and administrative work
  • Handling and restrain of animals
  • Surgical preparation and monitoring
  • Hospitalisation care
  • In house diagnostics procedures
  • Cleaning and maintaining of the clinic
  • Good communication and intiative

The position offered would be full time and rotated with our new location at Yishun

4 shift from 10am - 11pm with 2 1/2hr break.


Requirements:

- Inititative and good communication is preferred

– No experience needed but some animal handling experience is preferred.

– A diploma or degree equivalent in veterinary nursing or 2 or more years’ experience post qualification would be advantageous but is not required.

– Good interpersonal and communication skills.

– Possess drive, initiative and ability to work independently as well as in a team.

– Able to work on weekends

The successful candidate can expect:

– Renumeration based on skill and potential

– Mentorship and guidance

– Up to 18 days Paid Annual Leave.

– Paid study leave

– Opportunities for Continuous Progress Development



Sounds like the job for you? Email your CV and a cover letter to info.pnc2020@gmail.com now! We regret to inform that only successful candidates will be contacted

title: Junior Network Engineer
raw_description:
Position Overview


We are seeking a passionate and proactive Junior Network Engineer to join our rapidly growing team in Singapore. You will work closely with senior engineers to maintain and optimize the network environments of our core clients—especially top-tier banks and financial institutions—and participate in delivering impactful network projects.


We value strong foundational IT knowledge and professional work ethics, which will be key to your success and long-term growth with us.
Outstanding students looking for internship opportunities are also welcome to apply.


Key Responsibilities

Network Support and Maintenance

  • Serve as first- or second-level support to respond to and resolve clients’ daily network requests and incidents in a clear and professional manner.
  • Conduct regular network health checks and inspections with precision and complete all related technical documentation accurately.
  • Assist in monitoring clients’ network performance, proactively report anomalies, and ensure network stability and availability.

Project Delivery and Implementation

  • Under the guidance of senior engineers, assist in on-site implementation of client network projects, including basic configuration and testing.
  • Demonstrate a strong sense of responsibility by ensuring implementation processes comply with standards and maintain detailed project documentation throughout.

Operations Collaboration and Learning

  • Work closely with the team and demonstrate strong teamwork and resilience when handling critical incidents.
  • Maintain a strong desire to learn, proactively master mainstream network technologies and operational processes, and prepare for relevant professional certifications.

Requirements


Education and Experience

  • Diploma or higher in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related fields.
  • 1–2 years of relevant work experience, or outstanding fresh graduates with strong learning ability and hands-on spirit.
  • Possession of HCIA, CCNA, or equivalent network certification is preferred.

Technical Skills


Core Networking

  • Solid understanding of TCP/IP, OSPF, BGP, VLAN, STP, and other core network principles.
  • Basic configuration and troubleshooting skills for mainstream network devices (e.g., Huawei, Cisco).

Systems and Security Fundamentals (Preferred)

  • Basic understanding of Windows Server and/or Linux systems, including common commands.
  • Awareness of fundamental network security concepts, including firewall policies and ACLs.
  • Familiarity with ITIL or other standard IT operations frameworks is an advantage.

Core Competencies and Soft Skills

  • Meticulous and disciplined: Strong attention to detail, adherence to procedures, and documentation accuracy.
  • Excellent learning and adaptability: Quick to grasp new technologies and processes; able to thrive in a fast-paced environment.
  • Clear communication and teamwork: Strong written and verbal communication skills; effective collaboration with teammates and clients.
  • Customer service mindset and accountability: Understand the importance of client satisfaction; take full ownership of assigned tasks and issues.
  • Process-driven and organized: Good time management and multitasking abilities with a structured approach to work.

What We Offer

  • A fast-growing platform with opportunities to work directly with international top-tier banking clients.
  • Hands-on exposure to large-scale and complex network projects under the guidance of experienced engineers.
  • A clear career development path and ongoing technical training support.
  • Competitive salary and benefits package.
  • Internship opportunities with potential conversion to full-time roles based on performance.

title: LLM Engineer / AI Engineer
raw_description:
Key Responsibilities

  • Participate in core research, development, and integration of LLM-based projects.
  • Design, train, fine-tune, and deploy models based on Transformer architectures (e.g., GPT, Gemini, LLaMA, Deepseek).
  • Independently handle model optimization, evaluation, and inference performance improvements.
  • Conduct prompt engineering to enhance model controllability, consistency, and safety.
  • Implement evaluation, safety, and reliability mechanisms for LLMs, ensuring robust deployment in production environments.
  • Build agentic AI systems by integrating APIs, contextual memory, and reasoning capabilities to accomplish complex tasks.
  • Collaborate with product and engineering teams to deliver real-world AI applications, including automation, analytics, and intelligent assistants.
  • Stay updated on advancements in open-source LLM ecosystems and contribute to model research or open-source communities when applicable.

Qualifications

Education & Background

  • Bachelor’s degree or above in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, or a related field.
  • Around 2 years of experience in AI SaaS or LLM-based application development, including model fine-tuning, deployment, or integration.
  • Prior project experience in the education, legal, or financial sectors will be an advantage.

Technical Skills

  • Deep understanding of LLM architectures (e.g., Transformer, RAG, instruction-tuning, quantization).
  • Proficiency in Python and at least one AI framework (PyTorch, TensorFlow, or similar).
  • Practical experience in prompt engineering, fine-tuning, or reinforcement learning with human feedback (RLHF).
  • Skilled in model evaluation metrics (accuracy, hallucination control, bias testing, safety alignment).
  • Experience deploying models via cloud AI services (AWS, Azure, GCP) or containerized environments.
  • Familiarity with LangChain, MCP protocol, or agentic frameworks is a plus.
  • Open-source contributions or published AI research papers are highly valued.

Other Requirements

  • Strong communication skills in both English and Mandarin; ability to communicate effectively with regional colleagues and partners is an advantage.
  • Able to work independently and learn new technologies quickly.
  • Highly self-motivated, with the ability to deliver results in a fast-paced environment.
  • Passionate about AI-driven product development and intelligent systems.

What We Offer

  • Competitive compensation and growth-oriented equity incentives.
  • Opportunities to work alongside leading AI entrepreneurs and technical experts.
  • An open, collaborative, and innovation-driven culture.
  • Flexible work arrangements and strong support for professional growth.
  • The opportunity to help build next-generation Agentic AI platforms for industries such as education, legal, and finance.

title: Senior Executive, Trade Pricing (Shipping Liner MNC)
raw_description:
The Client:

Our client is a prominent and growing global shipping carrier, known for its excellence in providing reliable and efficient transportation solutions. With a strong presence in both domestic and international markets, they offer dynamic career opportunities within a fast-paced and innovative industry, making it an exciting business to be part of.


Job Responsibility:

· Analyse pricing strategies at different geographical and trade levels, applying these to ensure trade lane efficiencies and reduction of cost control.

· Make key decisions on cost structures and pricing strategies to align with market trends and the company’s objectives.

· Lead ongoing improvements in cost models to adapt to market changes, optimizing pricing and cargo selection.

· Identify opportunities to adjust strategies based on shifting market conditions and competition.

· Regularly analyze differences between estimated and actual shipping costs, taking action to correct discrepancies and improve forecasting accuracy.

· Periodically assess container equipment availability and performance to inform decisions about balancing, repositioning, and managing costs effectively.


Qualifications:

· Bachelors Degree in Maritime, Business, or any related discipline is a plus.

· Experience in a shipping carrier and various trade lanes is ideal as the role requires this individual to have strong trade lane knowledge.

· Experience in yield management or pricing is highly advantageous.

· Knowledge or background in Power BI or Tableau is highly advantageous as the role requires this individual to use these systems for price forecasting.

· Fresh graduates with maritime background are welcome to apply.



Morgan McKinley Pte Ltd

Senjoo Khong

raw_description:

We are looking for a New Product Introduction Process Technician to join our growing Life Cycle Management (LCM) team to support new process development and process improvements for our manufacturing processes. The Process Technician will work with cross functional departments such as Automation and Manufacturing Sciences to execute analyses and/or studies for new products and improve manufacturing processes of existing products.


Position Responsibilities:

  • Own and execute experiments both in the laboratory and indoor manufacturing environments.
  • Operate automation lines and prepare materials required for the experiments.
  • Operate laboratory instruments such as pipettes, ICP-MS, FTIR, Spectrophotometers, SEM, Confocal Image analyses.
  • Partner with Engineers for complex experiment executions.
  • Prepare reports and/or PowerPoint slides on study results.

Listed responsibilities are an essential, but not exhaustive list, of the usual duties associated with the position. Changes to individual responsibilities may occur due to business needs.


Position Requirements:

  • Diploma in Science/ Engineering or equivalent in Chemical, Biochemical, Pharmaceutical, Material Science, Biochemistry and/or Bio-related field.
  • High degree of initiative and ownership, as well as being comfortable to with working with several different departments in a fast-paced environment
  • Experience in laboratory and/or manufacturing environment is desired.
  • Excellent organizational skills and attention to detail required.
  • Demonstrated ability to work independently.
  • Excellent verbal/written communication skills.

All listed requirements are deemed as essential functions to this position; however, business conditions may require reasonable accommodation for additional task and responsibilities.

title: Junior Business Development Executive (Fresh Graduate)
raw_description:
Job description

Salary: 50,000.00 (SGD) - 70,000.00 (SGD) per year


Job description:


International School Group – Singapore HQ Focus: Japan, Thailand & Southeast Asia (SEA) Expansion


Position Overview

We are looking for a motivated fresh graduate to join our Singapore-based international school group as a Junior Business Development Manager. This entry-level role is designed for high-potential talent eager to launch a career in global education expansion, with a focus on Japan, Thailand, and Southeast Asia (SEA).You will support senior leaders in researching new school opportunities, building partnerships, and laying the groundwork for campus launches in high-growth markets. This is a structured 18–24 month accelerated development program with mentorship, regional travel, and clear progression to full Business Development Manager within 2 years.


Location: Singapore Start Date: Q1 2026 Salary: SGD 50,000 – 70,000 per annum + performance bonus (up to 2 months)

Travel: 20–30% (Japan, Thailand, Vietnam, Indonesia, Malaysia)


Key ResponsibilitiesMarket Intelligence & Research

Conduct desktop and field research on education regulations, demographics, and competitor schools in Japan (Tokyo, Osaka), Thailand (Bangkok, Chiang Mai), and SEA cities.


Build and maintain a live database of potential partners: local schools, real estate developers, government bodies, and investor groups.


Partnership Support

Assist in outreach to local education ministries, MOUs, and joint-venture discussions.
Prepare pitch decks, feasibility summaries, and data rooms for senior review.


Project Coordination

Support end-to-end project timelines: from site visits and due diligence to pre-launch marketing plans.
Coordinate with curriculum, admissions, and operations teams in Singapore.


Networking & Representation

Attend education fairs, agent briefings, and regional conferences (e.g., EARCOS, ISAT, BETT Asia) as a junior delegate.
Represent the brand in agent training sessions and parent information events.


Analytics & Reporting

Track KPIs: lead generation, conversion rates, market entry timelines.
Prepare monthly dashboards and board updates using Google Sheets, Tableau, or CRM tools.


Language & Cultural Bridge

Leverage Japanese, Thai, or SEA language skills (if any) to support translation and relationship building.
Participate in cultural immersion trips (e.g., 1-week homestay in rural Thailand or school shadowing in Japan).


Who We’re Looking For (Fresh Graduates)Education

Bachelor’s degree (any discipline) from a recognized university
Preferred majors: Business, International Relations, Education, Marketing, Japanese Studies, Southeast Asian Studies
Final-year GPA: 3.4/4.0 or Second Upper Honours (or equivalent) Experience
No prior full-time work required
Valued (not required):
Internship in education, real estate, consulting, or startup environments
Student leadership (e.g., club president, event organizer)
Study abroad or exchange in Japan, Thailand, or SEA


Skills & Traits

Research & Analytical: Comfortable with Excel, Google Workspace, and basic data visualization
Communication: Clear writer and confident presenter (English fluency required)
Languages (bonus, not mandatory):
Japanese (JLPT N3 or higher)
Thai, Bahasa Indonesia/Malaysia, or Vietnamese
Adaptability: Thrives in ambiguity; eager to learn fast in a dynamic environment
Cultural Curiosity: Genuine interest in Asian education systems and family decision-making


What We Offer

Fast-Track Career: Clear path to mid-level role in 2 years
Regional Exposure: Work and travel across Japan, Thailand, Vietnam, Malaysia
Wellness & Growth:
Annual learning budget (SGD 1,500)
16 days annual leave + 1 study leave day per quarter


How to Apply

Email: careers@kbh.edu.sg

We are an equal opportunity employer. Singapore citizens, PRs, and international graduates with employment pass eligibility are welcome


Job Type: Full-time

Benefits:Health insurance

title: Account Assistant/Executive ( 1 Year Contract)
raw_description:
KEY JOB PURPOSE

The successful candidate will be part of our company and contribute to administrative and accounting activities. He/she will provide assistance to the corporate lab enabling the company and R&D staff to accomplish their R&D activities effectively and efficiently.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Manage full set of financial statements (Balance Sheets, P&L and cash flow) for two entities.
  • Manage full set of account including vendor invoice processing, payment arrangements, journal entries, inter-company charges and GL maintenance
  • Perform financial operations like checking of invoice, expense claim/reimbursement from employee
  • Ensure accurate and timely monthly financial reporting, budgeting, forecasting and analysis.
  • Fixed asset register, tracking and depreciation recordings
  • Prepare prepayment, accrual and account payable list report
  • Prepare yearly audit schedules
  • Manage office and Lab supplies inventories
  • Any Ad Hoc duties assign by management


COMPETENCIES AND QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS

  • Possess at least a Diploma in Finance/Accountancy/Banking or equivalent in a related financial field.
  • At least 1–2 years of accounting-related working experience is preferred.
  • Fresh graduates with accounting-related certifications are welcome to apply.
  • Able to work under pressure and equipped with good communication skills.
  • Able to start immediately or at short notice will be an advantage.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Excel (spreadsheets and formulas).
  • Able to work independently, self-motivated, and with a positive working attitude.
  • Bilingual in English and Mandarin (to communicate effectively with Mandarin-speaking counterparts at the Headquarters in China).


ABRIDGED VERSION OF JOB DESCRIPTION AND REQUIREMENTS

We are looking for a focused and highly motivated individual to contribute to a dynamic and growing team. This team focuses on the fabrication of large-area perovskite panels in our flagship project, working towards the development of world leading high-efficiency perovskite photovoltaics as well as the module integration.

We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: GRIT Trainee - [Marketing]
raw_description:
About Team

Our Marketing teams conceptualise and execute innovative go-to-market strategies to grow our brand presence and engage millions of users. We work closely with regional teams to localise campaigns, ensuring that our marketing efforts resonate with diverse audiences across Southeast Asia and beyond.


As a GRIT Trainee in the Marketing team, you will get the opportunity to learn from experienced marketers, build a strong foundation in digital marketing, and contribute to campaigns that shape the future of e-commerce.


Job Description

This is a 6 months full-time onsite role based in Singapore as part of the GRIT Programme. The GRaduate Industry Traineeships (GRIT) Programme is an initiative by Workforce Singapore (WSG) that offers fresh graduates with traineeship opportunities across various sectors.

  • Assist the Livestream team with daily activities and execution of livestream campaigns
  • Relationship management with livestream sellers; assisting them in their needs and maintaining a positive relationship
  • Assist in analysing livestream performance data to generate meaningful insights and reports
  • Conduct competitor analysis to provide recommendations on new initiatives or areas of improvement
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams such as Business Intelligence, Marketing and Business Development as required
  • Assist in administrative tasks and marketing operations

Job Requirements

  • Graduated or graduating in 2024 and 2025 from universities, or graduated earlier and completed National Service in 2024 or 2025
  • Singapore Citizen or Singapore Permanent Resident
  • Excellent stakeholder management skills with strong ability to manage expectations, drive for performance and collaborate with internal stakeholders to deliver quality work within timelines
  • Strong communication and writing skills
  • Good team player with can-do attitude and a great attitude towards learning
  • Detail-oriented, analytical and structured thinker who is able to derive insights from data
  • Independent and self-motivated, with good end-to-end ownership of project and an openness to taking on new projects

title: Graduate Electrical Engineer-Building Services
raw_description:
The Team


We are an award winning global consultancy and have been named Building’s Engineering Consultant of the Year in 2024, 2022 and 2021. We are at the forefront of the industry, priding ourselves on our exemplary track record of designing innovative and sustainable projects.


Sustainability runs through everything we do at Cundall, and our people are empowered to drive change and decarbonise the built environment. By the end of this decade, we have set ourselves an ambitious goal to only work on projects that meet our zero carbon design criteria, a commitment we have called our Zero Carbon Design 2030 goal. We need engineers who share this passion for sustainability and thrive in designing energy efficient buildings and communities.


Our Building Services teams at Cundall have a mission: they want to play a key role in making buildings more energy efficient, cost effective to run and, ultimately, more sustainable. To do this, we’re looking for graduates who have a desire and enthusiasm to make a difference: in our homes, our offices, our projects, our industry and our community.


Working across a variety of industry sectors, our Building Services engineers are a source of world leading expertise; producing innovative and award-winning sustainable engineering solutions throughout the lifecycle of any building project.


Our Building Services teams have accomplished amazing projects like an innovative office for Google, the largest arts centre in Scotland, and an energy efficient data centre for Facebook. As a graduate in the team, you could play a key part in our next big project.


The Role


In this Graduate Engineer role based in our Singapore office, you’ll join a team who specialise in the design of some of the most exciting construction projects in Asia and worldwide.


Working closely with colleagues across our multi-disciplinary practice, you’ll provide specialist consultancy input on projects across a wide range of sectors including education, healthcare, workplace, retail and critical systems (data centres).


Under the guidance of our team of experienced engineers, we’ll equip you to undertake detailed building services engineering design and analysis, defining and translating clients’ needs into designs for buildings, facilities and services - all with sustainability at the forefront of the design.


You’ll have the opportunity over time to assume greater levels of responsibility and autonomy as your professional skills develop and with accredited professional development programmes from CIBSE and IET, you’ll be fully supported towards achieving chartership.



The Skills


Accredited with, or on course to achieve an MEng degree level (or equivalent) in either Electrical, or Building Services Engineering, you’re passionate about innovative and sustainable Electrical engineering for the built environment. Ideally, you’ll have furthered this interest by undertaking some form of relevant work experience or placement/internship.

You have an appreciation for the core elements of building services engineering and crucially, have a love for creative and intuitive design. Digital engineering is a big focus at Cundall, so ideally, you’ll have an understanding of Parametric Modelling Techniques (Grasshopper or Dynamo), or at least one modern programming language (e.g. Python, C#, VB). An understanding of Engineering Optimisation Techniques, the principles of structured programming and basic knowledge in the application of Machine Learning would also be desirable.

You are organised and methodical in your approach to managing your tasks and priorities, you strive to meet deadlines and have a close appreciation of the commercial factors which affect design solutions.

Collaborative in nature, you’ll be able to empathise with your clients’ needs and utilise excellent communication skills, both in writing and verbally, with internal and external stakeholders. You’re used to using your initiative to overcome challenges and have a positive, can-do attitude.


What’s in it for you

We offer a competitive starting salary which grows as you develop, as well as many training and development opportunities to make sure you keep progressing. Aside from this, those who successfully join our graduate scheme also enjoy:

  • Professional career development and mentoring
  • Sponsorship for professional membership
  • Exposure to international opportunities

Graduates who are keen to ap-ply, please kindly submit your applications via our Early Careers Page:

https://www.cundall.com/early-careers/roles/graduate-electrical-engineer-45

title: Customer Service Analyst
raw_description:
The key responsibility of Customer Service Analyst (CSA) is to be the first point of contact for all walk-in customers at the Service Centre. The CSA is expected to provide high service standards to customers who visit the Service Centre.


Responsibilities:

  • Provide consistent and positive customer experience to all walk-in customers.
  • To ensure accuracy and compliance for all customers' requests.
  • Educating customers on the features and benefits of our digital tools.
  • Handle and manage appeals, feedback and first level escalations.
  • Constantly identify process improvements with the objective to improve overall customer experience.

Requirements:

  • At least a diploma or degree of any field
  • Minimum 2 years of experience in customer service, preferably in the insurance industry
  • Have a passion to deliver customer-centric service
  • Good communication skills, strong attention to detail and problem-solving skills
  • Resilient and able to work effectively under pressure
  • A team player with high level of commitment and flexibility

title: Life Skills Coach
raw_description:
Overview:

InSchool is a social enterprise that provides a structured before and after school enrichment programme, specially designed for neurodivergent children (aged 7-12) to build functional life skills, social confidence & independence.


We are seeking a driven, talented and dedicated coach to join our team. The ideal candidate will be passionate about working with students with special needs and will be committed to creating a supportive learning environment as we continue to make meaningful impact to society and build an inclusive future.


You will be involved in the end-to-end execution of your teaching work and experience how your effort is positively changing and impacting the lives of families and children with special needs.


Responsibilities:

- Handle screening and assessment of potential students.

- Develop, implement, and review lesson plans based on given curriculum.

- Conduct every session’s content based on the lesson plans provided.

- Track students’ progress, performance and behaviour.

- Adjust lesson plan content and teaching methods/strategies to meet the unique needs of each student.

- Collaborate with team members, parents and therapists to support students’ success.

- Build an inclusive and positive classroom environment that fosters student growth and development.

- Keep up to date with best practices in the special needs education space and take up professional development opportunities.


Requirements:

- Minimum 2-3 years of working experience within a SPED school and/or worked with children with special needs, including but not limited to autism, intellectual disabilities, down syndrome etc.

- Having a specialist diploma/degree in a relevant or related field or valid teaching certification in Special Education is good but optional.

- Some understanding of the special education laws and regulations.

- Strong in communicating, collaborating and building relationships with students, parents and team members.

- Good patience, empathy and desire to help students achieve their potential.

- Takes the initiative, can be independent, and positively influences your team.

- Resourceful, result-driven, can problem-solve, flexible and adaptable to the changing educational environment.

- Likes to learn and able to learn on the job.


Schedule:

  • Monday to Friday (in-person and on-site)

Locations:

· Common Ground Civic Centre, 21 Bedok North Street 1, #01-01, Singapore 469659

· Enabling Village, 20 Lengkok Bahru, #01-09, Canopy Block, Singapore 159053


Interested candidates please drop us a message at hello@inschool.sg or contact us at +65 8057 9838 (WhatsApp available).

title: Compliance Analyst (Name Screening & KYC)
raw_description:
Job Overview

We are looking for a meticulous and detail-oriented Compliance Officer to support our name-screening and due-diligence functions. This is a desk-bound, non-customer-facing role focused on reviewing alerts, assessing risks, and ensuring compliance with regulatory standards.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Review and clear name-screening alerts in accordance with internal policies and regulatory expectations
  • Perform KYC due-diligence checks, including sanctions and adverse-media assessments
  • Document rationale clearly and accurately, and escalate cases when required
  • Ensure timely, high-quality disposition of alerts
  • Work with large volumes of written information in a structured and consistent manner
  • Maintain strong awareness of major global sanctions regimes
  • Support the team in handling changes, multitasking, and managing a fast-paced workflow
  • Carry out any additional duties assigned by the supervisor or Management


Job Requirements:

  1. Bachelor’s degree preferred
  2. At least 2 years of relevant experience in name-screening alert review and disposition, ideally in banks or payment institutions
  3. Strong organizational skills with excellent attention to detail
  4. Meticulous, analytical, and able to commit to deadlines
  5. Self-motivated, resourceful, and a strong team player

You will work closely with researchers and process engineers to contribute to cutting-edge research in the field and help drive the development of next-generation photovoltaics.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

The job scope includes:

  • Able to work from 1:00pm - 10:00pm OR 08:30am - 05:30pm
  • Prepares substrates/precursors, loads samples, in charge of coating process, and prepares material coating process for experimentation in time and quality
  • Evaluates performance of process system.
  • Interface with team members, understand their detailed plan and requirement.
  • Monitor process and hardware performance.
  • Records process conditions and experimental observations.
  • Report data to the research team.
  • Assist in laboratory and equipment maintenance.
  • Perform routine shut down procedure, if needed.
  • Ensure cleanliness of work area, upkeep equipment and assists in maintenance program and operating of equipment.

COMPETENCIES AND QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS

  • Diploma or Bachelor's degree in materials science, chemistry, electrical engineering, chemical engineering, or a related field.
  • Enjoy hands-on duties daily (non-desk bound) which may include carrying items, walking, working etc.
  • Capable to work in a clean laboratory environment daily
  • No disciplinary issues history
  • Self-disciplined, good team player
  • Responsible and independent worker
  • Reactive to malfunctions
  • High self-motivated and effective team player with good communication skills
  • Attentive to details
  • Knowledge of safety protocols and practices in a laboratory setting.

ABRIDGED VERSION OF JOB DESCRIPTION AND REQUIREMENTS

We are looking for a focused and highly motivated individual to contribute to a dynamic and growing team. This team focuses on the fabrication of next-generation photovoltaic technology in our flagship project. We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.
