title: (Internship)Business Assistant/Executive
raw_description:
Salary Range：12~25 per hour
Location: Near MRT stations of Telok Ayer, Downtown, Shenton Way and Raffles Place
Internship Type: Part-time Internship(≥ 3 days per week preferred)
Reports To: CEO
Duration: 3–6 months (flexible, open to extension)
Working Arrangement: Onsite / Hybrid
Industry: Medical, with the parent company listed in China (Shanghai Stock Exchange A-share).

1. About the Role

We are looking for a detail-oriented and responsible intern to support our corporate and tax compliance team. This role is well-suited for university undergraduates who want hands-on exposure to Singapore government incentive programs and corporate administration work.

The intern will assist primarily with document organising, form preparation, and administrative support relating to EDB tax incentive programs and other regulatory filings, and other ad hoc tasks assigned by CEO.

2. Key Responsibilities

Ø Assist in organizing and maintaining corporate and tax-related documents (softcopy & hardcopy)

Ø Support the preparation and filling of application forms for EDB tax incentive programs and other government incentive-related filings, including liaison internally to collect required supporting documents (e.g. contracts, invoices,& other records)

Ø Help with data entry, reconciliation, and cross-checking of information in forms and schedules

Ø Assist in basic drafting of cover letters, checklists, and submission trackers

Ø Perform other general administrative and ad-hoc tasks assigned by CEO and other colleagues

3. Requirements

Ø Current undergraduate student from Singapore local universities, preferred majors: Accounting, Business, Finance, Economics, Law, Public Policy, or Operation

Ø Able to commit at least 3 months with immediate start

Ø Strong attention to detail and organised working style

Ø Proficient in Microsoft Office software (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).

Ø Good written and spoken English (Chinese is a plus but not mandatory)

Ø Responsible, discreet, and able to handle confidential information

Ø Good command of writing and speaking in both Mandarin and English is preferred.

The Case Manager will work closely with the Case Integration Lead to oversee the triaging process, reviews and work towards achieving the COMIT goals of timely assessment, provision of intervention and support eg. psychoeducation, case management and counselling, as well as caregiver support and training.

In addition, the Case Manager (Social Worker) may also be involved in networking with other community partners or agencies, for purpose of collaboration and to facilitate co-management of cases where applicable and other adhoc duties assigned.

This candidate will be one of the key members driving the COMIT project.

  • Assist with the triaging process for the AIC COMIT Programme.

  • Ensure programme data and documents are kept and maintained, in accordance to the COMIT programme requirement, as well as the prevailing personal data protection and security guidelines

  • Work closely with the Case Integration Lead to oversee the triaging process, reviews and work towards achieving the COMIT goals of timely assessment, provision of intervention and support eg. psychoeducation, case management and counselling, as well as caregiver support and training.

  • Participate in relevant meetings with key stakeholders including AIC to share key learning points where required

  • May also be involved in networking with other community partners or agencies, for purpose of collaboration and to facilitate co-management of cases where applicable.

  • Any other adhoc duties assigned.

Requirements:

  • Recognized Bachelor's degree in Social work.

  • Has at least 3 years of working experience with case management, preferably in the Healthcare / Social service sector.

  • Team player with good interpersonal, oral and written communication skills.

  • Preferably with supervisory experience, able to provide case supervision to the team to provide intervention and support through the project.

  • Work experience in social service sectors or familar with the ASCAT/COMIT Programme would be an advantage.

To help us process applications more quickly, please submit yours in this link: https://apply.workable.com/intellecthq/j/0FDF6A0ED6/apply/

title: Talent Acquisition Executive
raw_description:
Fresh Graduates Are Welcome to Apply

Key Responsibilities

Recruitment and Placement

  • Manage the end-to-end recruitment process for frontline positions, including job postings, resume screening, phone interviews, interview coordination, and offer communication.
  • Work closely with business stakeholders to clearly understand job responsibilities and hiring requirements for frontline roles.
  • Develop, maintain, and expand recruitment channels for blue-collar hiring.
  • Effectively utilize both online and offline recruitment platforms, including but not limited to LinkedIn, JobStreet, MyCareersFuture, and on-site job fairs.
  • Establish and manage employee referral programs to encourage internal talent recommendations.
  • Leverage social media platforms (e.g. WeChat, TikTok) to enhance employer branding and promote job opportunities.

Candidate Relationship Management

  • Build strong, professional relationships with candidates and provide a positive, responsive candidate experience.
  • Support offer discussions, coordinate onboarding schedules, and assist with background checks.
  • Track onboarding progress and liaise with hiring managers and administrative teams to ensure smooth employee integration.

Data and Process Optimization

  • Track and analyze recruitment metrics such as interview attendance, offer acceptance rates, and turnover data.
  • Prepare regular recruitment reports and continuously improve recruitment processes to enhance efficiency, quality, and cost control.

Qualifications & Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree or above in Human Resource Management, Psychology, Business Administration, or related disciplines.
  • 1–2 years of relevant recruitment experience preferred; fresh graduates with strong interest in recruitment are welcome to apply.
  • Familiarity with blue-collar recruitment channels and hiring practices in industries such as manufacturing, logistics, or labor-intensive sectors is an advantage.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage frontline employees from diverse backgrounds.
  • Ability to work in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple priorities effectively.
  • Proactive, responsible, and results-driven mindset.

title: Summer Internship Programme
raw_description:
Summer Internship Programme

Sapience SG

Location: 1 Pasir Panjang Road, Labrador Tower, Singapore
Industry: Financial Planning (Banking & Financial Services)
Employment Type: Full-Time Internship
Allowance: SGD 1,500 – 2,000 per month

Programme Period

Start March 2026

Work Schedule

Monday to Friday

Programme Overview

The Sapience SG Summer Internship Programme is a structured development programme designed for students and fresh graduates who are exploring career pathways in finance, business, and strategy.

Participants will gain practical exposure through professional training, mentorship, and hands-on learning experiences that go beyond a traditional internship.

Key Responsibilities & Learning Outcomes

Participants will:

  • Attend structured training sessions focused on financial fundamentals, business concepts, and professional workplace skills
  • Participate in networking sessions with industry professionals and mentors
  • Work towards completing relevant certification papers to strengthen professional knowledge and credentials
  • Develop pitching, presentation, and communication skills through guided practice
  • Gain exposure to how financial planning, business operations, and strategic decision-making are carried out in real-world settings

Requirements

  • Aged 21 and above
  • Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident
  • Completed GCE A-Levels or a Local Diploma

Who Should Apply

  • Students or fresh graduates exploring potential career paths
  • Individuals eager to develop professional skills and industry exposure
  • Candidates who are motivated, open to learning, and keen to grow in a collaborative environment

What We Offer

  • Monthly allowance of SGD 1,500 – 2,000
  • Structured learning and close mentorship
  • Strong industry exposure and professional networking
  • A supportive environment focused on personal growth and career development

title: Events Manager
raw_description:
Seafood by the River is looking for a motivated and enthusiastic Events Manager to support the planning and execution of events that enhance our dining experience and brand presence. This role is suitable for fresh graduates or candidates with up to 2 years of experience who are keen to build a career in events management within the F&B and hospitality industry. Training and guidance will be provided.

Key Responsibilities

  • Plan, coordinate, and execute events such as corporate functions, private dining, brand collaborations, and festive promotions
  • Liaise with clients, partners, suppliers, and internal teams
  • Coordinate event marketing and promotional activities with relevant teams
  • Oversee on-site event operations, setup, and teardown
  • Resolve operational issues promptly during events
  • Conduct post-event evaluations and reporting
  • Ensure compliance with safety, hygiene, and regulatory standards

Requirements

  • Diploma or Degree in Hospitality & Tourism Management, Marketing, or related fields
  • Minimum 0-2 years of relevant experience, preferably in F&B or hospitality events
  • Strong interest in events planning and hospitality industry
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities
  • Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
  • Willingness to learn and take initiative
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office and basic event management tools
  • Able to work evenings, weekends, and public holidays when required

title: BIM Modeller (C&S, Archi)
raw_description:
Job Description

  • Work closely with clients & consultants for BIM modelling and coordinate/collaborate using Revit
  • Ensure construction documents, parameters and information accuracy for BIM modelling
  • Adhere to BIM execution plan and BIM projects workflow criteria
  • Prepare drawing and 3D model for construction, coordination and as built purposes
  • Monitor and update model/CAD drawing as per site condition changes
  • Participate in planning and design support to civil & structural engineering projects
  • Setting up project base files, ensures compliance with the approved BIM Execution, BIM Scope Plans and CAD standards
  • Review all works for accuracy and completeness, submit work according to deadlines
  • Demonstrate proactive approach to learning of new digital design tools
  • Any other ad-hoc duties as assigned by the management


Requirements

  • Preferably ITE / Diploma / Degree in Architecture, Engineering and Construction Industry (AEC) / Specialist Diploma in BIM / VDC advantage
  • Minimum two years of CAD + BIM experiences and preferred with 5 years of local working experience
  • Knowledge of, and proven practical background in, Autodesk Civil3D, Revit and related Building Information Modeling software.
  • Solid understanding of legacy CAD applications and practices, particularly AutoCAD.
  • Familiarity with Design Visualization applications such as BIM Collaborate and Navisworks.
  • Demonstrate a reliable, responsive and positive work ethic with the highest degree of integrity.
  • Demonstrated ability to develop learning material, deliver effective training and provide ongoing education.
  • Punctuality and Ability to adhere to deadlines


Suitable candidates are encouraged to apply to this position via Apply Now button or email to Career@kah.com.sg

Only shortlisted applicants will be notified.

title: Programme Associate
raw_description:
Role Description

The Programme Associate is essential in executing enriching and impactful programmes for the children (primary school levels) at our outreach centres. The incumbent will conduct a set of children’s programmes covering subjects such as life skills, art, sports, and STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering & Mathematics). He/She must be flexible, passionate, and dedicated to fostering children's growth.

Key Responsibilities

1. Programme Delivery:

  • Lead and facilitate structured activities for children
  • Adapt activities to cater to different learning needs and energy levels of the children
  • Prepare materials required for the session

2. Children Engagement:

  • Create a welcoming and inclusive environment that encourages participation
  • Ensure children’s well-being and safety during activities

3. Programme Evaluation:

  • Provide feedback on sessions and suggest improvements to enhance the engagement
  • Carry out programme evaluation to assess learning and satisfaction
  • Communicate any observations or recommendations to support continuous programme development.

Requirements

  • GCE ‘A’ Level and above preferably, or Minimally GCE ‘O’ Level with relevant experience
  • Fresh graduates are welcome to apply
  • Passion for working with children and creating a positive impact
  • Ability to lead and manage a group of children effectively
  • Ability to respond quickly and adapt lesson delivery accordingly
  • Effective communication and interpersonal skills
  • Experience in facilitation, teaching or working with children
  • Able to commit up to 3 months or more

Working Hours

Weekdays afternoon (adhoc) at our Outreach Centres from

  • 2:30pm to 5:30pm or;
  • 3:00pm to 6:00pm

How to Apply:
Please email your detailed CV to recruitment@chijsc.org.sg

title: Field Service Engineer
raw_description:
Nova Singapore is looking for a Field Service Engineer to support our business and drive its growth.

As a Field Service Engineer, you will play a pivotal role, contributing to the overall success of Nova. This position offers an exciting opportunity for individuals who are passionate about Semiconductor and are eager to make a meaningful impact through the role.

Roles & Responsibilities

1. Implement the Customer Support Policy for the OEM End User. Supervise, maintain and support company systems in the field.

2. Support OEM partners, and End Users internal service as defined under warranty and service support agreements.

3. Maintain, support, and guide proper training to all users, including OEM and End User personnel. (Training will be provided, in most cases, by an engineer dedicated to training).

4. Maintain proper reporting methods for all activities of customer support as defined by the Territory Service Manager/Customer Support Manager.

5. Maintain company’s parts inventory under the position’s control. Maintain proper level of stock in accordance with company spare parts tock policy, as set from time to time.

6. Assist in new equipment integration, “add-ons,” and upgrades.

7. Provide feedback to the headquarters based on experience gained with customers.

8. Support the customer beyond the defined working schedule as required.

9. Other tasks deemed necessary by the Management.

Requirements

1. Bachelor’s degree related to Semiconductor / Engineering industry.

2. Prior experience in semiconductor industry would be an advantage.

3. Good communication skills, team player, service orientate.

4. Able to accept work shift and oversea support if need.

5. Proficient in English.

6. Fresh graduates are welcome

Life at Nova:

  • Enjoy paid annual vacation and sick days, as well as allowance for transportation and spot bonus.
  • Get recognition of your special occasions and enjoy diverse holiday gifts and fun swag.
  • Employee Insurance (Outpatient Medical Plan, Hospitalization & Surgical Plan, Critical Illness Plan, Accidental Plan, Life Insurance Plan)
  • Take advantage of training and learning programs to further develop as a professional.


Get to know us better

Nova (NASDAQ: NVMI) is a global company and a leading provider of innovative metrology and process control solutions for semiconductor manufacturing.

With approximately 1,400 employees, three R&D centers and 31 sites worldwide, we provide valuable insights into the world’s most advanced industry. We leverage a variety of technological fields, including physics, optics, chemistry, and algorithms, to develop hardware and software solutions that measure nearly every advanced computer chip built in the world.

Nova dives deep into the atomic level, extracting unique insights using dimensional, materials and chemical metrology, providing our customers with crucial decision-making data, and addressing critical challenges by offering differentiated solutions.

Our outstanding, people-focused, and innovative culture means that every individual at Nova has the opportunity to make a significant impact and redefine the boundaries of what can be achieved through technology.

Sentronics Metrology GmbH (a Nova Company), acquired by Nova, is a leading metrology company specializing in high-precision measurement solutions for semiconductor manufacturing. Based in Mannheim, Sentronics brings over 25 years of expertise in developing cutting-edge metrology systems.

The company’s integration into Nova is already underway, and it is now an integral part of the Nova family, sharing the same values of innovation, quality, and customer satisfaction. The combination of Nova's global reach and Sentronics' advanced technology and specialized knowledge creates a unique platform for growth and innovation. As part of Nova, Sentronics will fully benefit from the exciting opportunities in the rapidly evolving semiconductor industry.


Join us if you are

A dreamer and brilliant high aimer who sees the impossible as the starting point and loves working in a multidisciplinary global team to create innovative breakthroughs.

With approximately 1,400 employees, three R&D centers and 31 sites worldwide, we provide valuable insights into the world’s most advanced industry. We leverage a variety of technological fields, including physics, optics, chemistry, and algorithms, to develop hardware and software solutions that measure nearly every advanced computer chip built in the world. Nova dives deep into the atomic level, extracting unique insights using dimensional, materials and chemical metrology, providing our customers with crucial decision-making data, and addressing critical challenges by offering differentiated solutions. Our outstanding, people-focused, and innovative culture means that every individual at Nova has the opportunity to make a significant impact and redefine the boundaries of what can be achieved through technology. Sentronics Metrology GmbH (a Nova Company) , acquired by Nova, is a leading metrology company specializing in high-precision measurement solutions for semiconductor manufacturing. Based in Mannheim, Sentronics brings over 25 years of expertise in developing cutting-edge metrology systems. The company’s integration into Nova is already underway, and it is now an integral part of the Nova family, sharing the same values of innovation, quality, and customer satisfaction. The combination of Nova's global reach and Sentronics' advanced technology and specialized knowledge creates a unique platform for growth and innovation. As part of Nova, Sentronics will fully benefit from the exciting opportunities in the rapidly evolving semiconductor industry. title: Intern - Key Clients and Institutions
raw_description:
About Lighthouse Canton

Lighthouse Canton is an independent asset manager to institutional investors and private clients, with headquarters in Singapore. Our wealth management business serves ultra-high-net-worth individuals, families, and family offices across their investment, estate, tax, and philanthropic needs. The asset management business offers strong internal product capabilities in hedge funds, private equity, and traditional fundamental analysis-based strategies. Lighthouse Canton has offices in Singapore, Dubai, and Bengaluru India.
We believe in hiring smart and curious people with the highest integrity. We offer talented professionals an opportunity to grow in a collaborative environment, surrounded by driven colleagues, and the opportunity to make an impact from the first day in the organization.

Job Purpose

We are seeking a highly motivated and detail-oriented individual to join the Key Clients and Institutions team. Successful applicants will gain hands-on exposure to international banking and wealth management projects, providing an opportunity to work closely with seasoned professionals in a dynamic environment.

Job Responsibilities

  • Conduct research and extract relevant data to support key projects.
  • Organize, analyze and interpret data to support business objectives and project outcomes.
  • Stay informed on market trends and provide insights that will contribute to project development.
  • Assist in administrative tasks, including documentation, reporting, and data management.
  • Coordinate meetings, track project timelines, and collaborate with various stakeholders to support project execution.
  • Support the review and preparation of legal and compliance-related documentation under guidance.
  • Assist in maintaining proper records of contracts, agreements, and regulatory submissions.
  • Conduct basic legal research on regulatory requirements and industry standards to support the compliance and legal teams.
  • Ensure confidentiality and adherence to internal policies and regulatory frameworks when handling sensitive documents and data.

Qualifications & Skills Required

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Business, or a related field, with a strong academic record.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Excel, PowerPoint and knowledge of basic data analysis tools is a plus.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with an interest in financial markets and wealth management.
  • Detail-oriented, proactive, and capable of working independently.
  • Good time management and organizational skills.
  • Strong communication skills, written and verbal, with the ability to interact with different levels of stakeholders.

Internship Duration

  • Start immediately and commit for at least 3 months. 6 months is highly preferred.

title: Executive, KYC
raw_description:
We invite highly motivated, dynamic professionals to join our growing Quality & Risk Management Team.


Our Quality & Risk Management Department disseminates timely and relevant information on developments in legislation and regulations, executes initiatives to manage our risk in a challenging environment. The position involves operating as part of a risk management and compliance team.

The AML Monitoring & Compliance function is part of Quality and Risk Management supporting the Firm in customer due diligence process to evaluate the KYC screening results, identify any potential risks prior to accepting or continuing business relationships, ensuring they falls within the regulatory and compliance expectations.


Job Description and Responsibilities:

  • Evaluate, perform validation of completeness and accuracy of the KYC screenings, evaluate and document the assessment of the results according to relevant regulations, guidance and procedures
  • Handle internal queries on regulatory requirements and compliance policies and procedures on AML-CFT
  • Provide support in the development and maintenance of in-house training content for periodic trainings on AML-CFT
  • Participate in establishing, implement and provide oversight AML/CFT policies, procedures, systems and controls
  • Manage and engage with internal stakeholders, setting service standards and resolving issues in accordance with organizational procedures, promote understanding and compliance with risk management policies and procedure


Job Requirements:

  • A recognized degree, preferably in Accounting, Banking & Finance or equivalent
  • Minimum 2 years of relevant experience in compliance and regulatory matters with a sizable financial institution/regulatory body, legal, brokerage and investment firm preferred
  • Candidates with Certified Fraud Examiner (CFE) or Association of Certified Anti-Money Laundering Specialists (ACAMS) will be an added advantage
  • Working knowledge of local regulatory guidelines, KYC and AML guidelines is essential
  • Possess strong analytical and problem-solving skills with excellent verbal/written communication and interpersonal skills
  • Ability to exercise sound judgement, escalate issues and propose solutions appropriately
  • Familiar with the use of research databases such as Lexis Nexis, World Check, Factiva, Corporate Registries and related research tools
  • Team player, self-motivated, resourceful and has an eye for detail

⠀

Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted by KPMG Talent Acquisition team, personal data collected will be used for recruitment purposes only.

At KPMG in Singapore we are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace. We believe that diversity of thought, background and experience strengthens relationships and delivers meaningful benefits to our people, our clients and communities. As an equal opportunity employer, all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment regardless of age, race, gender identity or expression, colour, marital status, religion, sexual orientation, disability, or other non-merit factors. We celebrate the different talents that our people bring and support every staff member in their journey to achieve personal and professional growth. One of the ways we do this is through Take Charge: Flexi-work, our flexible working framework which enables agile and innovative teams to help deliver our business goals.

title: IT Support
raw_description:
You will play a crucial role in supporting the IT Exec and assisting with various IT-related tasks within the organization. This position requires a diligent and meticulous individual who can handle multiple responsibilities, troubleshoot technical issues, manage data entry, liaise with vendors and suppliers, and uphold strict data privacy standards.

Responsibilities:

  1. Provide support to the IT Executive: Assist in all IT-related tasks, projects, and initiatives within the organization.
  2. Troubleshooting and Technical Support: Respond to technical issues reported by employees or outlets. Resolve problems remotely through Zoom or visit outlets for on-site troubleshooting when required.
  3. Data Entry and Documentation: Maintain a comprehensive record of all scenarios and cases, ensuring meticulous and accurate data entry for tracking purposes.
  4. Time and Task Management: Efficiently manage time and prioritize multiple tasks to meet deadlines and address urgent IT-related issues.
  5. Vendor and Third-Party Liaison: Interact with vendors, suppliers, or third parties for obtaining quotes, resolving issues, or coordinating relocations, ensuring effective communication and follow-up.
  6. Data Privacy and Confidentiality: Take the protection of company data seriously and strictly adhere to the Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA). Obtain necessary approvals before sharing private or confidential information.
  7. Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs): Adhere to established SOPs and guidelines to ensure consistency and compliance in IT operations.
  8. Continuous Learning: Stay updated with industry trends, technologies, and best practices to enhance knowledge and skills required for the role.
  9. Collaboration and Communication: Work collaboratively with team members, colleagues, and stakeholders to ensure smooth IT operations and effective problem resolution.
  10. Documentation and Reporting: Prepare reports, maintain documentation, and provide regular updates to the IT Executive regarding ongoing projects, issues, and resolutions.

Qualifications and Skills:

  • A diploma or degree in an IT-related field or equivalent work experience is a bonus.
  • Strong technical knowledge and troubleshooting skills in computer hardware, software, and network systems.
  • Excellent attention to detail and accuracy in data entry and documentation.
  • Proven ability to manage time efficiently and handle multiple tasks simultaneously.
  • Effective communication skills to interact with vendors, suppliers, employees, and stakeholders.
  • Familiarity with data privacy regulations and commitment to upholding confidentiality standards.
  • Proficient in following SOPs and guidelines to ensure consistent and compliant IT operations.
  • Ability to work independently as well as in a team-oriented environment.
  • Willingness to learn and adapt to new technologies and industry trends.

title: Livestream Sales Admin
raw_description:
Job Title: Channel Executive (open part time and full time)

Report to: Channel Manager

Location: Singapore & Overseas


Role Responsibilities

· Support Channel Manager to manage/create and grow our brand channel.

· To provide administrative support to live-streamers during their live shows with customer support and payments administration.

· Establish clear brand channel strategy and positioning and marketing of the channel using FB and IG or other social media.

· Travelling for overseas streams.

· Planning calendar of sales events (Special stream or events).

· Support end to end operations and logistics and fulfilment for the brand channel.

· Posting and planning of social media contents.

· Review and analyse live stream data.

· Doing up daily/ weekly/monthly reports and KPI for the channel.

· Reconcile and prepare of delivery reports after livestream shows.

· Support sourcing new Merchants and live streamers.

· Merchants & Streamers Management.

· Customer Service.

· Other tasks as assigned by the hiring manager.


Opportunities/Progression

Great learning opportunity: As start-up company, we offer ample opportunity for you to learn things that you probably wouldn't be exposed to in other corporate organisation. Additionally, you will have direct access to the people behind the start-up and will be able to learn from them every step of the way. You will learn more about business strategy, marketing and sales, operations from the top management team.


Overseas Opportunities: There are tons of overseas opportunities and the potential to change the landscape of Livestreaming locally or overseas.


Competitive Career Path: Working in our company often experience more job satisfaction because you are typically more involved in the growth and evolution of the company. You would be promoted to Channel Manager; other functional directors’ opportunity is also available. The CEO has developed great excellent leaders in the company.


Qualification & Experience

· Minimum Diploma/degree holders, major in business management, marketing, communications etc. would be preferable but not a must.

· Retail experience is preferred.

· Intermediate Excel understanding and skill preferred.

· Holding a driving licence and able to drive in overseas. (MUST)

· Able to cope with fast work pace and meticulous to details.

· Candidates are required to be bilingual; livestream shows are done in both Chinese and English.

· Must be prepared to travel overseas (50%) for livestream shows when required.

· Willingness to work occasional overtime to accommodate various time zones.


About us

LiveCommerce Entertainment – a B2B Marketplace connecting Brands and Live Streamers to Start Live Selling on FB and IG Live. Live selling on FB/IG became a new way for people to stay engaged and entertained as they tuned in to Live Selling shows and connected and bonded with other viewers and hosts of the live shows.

Our mission is to Excite & Entertain by providing our customers Live shopping entertainment! title: Investment Analyst
raw_description:
Navis Capital Partners is a Kuala Lumpur based international private/public equity fund management company.

In line with our continuous growth, we invite suitably qualified and self-motivated individuals to join our Private Equity team at our Singapore office.

The Job

  • Build accurate Financial Model including operating KPIs
  • Assist in due diligence and analysis
  • Develop investment thesis
  • Prepare New Deal alerts and assist in Investment Committee deck preparation
  • Provide industry analysis and deal judgement
  • Provide Analytical support for decision making & assist in company specific initiatives
  • Update team on key industry trends & proactive updating of Senior Deal team
  • Assist in Document Preparation

Requirements

  • Relevant experience in direct equity, investment banking, securities analysis or management consulting with an international or top tier firm
  • Strong analytical skills and structured thinking
  • Proven to contribute and achieve in a team oriented environment
  • Outstanding results from a top, globally recognized university
  • Good command of English
  • Good command of other SE Asian languages is an added bonus

Interested applicants are invited to apply enclosing your detailed resume stating personal particulars, qualification, and employment history.

We offer competitive remuneration and excellent career prospects to suitable candidates. You may visit our website at www.naviscapital.com

Key Responsibilities

  • Social & Digital Platform Experimentation
    Drive and support experimentation across social and digital platforms, including but not limited to Xiaohongshu and Eternity X, to expand offshore audience reach.

  • International Brand Campaign Support
    Support the execution of international brand campaigns for the Treasures segment, ensuring alignment with global strategies while addressing local market nuances.

  • Lifestyle / Everyday CVP Communications & Product Launches
    Assist in the development and execution of communications for Lifestyle/Everyday Customer Value Proposition (CVP), as well as new product launches including Digibank, Cards, and Payments.

  • Event Support
    Provide end-to-end support for key events such as the Symposium, covering planning, coordination, and on-site execution.

  • Branch Communications
    Drive and implement effective communications for the branch network, including seasonal campaigns such as Chinese New Year (CNY) and Diwali.

  • Audience Insights & Analysis
    Support the extraction and analysis of audience insights, with a focus on key segments such as young affluents, to inform marketing strategies and campaign development.

title: Trainee – 12 months’ contract – Legal Counsel
raw_description:
Responsibilities
Providing legal advice and support to SG’s Global Banking and Advisory (GLBA) team and other departments of the bank in the Asia-Pacific region, including the transaction management of matters in relation to debt capital markets, third party note issuance, loan financing and securitisation; and other supplemental legal documentation entered into under or in connection with the above.

• Liaising with external counsel and external bodies including regulators, stock exchanges, clearing systems, paying, settlement and listing agents, distributors, registrars, common depositaries and other authorities with respect to the above.

• Reporting and escalating appropriate matters on an ongoing and regular basis to line managers, and liaising with other members of the legal department as necessary.

• Compliance with internal guidelines and procedures, and providing assistance in updating guidelines and procedures which relate to the above activities

It is an opportunity for a Junior Lawyer to be trained and be exposed to finance activities in a dynamic environment. The candidate will gain hands-on experience with drafting and reviewing legal documentation in relation to debt/note issuance, loan financing and securitisation (including transaction documentation and confidentiality agreements) and exposure to contract law. The candidate will gain a solid understanding of the investment banking setup and acquire valuable interpersonal soft skills

Required Profile required Academic Background and Certifications, Experience

• Degree in law (Singapore or Commonwealth law qualification preferred)

• Experience with capital markets and/or lending activities an advantage

• Good communication skills and good command of spoken and written English

• Problem solving and analytical skills; attention to detail

• Motivated and able to work independently, while respecting reporting procedures

Operational Skills

• Possesses basic post-graduate knowledge of fundamental legal principles within area of coverage

• Drafts core legal documents/memos/emails succinctly, thoroughly and in plain language

• Responds effectively to routine legal questions or issues raised by business partners or management

• Identifies and analyses core legal issues within area of coverage

• Identifies legal risks associated with a file

• Stays on top of regulatory changes and case law

• Researches points effectively

Behavioral Skills

• Team Spirit - Synergies: I make developing synergies within the Group a priority

• Innovation - Technology: I include technological breakthroughs in the strategies implemented

• Responsibility - Risk: In order to improve the Group's security, I am constantly on the lookout for risks

• Commitment - Care: I listen and demonstrate emotional intelligence to engage others

• Responsibility - Performance: I generate outstanding individual and collective performance

• Team Spirit - Collective mindset: I favour the company's best interest over my team's results

Business insight Our Culture: At Societe Generale, we live by our 4 core values of commitment, responsibility, team spirit and innovation. We are engaged and demonstrate consideration for others. We act ethically and with courage. We focus our talent and energy on collective success. We experiment and propose new ideas. This way, we maximize our ability to serve client needs and anticipate market changes. Societe Generale is committed to strengthening bonds with colleagues, communities, and the world in which we live, because relationships are at the heart of how we operate.

title: Business Development Intern
raw_description:
Company Overview

Anywheel is a homegrown Singapore micro-mobility startup focused on making first- and last-mile transport more accessible and sustainable. We operate a dockless bike-sharing platform across the island and are actively expanding into new revenue streams and partnerships with corporates, retail, and property owners. As a fast-growing team, we work closely across functions to test ideas quickly, build meaningful partnerships, and scale solutions that drive both business impact and greener urban living.


Role Overview

• We’re looking for a motivated and proactive Business Development Intern to support the expansion of Anywheel’s partnership ecosystem across Singapore. This role focuses on growing our Power Bank Sharing network into new locations and expanding Anywheel’s rewards programme (Partner Deals) with relevant partners across multiple industries.
• You’ll work closely with the Business Development team on partner outreach, onboarding, and experimentation with new growth ideas. This role is ideal for someone who is enthusiastic, comfortable with client-facing interactions, and eager to learn how partnerships are built from the ground up.


What You’ll Gain

• Hands-on experience in business development and partnerships at a leading micromobility company
• Exposure to real-world sales, negotiations, and onboarding processes
• Opportunity to contribute ideas that can be implemented systematically and scaled
• Mentorship from an experienced Business Development team
• Potential opportunity for conversion to a full-time role (performance-based)


Key Responsibilities

• Identify and explore new locations and partnership opportunities to expand Anywheel’s Power Bank Sharing service
• Support the growth of Anywheel’s Partner Deals rewards programme through merchant onboarding, ongoing account management, and relationship building
• Conduct outreach to partners across F&B, retail, gyms, schools, offices, and other high traffic venues
• Assist with the end-to-end partnership process, including initial contact, coordination, onboarding, and follow-ups
• Collaborate with the Business Development team to brainstorm, test, and execute new partnership acquisition strategies
• Research new markets, industries, and use cases that could add value to Anywheel users
• Support basic tracking and reporting of outreach efforts, partnerships onboarded, and expansion progress


Requirements

• Currently pursuing or recently graduated from a diploma or degree in Business, Marketing, Communications, or related fields
• Strong communication skills and comfortable with outreach and pitching via email, WhatsApp, calls, or in person
• Confident in presenting partnership ideas and able to adapt pitches based on different partner needs
• Flexible and open in negotiations, with the ability to find win-win solutions
• Creative, proactive, and willing to experiment with new ideas
• Good organisational skills and attention to detail
• Able to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment

title: Product Development Intern
raw_description:
Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition

Headquartered in Singapore, Agrocorp International is a leading integrated agricultural commodity and food solutions provider with presence in 16 countries and 25 offices. For over 30 years, Agrocorp has played a distinct role in nourishing the changing world with our agri-food supplies and solutions. To better serve our vision of nourishing a changing world, we’ve grown in size and business verticals from farm partnerships to processing plants and to retail market distribution. Our product range has widened to include a wide variety of commodities & food ingredients. In retail space, Sansar foods offers a wide range of premium pulses and HerbYvore is our plant based food range which includes non diary cheese, eggless egg, vegan ice cream etc.

Job Summary

As a Product Development Intern, you will gain hands-on experience in developing next-generation plant-based ingredients and products for Herbyvore, Agrocorp’s global sustainable food and ingredient brand. You will collaborate with R&D, Marketing, and Commercial teams to support product innovation, prototype development, and sensory evaluation—directly contributing to functional, nutritious, and market-ready plant-based solutions.

Responsibilities

  • Assist in developing formulations for plant-based products including protein powders, starches, and functional blends to support product innovation goals
  • Conduct ingredient testing, batch preparation, and optimization trials to improve product performance and quality
  • Document experimental results, ingredient functionality, and prototype iterations to maintain accurate R&D records
  • Research emerging plant-based ingredients, functional additives, and consumer trends to inform product development strategies
  • Perform sensory testing on taste, texture, and solubility to evaluate product quality and guide optimization efforts
  • Record and analyze trial data to support continuous product improvement and decision-making
  • Manage ingredient and sample inventory to ensure availability for R&D activities
  • Collaborate with Marketing to align product features with consumer insights and support claims development
  • Support Commercial team by preparing product sheets, launch briefs, and technical documentation for market readiness
  • Participate in cross-functional meetings, contributing insights from experimental data to influence product strategy
  • Prepare technical reports, formulation notes, and project documentation to facilitate R&D review and knowledge sharing
  • Track project timelines and progress to ensure timely delivery of assigned product development initiatives
  • Assist in compiling data for regulatory and quality assurance submissions to support compliance and product approval

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Currently pursuing or recently graduated with a degree in Food Science, Nutrition, Biotechnology, or related field
  • Basic knowledge of food formulations, plant-based ingredients, or functional nutrition
  • Strong interest in plant-based food innovation and sustainability
  • Detail-oriented with the ability to follow laboratory protocols accurately
  • Analytical mindset capable of documenting and interpreting experimental data effectively
  • Strong communication and teamwork skills to collaborate across diverse teams
  • Fluent in English

title: Junior Investment Analyst
raw_description:
Broad Peak is looking for a junior investment analyst to support its investment process focused on investing in publicly traded equities and debt instruments in the Pan Asia region. This is a graduate hire role and is not open to those with prior employment or anyone who has graduated for more than twelve months.  

What Will You Learn & Do In The Role

In this role, you will be required to support Broad Peak’s senior investment analysts in:

  • developing and maintaining financial models, performing investment due diligence and generally helping in tasks needing to be performed as part of the investment process; 
  • undertaking industry research and financial analysis;
  • monitoring news and other events related to investments in the portfolio; and
  • helping develop tools and executing on projects that will help improve the overall investment process.

What Attributes Should You Have

You should be able to demonstrate the following attributes:

  • rigorous research skills and good attention to detail;
  • excellent numerical skills and the ability to apply them;
  • proficiency in excel and the ability to build foundational financial models;
  • proficiency in using Bloomberg with the knowledge of how to extract financial information into spreadsheets and other destinations;
  • a keen interest in current affairs;
  • the ability to work effectively under pressure and within tight time constraints;
  • an inquisitive and analytical mind with the ability to problem-solve;
  • a good communicator and a team player who is able to listen to and take instructions effectively; and
  • having a genuine interest in investing (and possibly having done some personal investing already).

title: Senior/ Staff/ Principal Engineer, Product Engineering, Adv Data Analytics, Heterogeneous Integration Group (HIG), High Bandwidth Memory (HBM)
raw_description:
Key Responsibilities

  • Product Disposition Solutions: Develop, validate and deploy product-disposition solutions (wormhole) to ensure quality and reduce Defects Per Million (DPM) through a comprehensive product disposition program/workflow.

  • Data Analysis for Yield Improvement and Reliability: Utilize in-house statistical tools, machine learning, and AI for engineering data analysis to enhance yields and reliability, integrating these improvements within the Dispo workflow.

  • Deviation Alert System: Create a deviation alert system to monitor yield loss, characteristic/parameter trends, and wafer/die/cube shading.

  • Models/Algorithm Development: Develop state-of-the-art algorithms, including Machine Learning and Deep Learning models, to advance data mining and pattern recognition for quality enhancement, yield improvement, wafer/die level screening and efficiency enhancement.

  • Mentorship and Development: Actively mentor and develop team members to foster growth and development within the team and the organization.

  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with various cross-functional teams, including Fab, HBM Technology Development, HBM Design, System Development, and Quality/Reliability teams, to ensure the holistic development and successful shipping of end products.

  • Promotion of Innovation: Promote innovation and drive changes that provide a technical advantage over competitors, maintaining the company's competitive edge in the market.

  • AI/ML Advocate: Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop, deploy, and validate AI/ML models aimed at enhancing key performance indicators (KPIs) such as Quality, Cost, Cycle Time, and Scale.

Requirements

  • Bachelor's or Master's degree in Electrical and Electronics Engineering, Statistics, or related engineering fields.

  • At least 3 years of experience in data analytics/science industry. Experience in Product Engineering or the semiconductor industry is preferred.

  • Demonstrate strong leadership and technical skills with a deep understanding of data analytics, data extraction, and analysis tools such as JMP, Tableau, and Power BI.

  • Excellent communication skills to present findings and insights to non-technical stakeholders.

  • Proven track record of collaborative work within/across teams to address sophisticated business and engineering problems. Experience in mentoring and coaching team members to develop their skills.

  • Dedicated and highly motivated with a flexible approach towards adapting to different roles in a dynamic working environment (from leading the team to leading technical programs).

  • Strong sense of responsibility and accountability towards assigned role with professional work ethic.

  • Work on site in Singapore with international travel to Taiwan.

raw_title: Engineer CE PYE (Physical Failure Analysis/Fault Isolation)
title: Engineer CE PYE (Physical Failure Analysis/Fault Isolation)
raw_description:
We are currently looking for motivated individuals to join Micron Technology Inc. Central Engineering (CE) - Product Yield Enhancement (PYE) Engineering Team. As CE PYE Failure Analysis (FA) Engineer for Micron Technology, Inc., you will be responsible for providing full fault isolation and failure analysis support for qualifications, customer returns, new device introduction, and test failures. You will become the subject matter expert on fault isolation and physical failure analysis methods. As the CE PYE FA Engineer, you will be responsible for understanding all relevant methodologies to isolate electrical failures by working closely with Product Engineering Teams. You will also have a thorough understanding of the product architecture, process flow, and test sequence to work with Process Integration, Product, Test, and Quality Reliability Assurance Engineering groups ensuring physical defects are root-caused and classified.

Job Description:

Responsibilities:

•             Perform sophisticated electrical device characterization to determine electrical fail mechanisms.

•             Operate fault isolation equipment to accurately narrow down failure defect locations.

•             Conduct physical failure analysis to identify and classify physical defects.

•             Establish and hypothesize potential electrical or process fail mechanisms based on detailed electrical fail models and physical failure analysis characterization, supporting process improvement.

•             Collaborate closely with a diverse range of collaborators, including teams from frontend to backend and the Global Quality (GQ) department, to address and resolve defectivity issues.

•             Prepare and present comprehensive electrical and physical failure mechanisms on a weekly basis to drive continuous improvement.

•             Develop and implement new fault isolation and physical failure analysis techniques to stay ahead of industry standards.

•             Coordinate the maintenance and high uptime of fault isolation and physical failure analysis tools to ensure world-class operational efficiency.

Requirements:

•             Bachelor's or equivalent experience, Master's or equivalent experience, or PhD degree in Electrical/Electronic Engineering or Material Science.

•             Proven experience as a great team player with outstanding analytical and problem-solving skills and a strong drive to excel.

•             Excellent communication skills, with the ability to present complex information effectively in large meetings.

•             Highly motivated individuals with a passion for innovation and excellence.

•             Knowledge of fault isolation equipment, techniques, and physical failure analysis is a significant advantage.

•             Strong understanding of NAND/DRAM/HBM devices and knowledge of Fab processes is a desirable asset.

•             Ability to support shift rotation as required to meet operational needs.

Manages production support engineering for a specific product or group of products after transfer from design to high volume production. Interfaces with design, process, test and reliability engineering to solve problems. Sustains products with cost reduction and yield improvements.

title: SENIOR INDIRECT REGIONAL SUPPLIER MANAGER
raw_description:
Our vision is to transform how the world uses information to enrich life for all.

Micron Technology is a world leader in innovating memory and storage solutions that accelerate the transformation of information into intelligence, inspiring the world to learn, communicate and advance faster than ever.

The Global Procurement team is an integral engine to Micron’s growth, delivering best-in-class total cost and supply chain resiliency through cutting-edge predictive capabilities, risk analysis and a focus on sustainability and diversity. Our business goals are directly tied to the value we place on our team members – our greatest asset. We invest in our people through a skills-based learning and development model to create clear career pathways for development and career growth. We also strive to create an engaging and inclusive culture through building camaraderie and celebrating the diverse perspectives of all our global team members. Join us and discover why Micron’s Global Procurement team is the preferred destination to unleash your talent!

As a SENIOR INDIRECT REGIONAL SUPPLIER MANAGER, you will be responsible for developing a category strategy for managed categories, validating strategies with Global and Regional Category Managers/s and the respective internal partners and collaborators. You will be conducting strategic sourcing initiatives, plan and execute negotiations using supplier and industry analysis, benchmark and cost management. Through management of the category strategy, you will also be building trusted partnerships with internal stakeholders, driving best-in-class, cost, supply and resilience.

Key Responsibilities

Category Management & Strategic Sourcing

  • Implement processes to monitor stakeholder satisfaction with key suppliers.

  • Analyze sourcing trends and make recommendations for assigned categories.

  • Participate in supplier segmentation and strategic goal-setting.

  • Develop category strategies for managed categories and validate strategies with Global and Regional Category Managers/s and internal partners.

Cost Management & Analysis

  • Identify and evaluate category-specific savings/revenue levers

  • Drive category savings/revenue and scorecard metric targets

  • Provide market intelligence and category price trends to relevant internal stakeholders

  • Utilize should-cost models for assigned categories that are benchmarked and relevant to the global marketplace

Negotiation

  • Lead or participate in negotiations for contract extensions and renewals, renegotiation of prices, transfers, etc

  • Lead sourcing events/RFQ process into bundled negotiation

  • Incorporate savings targets and contract requirements into negotiations

  • Develop a contract strategy for suppliers in assigned categories and create, negotiate, monitor and enforce contracted terms and conditions

  • Conduct strategic sourcing initiatives, negotiations, and cost management using supplier and industry analysis.

Business Partnering

  • Collaborate with Stakeholders to Deliver Strategic Solutions

  • Develop and maintain expertise in the assigned spend categories through relationships with key stakeholders

  • Drive customer-centric approach to solving issues and representing Procurement

  • Develop and maintain collaborative partnerships to enhance cost-effectiveness, supply chain efficiency, and resilience.

Requirements

  • Minimum 3 years of experience in Engineering, Supply chain management

  • A relevant Bachelor's degree or equivalent experience.

  • Proven experience in category management and strategic sourcing.

  • Strong negotiating and analytical skills.

  • Strong communication skills and ability to effectively manage relationships with various individuals involved.

title: L1 IT Support Specialist (Retail & Office)
raw_description:
Responsibilities

  • Provide first-line IT support for corporate office and boutique users, ensuring smooth daily operations across retail and business environments.
  • Troubleshoot and resolve hardware, software, network, and connectivity issues for end-user devices such as laptops, printers, mobile devices, and peripherals.
  • Perform user and access administration, including email, Office 365 accounts, and system permissions, in line with IT policies.
  • Support device lifecycle activities such as setup, deployment, replacement, and decommissioning for staff movements and store openings.
  • Assist with routine IT operations including system updates, patching, basic maintenance, and health monitoring under guidance from senior IT members.
  • Maintain accurate IT asset records, manage software installations and licences, and coordinate with vendors for repairs, replacements, and warranties.
  • Support basic network and infrastructure matters (LAN, Wi-Fi, VPN), and assist during boutique renovations, relocations, or IT upgrades.
  • Log, track, and document incidents and resolutions in the ticketing system, support security and compliance requirements, and provide operational support for IT projects and go-live activities.

Requirements:

  • Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related discipline, with 2–5 years of hands-on IT support experience.
  • Experience supporting end users in office and/or retail environments, with working knowledge of Windows/macOS, Office 365, and basic networking.
  • Familiarity with IT service desk tools, asset management, hardware/software troubleshooting, and basic IT security practices; POS system exposure is an advantage.
  • Customer-focused, organised, and dependable, with strong communication skills and the flexibility to support a fast-paced retail environment, including occasional after-hours support when required.

Please submit your updated resume in Word format by using the Apply Now Button.
We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Executive (Sustainability) - JY
raw_description:
Job ID: 20009

  • Location: HarbourFront
  • Working hours: 9am - 6pm
  • Salary: up to $4,000 (Depending on experiences)

Job Description

Our client is seeking a dedicated Executive/Senior Executive (Sustainability) to join their team and drive their commitment to responsibly sourced natural rubber. If you are passionate about creating positive change and making a meaningful impact on the environment, we would love to connect with you!

  • Engage suppliers to ensure alignment with The Group’s sourcing policies, industry requirements, and regulations (e.g., EUDR, GPSNR). Includes conducting on-site audits with suppliers.
  • Conduct supply chain due diligence. Monitor risk assessments, identify priority areas for risk mitigation
  • Collaborate with stakeholders, including CSOs, governments, industry associations, and local communities, to drive adoption of best practices and other sustainable natural rubber initiatives
  • Support the development and implementation of capacity building projects with smallholders across The Group’s sourcing regions
  • Report on progress and performance of The Group’s sustainability efforts to internal and external audiences

Job Requirement

  • A Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Environmental Studies, Geography, or a related field.
  • 1-3 years’ experience in corporate sustainability (relevant internships included).
  • Comprehensive grasp of sustainability challenges in supply chains (e.g. deforestation, labour, carbon emissions).
  • Strong critical thinking and decision-making abilities. Self-directed, proactive attitude.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication in English. Fluency in Bahasa Indonesia or Thai is a plus to liaise with suppliers.
  • Experience with geospatial analysis (QGIS) is ideal.
  • Professional experience in the commodities sector and/or knowledge of the natural rubber industry is a significant advantage.
  • Prepared to travel frequently (up to 30%) to natural rubber sourcing regions, which will include visits to outlying areas.

Interested applicants, kindly send your updated resume to Joanne (R21101135) Joanne@pasona.com.sg with the email subject header "Job Application for "20009, Executive (Sustainability)".

Only shortlisted candidates will be notified, other applications will be updated to our database for future job opportunities.

title: Automation Sales Engineer
raw_description:
We’re Hiring: Automation Sales Engineer (In-House) – Electronics, Assembly & Test Segment

We are seeking a motivated Automation Sales Engineer (ASE) to drive customer development and manage an assigned portfolio from acquisition to retention. This role focuses on achieving sales targets, increasing market share, and ensuring customer satisfaction, working primarily through virtual channels and digital tools.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Achieve assigned sales turnover and growth targets

  • Proactively manage sales forecasts, opportunities, and customer portfolios

  • Build and maintain strong customer relationships through consultative, virtual selling

  • Engage with decision-makers and influencers at all levels of key accounts

  • Collaborate closely with Global Key Account Managers and cross-functional teams

  • Identify new business opportunities and support market development

  • Ensure accurate customer data and provide regular sales reports

Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Mechatronics, Electrical, or Mechanical) preferred.

  • 1–4 years of relevant sales or technical experience, ideally in pneumatics/electrical automation.

  • Strong communication, negotiation, and problem-solving skills.

  • Proactive, customer-oriented, and able to work independently as well as in a team.

  • Fluent in English and local language; proficient in MS Office and SAP.



What you can expect from us:

  • You will be joining a German multinational with national and global prestige, recognized as a technological.

  • Leader in the market of components and solutions for industrial automation.

  • The reconciliation of family and professional life is important to us, which is why we have implemented teleworking twice/week.

  • We respect rest time, favoring digital disconnection.

  • You will be able to have a broad vision of the business through participation in transversal projects in which, in addition to learning, you will be able to contribute.

  • Continuous improvement through training and professional development opportunities.

  • Working in a good working environment. We are looking for people with a proactive attitude, a desire to learn, a spirit of self-improvement, a capacity for work and determination in decision-making.

title: Geotechnical Design Engineer
raw_description:
We are looking for a Geotechnical Design Engineer with experience in geotechnical design, analysis and writing technical proposals. The Geotechnical Design Engineer should be knowledgeable in basic soil mechanics, as well as experienced in geotechnical analysis and design software.

Reporting to the Design Manager, you will have the opportunity to collaborate with the Business Development Team to provide design support for tenders. You will be expected to review tender specifications, requirements and draft the technical proposals. Likewise, you will be working closely with the Operations team to perform design-related coordination work, analysing geotechnical issues at project sites, and preparing detailed drawings for the site team.

To be successful as a Geotechnical Design Engineer, you should be independent, demonstrate strong analytical thinking and be able to have prompt, open and direct communications with internal and external stakeholders to achieve project expectations. Outstanding candidates should be meticulous, detail oriented, demonstrate willingness to learn and display a positive attitude with a drive for results.

Think you’ve got what it takes?

A day in the life of Geotechnical Design Engineer

Design support for tenders:

  • Review and summarize soil condition reports, tender specifications and requirements.
  • Identify and summarise geotechnical problems and technical risk.
  • Analyse geotechnical problems and draft technical proposal.

Design-related coordination work for ongoing projects:

  • Perform analysis and design in accordance with current legislation, standards and codes of practice
  • Prepare detailed construction drawings with the assistance of draughtsperson.
  • Conduct regular site visits to integrate design concept and actual ground conditions.
  • Quality Control (QC) review and verification between design assumptions and actual site conditions.
  • Carry out geotechnical analysis and design using tools such as Slope/W and Plaxis as well as Keller’s in-house software.
  • Conduct back analysis and research for future improvements based on actual performance and as-built data.
  • Prepare technical proposal for remedial work during construction when required.
  • Liaise with clients/consultants/ sub-contractors on technical related issues.

Note: This role requires candidates to perform regular site walks at outdoor construction sites

You will need:

  • A professional degree in Civil or Geotechnical Engineering
  • 1-3 years of relevant working experience in Geotechnical Engineering
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Powerpoint), AutoCAD, Plaxis 2D and Plaxis 3D
  • A valid Supervise Construction Work for WSH certificate (preferable)

What we offer:

  • Annual Leave, Medical Insurance, Medical Leave, Variable Bonus, Learning & Development Opportunities, Referral Rewards & Employee Assistance Programme
  • Global & Regional networking opportunities
  • Friendly team and enjoyable working environment

We encourage applications from the right candidates regardless of age, gender identity, sexual orientation, religion, belief or race. Learn more about Keller's Inclusion Commitment at https://www.keller.com/how-we-work/diversity-equity-and-inclusion

Find out more here:

https://www.keller.com/

title: Administration & Operations Executive - WCAN
raw_description:
Responsibilities:

  • Manage confidential student and assessment data in the student and learning management systems.
  • Prepare and coordinate all assessments, including quizzes, class tests, and exams, ensuring proper planning and documentation.
  • Liaise with faculty and module leaders to review and finalize assessment materials before administration.
  • Plan and allocate resources, including booking venues and assigning staff for assessment support.
  • Maintain accurate assessment schedules and ensure all materials are prepared for on-campus or online assessments.
  • Brief and coordinate staff to ensure smooth administration of assessments.
  • Oversee the conduct of assessments, ensuring procedures are followed and addressing any issues that arise.
  • Manage post-assessment processes, including tracking marking timelines, liaising with faculty for moderation, and updating student records.
  • Prepare and coordinate meetings with relevant boards or committees, ensuring all required documentation is complete.
  • Handle assessment-related administrative tasks, procurement, and finance matters according to timelines.
  • Support the launch of new programs by planning initial assessments and drafting operational workflows.
  • Liaise with internal and external stakeholders to establish clear processes and timelines for assessments.
  • Coordinate with outsourced partners to monitor and track operational processes.
  • Assign and deploy staff for online and on-campus assessment support.
  • Perform any other duties as assigned by the reporting officer.


Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in any field.
  • 1–2 years of relevant experience is a plus; entry-level candidates are welcome to apply.
  • Experience in examination management, academic operations, or a similar administrative environment is preferred.
  • Excellent organizational and multitasking abilities.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills to liaise effectively with various stakeholders.
  • Detail-oriented with the ability to maintain accurate records.
  • Capable of prioritizing workload and meeting deadlines.
  • Able to work under pressure during peak assessment periods.
  • Willing to work 1–2 hours beyond regular working hours on weekdays or Saturdays when requested.


We regret only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. All applications will be handled confidentially. By submitting your application, you agree to the collection, use, retention, and disclosure of your personal information to prospective employers.

title: CRM Support Specialist
raw_description:
Company Overview

ESET® provides cutting-edge digital security to prevent attacks before they happen. By combining the power of AI and human expertise, ESET stays ahead of known and emerging cyberthreats — securing businesses, critical infrastructure, and individuals. Whether it’s endpoint, cloud or mobile protection, our AI-native, cloud-first solutions and services remain highly effective and easy to use. Driven by science, ESET technology includes robust detection and response, ultra-secure encryption, and multifactor authentication. With 24/7 real-time defense and strong local support, we keep users safe and businesses running without interruption. An ever-evolving digital landscape demands a progressive, evidence-based approach to security. ESET is committed to world-class scientific research and powerful threat intelligence, backed by R&D centers and a strong global partner network.

ESET's global headquarters are in Bratislava, Slovakia, supporting its worldwide operations. The Asia-Pacific (APAC) region is managed from ESET’s regional headquarters in Singapore, with local offices in Tokyo, Sydney, and the SAARC region and is represented by an extensive global partner network.

Get to know us better – ESET.com, YouTube, Twitter, LinkedIn, Great Place To Work

General Position Overview

In APAC, our fast-growing, multicultural team spans Singapore, India, and Australia—working together across time zones to support customers and colleagues with resilience, integrity, and innovation.
We are now looking for a Systems Support Specialist to join our APAC team. This role is perfect for someone who enjoys solving problems, supporting internal users, and working with business systems (including CRM/Salesforce).

Job Description
Daily Support & User Engagement

  • Provide first-line and second-line support for business systems through tickets, walk-ups, and virtual channels
  • Communicate with end-users to clarify incidents and requests, ensuring timely and accurate resolution
  • Investigate, troubleshoot, and resolve system incidents and user queries

CRM & Systems Operations

  • Support CRM administration (Salesforce experience is a plus)
  • Assist in reviewing, testing, and validating new or modified business systems
  • Develop test data, evaluate results, and provide structured feedback

Documentation & Process Improvement

  • Create, maintain, and distribute user manuals, guides, and support documentation
  • Maintain and enhance system support processes and knowledge bases
  • Monitor and report on incident trends to help improve service quality

Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in Information Systems or a related field
  • 2 years of experience in business systems support or application support
  • Experience with Salesforce or CEM platform would be advantageous
  • Fluent in business English
  • Strong time management and ability to prioritize in a multi-country environment
  • Comfortable working in cross-functional, multicultural teams
  • Reliable, detail-oriented, proactive, and willing to learn
