title: Investment Banking Analyst Discovery Programme 2026 Singapore
raw_description:
The Discovery Programme is designed for students to experience what it means to be at Barclays firsthand. Through interactive sessions, students gain exposure to the industry, develop professional skills, and build connections with Barclays employees.

This programme is tailored for high-achieving students who want to explore the impact they can make in a global investment bank. It’s a perfect stepping stone to our 10-week internship as you can be assessed for Summer Internships in 2027 before anyone else.

Investment Banking


Every day, corporations worldwide come to Barclays for our banking expertise. We help drive global commerce by providing comprehensive financial, advisory and capital-raising services. Working on a broad range of corporate clients, our bankers deliver holistic, structured solutions that help our clients deliver on their goals; whether that’s through mergers and acquisitions, equity and debt origination, loans, capital raising, structured solutions or risk management.

What you can expect


Part 1: Tuesday, 17th March 2026

  • In-person programme
  • Join seminars and workshops, where you’ll learn about financial services, your chosen business area, the recruitment process and Barclays as an employer
  • Participate in specific breakout sessions for your business area, where you'll gain insights of what you can expect from the Summer Internship in 2027
  • Build your understanding of the assessment process and next steps
  • Connect with people from across our business, including current graduates who have already made the journey that you are starting out on
  • Attend dedicated networking sessions with senior leaders and graduates aligned to your chosen business area


Part 2: Multiple dates during the first two weeks of April (dates will be shared if you are shortlisted)

  • You can be assessed for the 2027 Summer Internship by taking part in a series of interviews


What we look for

  • You are graduating between December 2027 and June 2028
  • You have a GPA of 3.2 or above
  • You can be studying any degree discipline
  • You are curious to understand how a global bank adapts in a constantly evolving environment


Application Deadline: Monday, 2nd March 2026

This is a future position to start in June 2027

Ready to apply?

Visit search.jobs.barclays to learn more about our opportunities and apply.

Barclays is required by law to confirm that you have the Legal Right to Work in any role that you apply for. If you currently hold a work visa sponsored by Barclays, or you would require sponsorship from Barclays, you must declare this as part of your application. Sponsored visas are role and entity specific, and any changes must be reviewed. It is important that you ensure you are working on the correct visa at all times. Failure to accurately disclose your visa status or Legal Right to Work may result in your application or any employment offer being withdrawn at any time.

It is the policy of Barclays to ensure equal employment opportunity without discrimination or harassment on the basis of race, color, creed, religion, national origin, alienage or citizenship status, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital or domestic/civil partnership status, disability, protected veteran status, genetic information, or any other basis protected by law.

Purpose of the role

To participate in the day-to-day activities of the investment banking division. Gain broad exposure to different types of clients, transactions and focus on specialist areas.

Accountabilities

  • Collaboration on live deals, client pitches, support the team with advising on a broad range of corporate clients,. delivering holistic, structured solutions to deliver on client goals.
  • Collaboration with cross-functional teams to support business initiatives.
  • Participation in training and development programs to enhance skills and knowledge.
  • Identification of opportunities, development of business cases, management of the deployment and launch of new products and services for investment banking.
  • Management of client relationships and provision of customer service support to clients and customers, under steadily decreasing supervision, responding to questions about products and services, processing of transactions.
  • Development and implementation of financial models and strategies that support in decision making in investment banking. Understand how businesses work and learn from experts in the industry.

Analyst Expectations

  • To perform prescribed activities in a timely manner and to a high standard consistently driving continuous improvement.
  • Requires in-depth technical knowledge and experience in their assigned area of expertise
  • Thorough understanding of the underlying principles and concepts within the area of expertise
  • They lead and supervise a team, guiding and supporting professional development, allocating work requirements and coordinating team resources.
  • If the position has leadership responsibilities, People Leaders are expected to demonstrate a clear set of leadership behaviours to create an environment for colleagues to thrive and deliver to a consistently excellent standard. The four LEAD behaviours are: L – Listen and be authentic, E – Energise and inspire, A – Align across the enterprise, D – Develop others.
  • OR for an individual contributor, they develop technical expertise in work area, acting as an advisor where appropriate.
  • Will have an impact on the work of related teams within the area.
  • Partner with other functions and business areas.
  • Takes responsibility for end results of a team’s operational processing and activities.
  • Escalate breaches of policies / procedure appropriately.
  • Take responsibility for embedding new policies/ procedures adopted due to risk mitigation.
  • Advise and influence decision making within own area of expertise.
  • Take ownership for managing risk and strengthening controls in relation to the work you own or contribute to. Deliver your work and areas of responsibility in line with relevant rules, regulation and codes of conduct.
  • Maintain and continually build an understanding of how own sub-function integrates with function, alongside knowledge of the organisations products, services and processes within the function.
  • Demonstrate understanding of how areas coordinate and contribute to the achievement of the objectives of the organisation sub-function.
  • Make evaluative judgements based on the analysis of factual information, paying attention to detail.
  • Resolve problems by identifying and selecting solutions through the application of acquired technical experience and will be guided by precedents.
  • Guide and persuade team members and communicate complex / sensitive information.
  • Act as contact point for stakeholders outside of the immediate function, while building a network of contacts outside team and external to the organisation.

All colleagues will be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Values of Respect, Integrity, Service, Excellence and Stewardship – our moral compass, helping us do what we believe is right. They will also be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Mindset – to Empower, Challenge and Drive – the operating manual for how we behave.

Accountabilities Collaboration on live deals, client pitches, support the team with advising on a broad range of corporate clients,. delivering holistic, structured solutions to deliver on client goals. Collaboration with cross-functional teams to support business initiatives. Participation in training and development programs to enhance skills and knowledge. Identification of opportunities, development of business cases, management of the deployment and launch of new products and services for investment banking. Management of client relationships and provision of customer service support to clients and customers, under steadily decreasing supervision, responding to questions about products and services, processing of transactions. Development and implementation of financial models and strategies that support in decision making in investment banking. Understand how businesses work and learn from experts in the industry. Analyst Expectations To perform prescribed activities in a timely manner and to a high standard consistently driving continuous improvement. Requires in-depth technical knowledge and experience in their assigned area of expertise Thorough understanding of the underlying principles and concepts within the area of expertise They lead and supervise a team, guiding and supporting professional development, allocating work requirements and coordinating team resources. If the position has leadership responsibilities, People Leaders are expected to demonstrate a clear set of leadership behaviours to create an environment for colleagues to thrive and deliver to a consistently excellent standard. The four LEAD behaviours are: L – Listen and be authentic, E – Energise and inspire, A – Align across the enterprise, D – Develop others. OR for an individual contributor, they develop technical expertise in work area, acting as an advisor where appropriate. Will have an impact on the work of related teams within the area. Partner with other functions and business areas. Takes responsibility for end results of a team’s operational processing and activities. Escalate breaches of policies / procedure appropriately. Take responsibility for embedding new policies/ procedures adopted due to risk mitigation. Advise and influence decision making within own area of expertise. Take ownership for managing risk and strengthening controls in relation to the work you own or contribute to. Deliver your work and areas of responsibility in line with relevant rules, regulation and codes of conduct. Maintain and continually build an understanding of how own sub-function integrates with function, alongside knowledge of the organisations products, services and processes within the function. Demonstrate understanding of how areas coordinate and contribute to the achievement of the objectives of the organisation sub-function. Make evaluative judgements based on the analysis of factual information, paying attention to detail. Resolve problems by identifying and selecting solutions through the application of acquired technical experience and will be guided by precedents. Guide and persuade team members and communicate complex / sensitive information. Act as contact point for stakeholders outside of the immediate function, while building a network of contacts outside team and external to the organisation. All colleagues will be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Values of Respect, Integrity, Service, Excellence and Stewardship – our moral compass, helping us do what we believe is right. They will also be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Mindset – to Empower, Challenge and Drive – the operating manual for how we behave.

title: Operations Coordinator - WCAN
raw_description:
Responsibilities:

  • Manage confidential student and assessment data in the student and learning management systems.
  • Prepare and coordinate all assessments, including quizzes, class tests, and exams, ensuring proper planning and documentation.
  • Liaise with faculty and module leaders to review and finalize assessment materials before administration.
  • Plan and allocate resources, including booking venues and assigning staff for assessment support.
  • Maintain accurate assessment schedules and ensure all materials are prepared for on-campus or online assessments.
  • Brief and coordinate staff to ensure smooth administration of assessments.
  • Oversee the conduct of assessments, ensuring procedures are followed and addressing any issues that arise.
  • Manage post-assessment processes, including tracking marking timelines, liaising with faculty for moderation, and updating student records.
  • Prepare and coordinate meetings with relevant boards or committees, ensuring all required documentation is complete.
  • Handle assessment-related administrative tasks, procurement, and finance matters according to timelines.
  • Support the launch of new programs by planning initial assessments and drafting operational workflows.
  • Liaise with internal and external stakeholders to establish clear processes and timelines for assessments.
  • Coordinate with outsourced partners to monitor and track operational processes.
  • Assign and deploy staff for online and on-campus assessment support.
  • Perform any other duties as assigned by the reporting officer.


Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in any field.
  • 1–2 years of relevant experience is a plus; entry-level candidates are welcome to apply.
  • Experience in examination management, academic operations, or a similar administrative environment is preferred.
  • Excellent organizational and multitasking abilities.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills to liaise effectively with various stakeholders.
  • Detail-oriented with the ability to maintain accurate records.
  • Capable of prioritizing workload and meeting deadlines.
  • Able to work under pressure during peak assessment periods.
  • Willing to work 1–2 hours beyond regular working hours on weekdays or Saturdays when requested.


We regret only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. All applications will be handled confidentially. By submitting your application, you agree to the collection, use, retention, and disclosure of your personal information to prospective employers.

title: Treasury Officer
raw_description:
Job Title: Treasury Officer
Location: Singapore
Department: Group Treasury Centre
Reports To: General Manager / Group Treasurer

About the Role

We are looking for a detail-oriented and responsible Treasury Officer to join our Treasury Centre team. The role will focus on the administration and maintenance of bank account/broker account records, signatory information, user access, and electronic banking tokens across multiple group entities.

Key Responsibilities

1. Bank Account Management

  • Maintain an up-to-date master list of all bank accounts within the Group (approximately 60 accounts), including account details, currency, and status.
  • Manage and update authorized signatories, account mandates, and approval limits in coordination with group companies and banks.
  • Administer internet banking user access, including the setup, modification, and removal of initiators and approvers.
  • Track and control the issuance and return of physical and digital bank tokens, ensuring security and compliance.
  • Liaise with banks for account opening, closure, and mandate updates.
  • Prepare regular account reports and ensure accuracy and consistency across all records.
  • Support internal and external audit requests relating to bank accounts and authorization.
  • Assist the Treasury team in maintaining compliance with internal policies and banking requirements.

2. Banking & Payment Operations

  • Execute and validate domestic and cross-border payments in a timely manner
  • Maintain bank account documentation
  • Coordinate with banks to resolve operational issues and maintain smooth workflows

3. Internal Lending Support

  • Assist with intercompany loan disbursements and repayments according to group policies.
  • Track loan balances and interest charges, supporting treasury schedules.

4. Reporting & Compliance

  • Assist with month-end closing and reconciliation of treasury-related accounts
  • Ensure compliance with MAS regulations, internal audit requirements, and group policies

Requirements

  • Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related discipline.
  • 2–4 years of relevant experience in treasury operations, bank account administration, or corporate banking preferred.
  • Strong attention to detail and good organizational skills.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Excel.
  • Good communication skills in English (Chinese proficiency is a strong plus for communicating with Headquarters and counterparts in Greater China).
  • Responsible, reliable, and able to work independently with minimal supervision.

raw_title: Quantitative Analytics Associate Discovery Programme 2026 Singapore
title: Quantitative Analytics Associate Discovery Programme 2026 Singapore
raw_description:
The Discovery Programme is designed for students to experience what it means to be at Barclays firsthand. Through interactive sessions, students gain exposure to the industry, develop professional skills, and build connections with Barclays employees.

This programme is tailored for high-achieving students who want to explore the impact they can make in a global investment bank. It’s a perfect stepping stone to our 6-month internship as you can be assessed for the Off Cycle Internship in 2027 before anyone else.

Quantitative Analytics


Our Quantitative Analytics team is a global organisation of highly specialized modellers and developers responsible for researching, innovating, developing, testing, implementing, and supporting all quantitative models used for valuation and risk management across all asset classes. The ability to articulate your ideas will be crucial as you work closely with business partners to develop models and tools. Joining a group that partners with traders and stakeholders across the bank, you’ll gain the widest exposure to a variety of modelling techniques and instruments to help drive business strategy.

What you can expect


Part 1: Tuesday, 17th March 2026

  • In-person programme
  • Join seminars and workshops, where you’ll learn about financial services, your chosen business area, the recruitment process and Barclays as an employer
  • Participate in specific breakout sessions for your business area, where you'll gain insights of what you can expect from the Off Cycle Internship in 2027
  • Build your understanding of the assessment process and next steps
  • Connect with people from across our business, including current graduates who have already made the journey that you are starting out on
  • Attend dedicated networking sessions with senior leaders and graduates aligned to your chosen business area


Part 2: Multiple dates during the first two weeks of April (dates will be shared if you are shortlisted)

  • You can be assessed for the 2027 Off Cycle Internship (6 months) by taking part in a series of interviews


What we look for

  • You are graduating between December 2027 and June 2028
  • You have a GPA of 3.2 or above
  • You are pursuing a Masters or a PhD in a technical discipline such as Physics, Mathematics, Operations Research, Quantitative Finance, Economics, Statistics, Stochastic Calculus, Computer Science, or other STEM subjects
  • Ideally, you'll have strong programming skills (particularly in Java, C++, or Python) along with a knack for logical thinking and creative problem solving. You'll be great at communicating and collaborating
  • You are curious to understand how a global bank adapts in a constantly evolving environment


Application Deadline: Monday, 2nd March 2026

This is a future position to start in June 2027

Ready to apply?

Visit search.jobs.barclays to learn more about our opportunities and apply.

Barclays is required by law to confirm that you have the Legal Right to Work in any role that you apply for. If you currently hold a work visa sponsored by Barclays, or you would require sponsorship from Barclays, you must declare this as part of your application. Sponsored visas are role and entity specific, and any changes must be reviewed. It is important that you ensure you are working on the correct visa at all times. Failure to accurately disclose your visa status or Legal Right to Work may result in your application or any employment offer being withdrawn at any time.

It is the policy of Barclays to ensure equal employment opportunity without discrimination or harassment on the basis of race, color, creed, religion, national origin, alienage or citizenship status, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, marital or domestic/civil partnership status, disability, protected veteran status, genetic information, or any other basis protected by law.

Purpose of the role

To support the day-to-day operations of the Quantitative Analytics (QA) group by providing analytical insights and expertise that will help our business leaders and stakeholders make informed decisions, support existing trading strategies, develop new products strategies and services, as well as identify new market opportunities

Accountabilities

  • Application of the latest quantitative techniques to solve business problems, research, development, and implementation of new models and solutions and improve the bank’s computing and data infrastructure.
  • Collaboration with cross-functional teams and senior management to progress business initiatives.
  • Participation in training and development programs to enhance skills and knowledge. Conduct research to support strategic decision making, prepare presentations and reports to communicate research findings, and collaborate with senior management to implement research findings.
  • Design, development of the quantitative research framework using domain specific languages.
  • Participation in technical design and development of the global team’s quantitative research systems, research notebooks and products.

Assistant Vice President Expectations

  • To advise and influence decision making, contribute to policy development and take responsibility for operational effectiveness. Collaborate closely with other functions/ business divisions.
  • Lead a team performing complex tasks, using well developed professional knowledge and skills to deliver on work that impacts the whole business function. Set objectives and coach employees in pursuit of those objectives, appraisal of performance relative to objectives and determination of reward outcomes
  • If the position has leadership responsibilities, People Leaders are expected to demonstrate a clear set of leadership behaviours to create an environment for colleagues to thrive and deliver to a consistently excellent standard. The four LEAD behaviours are: L – Listen and be authentic, E – Energise and inspire, A – Align across the enterprise, D – Develop others.
  • OR for an individual contributor, they will lead collaborative assignments and guide team members through structured assignments, identify the need for the inclusion of other areas of specialisation to complete assignments. They will identify new directions for assignments and/ or projects, identifying a combination of cross functional methodologies or practices to meet required outcomes.
  • Consult on complex issues; providing advice to People Leaders to support the resolution of escalated issues.
  • Identify ways to mitigate risk and developing new policies/procedures in support of the control and governance agenda.
  • Take ownership for managing risk and strengthening controls in relation to the work done.
  • Perform work that is closely related to that of other areas, which requires understanding of how areas coordinate and contribute to the achievement of the objectives of the organisation sub-function.
  • Collaborate with other areas of work, for business aligned support areas to keep up to speed with business activity and the business strategy.
  • Engage in complex analysis of data from multiple sources of information, internal and external sources such as procedures and practises (in other areas, teams, companies, etc).to solve problems creatively and effectively.
  • Communicate complex information. 'Complex' information could include sensitive information or information that is difficult to communicate because of its content or its audience.
  • Influence or convince stakeholders to achieve outcomes.

All colleagues will be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Values of Respect, Integrity, Service, Excellence and Stewardship – our moral compass, helping us do what we believe is right. They will also be expected to demonstrate the Barclays Mindset – to Empower, Challenge and Drive – the operating manual for how we behave.

title: Admin Associate / Executive (Social Service)
raw_description:
Monday to Friday

8:00 AM – 5:30 PM

Location: Toa Payoh/ Elizabeth Drive

Job Responsibilities

  • Gather social and financial background information of residents and/or their families prior to the application of financial assistance schemes.
  • Update and maintain accurate records in the referral and enquiries database and information system.
  • Ensure timely submission of financial assistance applications such as Medifund, MFEC (Medical Fee Exemption Card), IDAPE (Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly), PGDAS (Pioneer Generation Disability Assistance Scheme), and others.
  • Act as the main point of contact and liaison between residents, families, and healthcare institutions to coordinate referrals and process applications for potential residents.
  • Ensure proper documentation of case files, referrals, and other relevant documents for effective case management and audit purposes.
  • Perform data entry and regularly update information to maintain accuracy of databases.
  • Handle administrative tasks including enquiries, referrals, and admissions.
  • Facilitate residents’ transfers to other facilities or healthcare institutions, as well as coordinate discharge processes.
  • Undertake any other duties or projects as assigned by the supervisor.
  • May required to be stationed in the office to handle incoming phone calls, walk-in enquiries, and other front-desk administrative duties as and when required

Requirements

  • Minimum 1 year of relevant working experience in social service, community service, healthcare, or related sectors.
  • Prior exposure to financial assistance schemes (e.g. Medifund, MFEC, IDAPE, PGDAS) will be an advantage
  • Diploma or Degree in Social Work, Social Sciences, Community Services, Healthcare Administration, or related disciplines preferred.
  • Comfortable liaising with residents, families, healthcare institutions, and external agencies.
  • Strong administrative and documentation skills, with attention to detail for case files and audit purposes.

Candidates pursuing a part-time diploma or degree in a relevant field (e.g. Social Work or Psychology) are welcome to apply


Interested candidates please WhatsApp me at 8779 6100 or send in your resume to willie.goh@swiftsearchglobal.com

We regret to inform you that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Strong administrative and documentation skills , with attention to detail for case files and audit purposes. title: Sample Coordinator - Bioanalytical
raw_description:
Responsibilities:

  • Performs any combination of sample accession tasks, including opening, entering, tracking, labeling, shipping and disposition of samples. 
  • Reviews sample inventory against samples to ensure accuracy and communicate discrepancies.
  • Communicates confirmation of sample receipt to clients, if appropriate. 
  • Distributes sample information and paperwork.
  • Manage reference standards and blank matrix in system as needed.
  • Solves problems independently, contacting internal or external clients for clarification as needed.
  • Handles internal and external customer inquiries and requests
  • Approved as an authorized environmental system monitor and after hours responder. 
  • Assists in maintaining the logbooks, if appropriate, for the department and ensures accuracy of information they contain.
  • Certified to be an “after hours sample receipt” team member, as appropriate.
  • Participates on teams and committees representing the sample management group.
  • Participates in client visits as needed, assists in communicating technical information.
  • Assist Management with responding to QAU, client and regulatory questions.
  • Work with procurement to order lab supply for the studies.
  • Maintains a clean and safe laboratory work environment.
  • Participates in process improvement initiatives.
  • May assists in writing text for SOP, protocols, methods or reports.
  • Contributes towards a cohesive team environment.
  • Introduces and develops improvement ideas for the group, working with management.
  • Performs other related duties as assigned.

Requirements:

  • Bachelor of Science/Arts (BS/BA) degree in chemistry, logistic, supply chain or related field.
  • Experience may be substituted for education.
  • Ability to perform basic computer skills (e.g., Word, Excel).
  • Expectations to liaise with China counterparts, proficiency in Chinese is advantageous

raw_description:
We are looking for a Account Assistant who will be responsible for handling day-to-day accounting functions, including bookkeeping, general ledger entries, accounts payable/receivable, and invoice management. The role supports the Finance Manager in preparing financial reports, monitoring payments, and ensuring compliance with company policies and accounting standards.

Key Responsibilities

  • Maintain day-to-day invoicing, reconciling bank statements.
  • Handle accounts receivable, including invoice preparation.
  • Assist in monthly closing activities.
  • Support the Finance Manager in preparing financial statements and reports.
  • Ensure all accounting records are accurate, complete, and compliant with company policies.
  • Follow instructions and guidelines to ensure smooth daily financial operations.

  • Diploma/Degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field.
  • Minimum 1 year of relevant working experience (fresh graduates with strong interest in accounting may be considered).

  • Detail-oriented, organized, and able to meet deadlines.
  • Good communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Ability to work with the team.
  • High level of integrity and professionalism in handling confidential information.

title: Junior Product Designer - Jewellery
raw_description:
As a Junior Product Designer at Ask & Embla, you will support the design and development of innovative, trend-relevant jewelry that aligns with the brand’s vision and customer identity. You will assist in research, conceptualization, and technical design work while learning to translate creative direction into production-ready deliverables. This role is ideal for someone with foundational jewelry design skills who is eager to grow into a full-fledged designer role.

What You'll Do

Design Research & Concept Development

  • Assist with researching trends, customer preferences, materials, and techniques across industries to inform design direction.
  • Contribute to the development of mood boards, design decks, and seasonal concept presentations.
  • Support the Creative Director and Product Design team by sketching and proposing jewelry concepts aligned with the brand’s vision and assortment plan.

Technical Design & CAD Support

  • Learn and apply CAD tools (e.g. Rhino, ZBrush) to translate sketches into 3D models and technical drawings.
  • Assist in preparing tech packs and design specifications for sampling and production.
  • Provide support in the creation of prototypes, including working with 3D printing processes.

Collaboration & Iteration

  • Work closely with the Product Design and Product Development team to refine designs during sampling stages.
  • Incorporate feedback into revisions of sketches, 3D models, and technical documentation.
  • Participate in cross-functional meetings, offering input from a design perspective.

Project Support & Administration

  • Help maintain organized design records, references, and material libraries.
  • Assist in compiling decks, reports, and design documentation for internal use.
  • Support the design team in day-to-day tasks to ensure smooth workflow and timely delivery.

Who You Are

  • A graduate of a product design or industrial design course, and experience in a product design or jewelry design role, is preferred.
  • Foundational knowledge of jewelry design principles, materials, and manufacturing processes.
  • Familiarity with jewelry CAD software and 3D prototyping - Rhino and ZBrush preferred.
  • Strong sketching and visualization skills, with attention to detail and creativity.
  • Ability to manage multiple tasks under deadlines while maintaining design quality.
  • A collaborative mindset with a passion for learning and growing in jewelry design.

About Ask & Embla

Who We Are:

Ask & Embla is a rapidly growing Alternative Body Jewelry brand based in Singapore with a collective following of more than 500,000 across our social media channels. As an E-commerce brand, we ship internationally to a diverse range of customers and we are proud to be one of the leading brands worldwide in the alternative jewelry space.

At Ask & Embla, we’re passionate about creating a company that brings out the best in everyone and where individuals love to work and grow as a team. That means constantly learning and building new things, being flexible about how and where we work, and empowering each other to do the proudest work of our lives.

Ask & Embla, led by an ex-Googler and ex-Amazonian, has dozens of employees across multiple countries. We are building the foundation to expand several business verticals beyond e-commerce, while supporting existing business functions for fulfilling thousands of orders each month.

Ready to Join Us?

If you're a results-oriented professional who thrives in a fast-paced environment, we encourage you to apply!

Learn More about Ask & Embla:

Website - https://askandembla.net/

Instagram - https://www.instagram.com/askemblastore/

Facebook - https://www.facebook.com/AskEmblastore/

Pinterest - https://www.pinterest.com/askemblastore/

TikTok - https://www.tiktok.com/@askandembla_official

Why Work With Us?

At Ask & Embla we learn and build fast together, sharing the purpose and passion of creating not just beautiful, expressive and sustainably-made jewelry, but a community that celebrates the misfits, the believers, the round pegs in square holes and everyone in between. Everyone deserves to own who they are; we create the jewelry so they can do it with pride.

Job Details

Job Type: Full-time Pay: Ranging from SGD 2,600 - 3,200 per month

Benefits:

  • Hybrid setup
  • Health insurance
  • Dental insurance
  • Paid time-off
  • Employee discounts
  • Professional development opportunities

Schedule:

  • Day shift

Location:

  • Singapore

title: Marketing Intern
raw_description:
Marketing Internship Opportunity

Duration: 3 - 6 months

Location: Hybrid

Availability: Open to diploma students and undergraduates

Are you a storyteller at heart with a creative eye for design? Join our small but dynamic marketing team! This isn't your typical "coffee-run" internship. You’ll be right in the thick of it—brainstorming campaigns, filming content, and helping us build a vibrant community. If you are proactive, love lifestyle branding, and aren’t afraid to get your hands dirty, we want to hear from you.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Graphic Design & Visuals: Design eye-catching assets, including stickers, social media graphics, and marketing materials that resonate with our audience.
  • Creative Content Creation: Collaborate with the team to conceptualize, script, and film short-form videos (TikTok/Reels style) and static content for cross-channel campaigns.
  • Community & Events: Comfortable with engaging users face-to-face and helping to execute on-ground activations.
  • Brand Voice: Ensure all written copy and visual designs align with our "fun and relatable" brand personality.

You’ll be a great fit if you are:

  • Undergraduate student or diploma holder in Marketing/Communications, Graphic Design, Media, or related fields.
  • You move fast and aren’t afraid to get your hands dirty — whether it’s writing copy or filming a video.
  • A highly motivated team player who enjoy working in a collaborative environment but is also comfortable taking initiative and working independently.
  • Have a flair for writing and can articulate ideas clearly in a tone that’s fun and relatable.
  • Can juggle multiple tasks, meet deadlines, and thrive in a fast-paced environment.
  • Bonus if you have skills in Motion Graphics, Adobe Illustrator, Adobe Premiere Pro or other similar softwares. Extra bonus if you love cycling!

Apply now with your resume and portfolio with all relevant works to judith@anywheel.sg

raw_title: Research Scientist Project Intern (Multimodal Interaction & World Model) - 2026 Start (PhD)
title: Research Scientist Project Intern (Multimodal Interaction & World Model) - 2026 Start (PhD)
raw_description:
Responsibilities

About the team
Welcome to the Multimodal Interaction & World Model team. Our mission is to solve the challenge of multimodal intelligence、virtual reality world interaction in AI. We conduct cutting-edge research on areas such as Foundations and applications of multimodal understanding models, Multimodal agent and inference, Unified models for generation and understanding, World Model. Our team is comprising experienced research scientists and engineers who are dedicated to developing models that boast human-level multimodal understanding and interaction capabilities. The team also aspires to advance the exploration and development of multimodal assistant products. We foster a feedback-driven environment to continuously enhance our foundation technologies. Come join us in shaping the future of AI and transforming the product experience for users worldwide.

As a project intern, you will have the opportunity to engage in impactful short-term projects that provide you with a glimpse of professional real-world experience. You will gain practical skills through on-the-job learning in a fast-paced work environment and develop a deeper understanding of your career interests.

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis - we encourage you to apply early.Successful candidates must be able to commit to at least 3 months long internship period.

Responsibilities
- Explore and research multi-modal understanding, generative, machine learning, reinforcement learning, AIGC, computer vision, artificial intelligence and other cutting-edge technologies.
- Explore the basic model of large-scale/ultra-large-scale multi-modal understanding and generation interweaving, and carry out extreme system optimization; Data construction, instruction fine-tuning, preference alignment, model optimization; Improve the ability of data synthesis, scalable oversight, model reasoning and planning, build a comprehensive, objective and accurate evaluation system, and explore and improve the ability of large models.
- Explore and break through the advanced capabilities of multi-modal models and world models, including but not limited to multi-modal RAG, visual COT and Agent, and build a universal multi-modal Agent for GUI/ games and other virtual worlds.
- Use pre-training, simulation and other technologies to model various environments in the virtual/real world, provide the basic ability of multi-modal interactive exploration, promote application landing, and develop new technologies and new products with artificial intelligence technology as the core.

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications:
- PhD degree in computer, electronics, mathematics and other related majors.
- Have in-depth research in one or more fields such as computer vision, multimodal, AIGC, machine learning, rendering generation, etc..
- Excellent analytical and problem solving skills; Ability to solve large model training and application problems; Ability to explore solutions independently.
- With good communication and collaboration skills, proactive work, able to harmoniously cooperate with the team to explore new technologies and promote technological progress.

Preferred Qualifications:
- With excellent basic algorithms, solid foundation of machine learning, familiar with CV, AIGC, NLP, RL, ML and other fields of technology, CVPR, ECCV, ICCV, NeurIPS, ICLR, SIGGRAPH or SIGGRAPH Asia and other top conferences/journals published papers are preferred.
- With excellent coding ability, proficient in C/C++ or Python programming language, ACM/ICPC, NOI/IOl, Top Coder, Kaggle and other competition winners preferred.
- In the fields of multimodal, large model, basic model, world model, RL, rendering generation, leading projects with too much influence is preferred.

By submitting an application for this role, you accept and agree to our global applicant privacy policy, which may be accessed here: https://jobs.bytedance.com/en/legal/privacy.

If you have any questions, please reach out to us at apac-earlycareers@bytedance.com.

title: Financial Advisor (Insurance) - Flexi Work + Mentoring + Training provided
raw_description:
Flexi Work Arrangements, Essential Financial Knowledge, Attractive Remuneration

We believe in providing a holistic approach in service to our clients, ensuring their needs are met and satisfied in our care. And we believe that it starts with us!


Join our team as a Financial Advisor and enjoy a flexible work setting, comprehensive training, support structure, together with market leading remuneration. This role is ideal for both fresh graduates and those seeking a mid-career switch. Connect with us and embark on a rewarding career in financial advisory


A Day in The Life of a Financial Advisor:

  • Client Networking: Establish and grow a robust client base through effective networking and prospecting strategies.
  • Financial Strategy Development: Analyze clients' financial situations and goals to create tailored financial strategies.
  • Insurance and Financial Advisory: Provide comprehensive advisory on insurance and financial planning, covering areas such as retirement, education, and investments, supported by our extensive product suite.
  • Financial Analysis: Presenting detailed financial summaries, investment reports, and income projections for clients.
  • Client Relationship Management: Maintain and strengthen client relationships through regular reviews and adjustments to financial plans based on life changes.

Benefits & Rewards:

  • 24-months Trainee Program: Provides monthly allowance + commission, supporting you in the first 24 months of your career.
  • Extensive Training and Support Structure: Regular workshops, training & sharing sessions within the agency and individual mentorship ensuring support along the way.
  • Flexi-Hybrid Work Setting: Enjoy a hybrid work setting with flexible work arrangements.
  • Attractive Rewards: We believe in 'work hard, play hard', with incentives for top performers & fun team-bonding events.
  • Market-leading Remuneration Structure: Market-leading remuneration package along with an attractive incentive system, including commissions, performance incentives, and up to 3 incentive trips a year.

Qualifications:

  • Singaporean / Singapore PR preferred
  • Minimum 21 years old with Polytechnic Diploma, GCE 'A' level Certification or Bachelor Degree in any field, as per MAS requirements.
  • Highly motivated with strong interpersonal skills

raw_title: Operations Executive (Air Freight Export and Import)
title: Operations Executive (Air Freight Export and Import)
raw_description:
Operations Executive, Air Export/Import

Location: Alps Avenue

You will support import/export air freight operations by managing bookings, coordinating cargo movement, preparing documentation, and ensuring compliance with safety and regulatory standards (e.g., IATA, RACAR). You'll be expected to liaise with customers and overseas partners to meet service excellence standards.

Principal Duties and Responsibilities

• Arrange shipment collection/traffic co-ordination

• Consolidation planning and trouble shooting

• Ensure that export/import job files are checked in system and supplier invoices

are registered as passed promptly. Responsible to complete the job process.

• Ensure that pre-alerts are sent in a timely manner to customers

• Execute export documentation, customs formalities, invoicing, cargo canvassing and bookings.

• Cargo Reservation. Place booking with airlines and/or co-loaders.

• Responding to overseas offices/partners e-mail

• To assist in ensuring safety and security compliance by stopping any suspicious cargo for further process and report to department supervisor/manager immediately. All Dangerous Goods to be handled in accordance with IATA Rules & Regulations.

• To prepare invoices with accurate charges to customers on a timely manner

• Adhere to the security procedures as per Regulated Air Cargo Agent Security Programme (RACAR)

• Any other duties assigned by the superiors. Including carry out duty after normal working hours, public holidays, on site job.

Requirements:

  • Minimum “O” Levels or equivalent

  • Strong experience in Air Export operation and document with basic air import clearance knowledge.

  • Minimum 3 years in air freight operations handling export/import activities.

  • Fresh graduates are welcome to apply

  • Effective Interpersonal skills

  • Experience in Air Freight operations and documentation.

  • Willing to work on PH and weekends due to operational exigencies.

  • Able to work under tight timeline.

  • Possess high integrity and strong commitment.

  • Good knowledge of documentation of import/export procedures with interest in continuous learning.

  • A good team player and yet able to work independently.

  • At least 1–3 years of experience in air freight operations (export/import)

  • Good knowledge of customs and air cargo procedures

  • Willingness to work on weekends or PHs if required

If you’re keen to be part of a trusted global brand in shipping and logistics, apply now!
Please indicate which position you are applying for (Sea Freight or Air Freight), and include your last drawn salary and expected salary in your application.

📍 Please note: Air Freight role is based at 70 Alps Avenue;

title: Process Engineer
raw_description:
Job Summary

Drive operational excellence by leading process optimization initiatives, analyzing data to improve efficiency, and managing projects that enhance petrochemical or refining processes. Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure compliance and deliver measurable improvements.

Responsibilities

  • Identify and implement process improvements to increase efficiency, reduce costs, and maintain compliance with safety and environmental standards
  • Analyze process data to detect trends, troubleshoot operational issues, and develop continuous improvement strategies
  • Lead or contribute to project initiatives focused on upgrading or expanding existing processes, ensuring projects meet timelines and budget targets
  • Collaborate with operations, maintenance, and safety teams to resolve process-related challenges and achieve operational excellence
  • Ensure all processes comply with local and international safety, environmental, and quality regulations and standards
  • Maintain accurate and current documentation of processes, procedures, and engineering calculations to support operational integrity
  • Provide training and guidance to operators to improve process understanding and promote adherence to best practices

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Chemical Engineering or a related field
  • 3-5 years of practical experience in process engineering within the petrochemical or refining industry
  • Proficiency in process simulation tools such as Aspen Plus and Excel-based calculations
  • Familiarity with safety and environmental regulations specific to the petrochemical sector
  • Demonstrated problem-solving and analytical skills applied to process engineering challenges
  • Effective communication skills to facilitate teamwork and cross-functional collaboration
  • Project management experience
  • SCEM and/or Water Efficiency Manager certification

title: Business Analyst ( Junior )
raw_description:
About Us

Innovate. Create. Elevate.

At Vertis, we believe innovation starts with people. We design and deliver exceptional digital solutions that elevate our clients' brands and the experiences they create for their customers. Through technology-driven creativity, we enable meaningful business transformation. Our team is made up of capable, confident, and motivated individuals who collaborate, challenge ideas, and grow together.

About the job

We are looking for a passionate and detail-oriented Junior Business Analyst to join our dynamic team. In this role, you will assist in gathering and analyzing business requirements, supporting documentation efforts, and facilitating communication between business stakeholders and technical teams. This is a fantastic opportunity for someone eager to develop their skills in business analysis, digital transformation, and agile methodologies.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in gathering, documenting, and analyzing business and system requirements.
  • Support requirements discussions and workshops with clients and internal teams.
  • Collaborate with developers, testers, and project managers to ensure the solution meets business needs.
  • Help translate business requirements into clear, concise functional specifications and user stories.
  • Participate in system testing, including preparation of test cases and test execution.
  • Assist in the preparation of user manuals, training materials, and process documentation.
  • Contribute to post-implementation reviews and user feedback sessions.
  • Support project teams in the implementation of Agile or SDLC processes.
  • Maintain and update documentation in tools such as JIRA and Confluence.
  • Participate in research, analysis, and proposal writing for business solutions.

Requirements and Qualification

  • 1–3 years of experience in a Business Analyst or similar role. Fresh graduates are encouraged to apply.
  • A Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field.
  • Basic understanding of the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) and Agile methodology.
  • Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving abilities.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
  • Familiarity with JIRA, Confluence, or similar tools is a plus.
  • Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident (SPR).

Security Requirement (Mandatory)

This role involves work on Singapore Government projects. Candidates must have the legal right to work in Singapore and must be eligible for the required security clearance. Appointment to this role is subject to successful completion of all security vetting and clearance requirements.

Benefits

  • Competitive pay
  • Medical benefits
  • International and multicultural work environment
  • Continuous training and development opportunities
  • Birthday leave to celebrate your special day
  • Career growth in a dynamic, fast-growing company

Our Hiring Process
After submitting your application, our Talent Acquisition team will review your profile. If your skills and experience align with the role, we'll get in touch to discuss the next steps. Please be cautious of recruitment fraud, only engage with emails from our official that end with @vertis.digital domain.

Vertis is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We believe that diversity of thought, background, and experience drives innovation and success. That's why were committed to building and nurturing a team that reflects a wide range of perspectives, abilities, identities, and experiences. At Vertis, every voice matters, and together, we achieve more. We appreciate all applications; however, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Thank you for your interest.

The use of artificial intelligence tools, including language models such as ChatGPT, during the interview process is strictly prohibited and will result in the disqualification of your application.

Singapore Citizens are welcome to apply. This position is supported by IMDAs Company-Led Training Programme.

title: construction supervisor
raw_description:
A Construction Supervisor

oversees daily on-site operations, managing crews, subcontractors, and vendors to ensure projects are completed on time, within budget, and in compliance with safety regulations and building codes

. They inspect work quality, schedule tasks, and act as the key liaison between project managers and field staff.

Key Responsibilities

  • Site Management: Direct daily operations, including material deliveries and equipment usage.
  • Safety Compliance: Enforce strict adherence to safety regulations (e.g., OSHA) and conduct regular site inspections.
  • Project Scheduling: Plan and coordinate construction phases to meet project deadlines.
  • Quality Control: Inspect work-in-progress to ensure it meets technical specifications and building codes.
  • Coordination & Communication: Liaise between subcontractors, engineers, architects, and project managers.
  • Resource Management: Supervise, train, and assign tasks to workers, as well as manage on-site material logistics. 

Required Skills and Qualifications

  • Experience: Proven experience as a construction supervisor, foreman, or similar role.
  • Knowledge: Deep understanding of construction procedures, materials, and project management principles.
  • Technical Skills: Ability to read and interpret construction blueprints, drawings, and plans.
  • Leadership: Strong team management, decision-making, and communication skills.
  • Education: High school diploma required; associate or bachelor’s degree in construction management is often preferred. 

Typical Working Conditions

  • Environment: Works on construction sites, often in various weather conditions.
  • Requirements: Must wear personal protective equipment (PPE) and may travel to multiple sites.

title: AI Research & Development Intern
raw_description:
About Cynapse

Cynapse is a leading AI software company specializing in enterprise-grade Video Intelligence Solutions Powered by Generative AI, tailored to meet the unique challenges of various industries. Our vertical-specific solutions empower organizations to enhance safety, operational efficiency, and security in complex environments such as roads, seaports, airports, and cities. By combining advanced Vision AI with Generative AI, we continually push the boundaries of video analytics, delivering insights and automation that transform operations.

Led by a global team with a proven track record of scaling startups into market leaders, we foster innovation, collaboration, and diverse perspectives. Headquartered from US, Cynapse serves clients worldwide, redefining what's possible with video intelligence.

Job Description

We are seeking a talented and highly motivated AI Research & Development Intern to join our AI Product group that delivers innovative Computer Vision solutions to global customers.

You will conform to high standards and work in a cutting-edge, dynamic, and professionally challenging environment.

As part of your role you will:

  • Implement practical solutions using Large Language Models (LLMs), foundational Computer Vision (CV) models, and agentic AI to address real-world challenges.
  • Conduct rigorous experiments to validate model performance, including data preparation, evaluation, and benchmarking, ensuring reliability and accuracy.
  • Develop prototypes and proof-of-concept systems to test innovative ideas and demonstrate their feasibility and value.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree (completed or in progress) in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, or a related technical field.
  • Familiar with machine learning and deep learning concepts, with hands-on project experience or strong interest in real-world applications.
  • Proficient in at least one programming language, and familiar with database systems, Linux environments, Docker, and Git version control tools.
  • Excellent analytical, problem-solving, logical reasoning, communication, and teamwork skills.

Duration:

  • Available for at least a 6-month internship.
  • Able to work at least 4 days a week (Preferably full-time / 5 days a week).
  • We are flexible with start and end dates and allow flexibility to accommodate exams, leave etc.

Why join cynapse?

  • Hands-on experience working on cutting-edge AI projects.
  • Mentorship from experienced engineers and researchers.
  • Opportunity to build impactful solutions and contribute to meaningful projects.
  • Exciting work environment with flexible hours

Note: Due to the nature of the role, the position is considered for candidates that are only based in Singapore or have experience studying/working in Singapore

title: Full Stack Developer ( Junior )
raw_description:
About Us

Innovate. Create. Elevate.

At Vertis, we believe innovation starts with people. We design and deliver exceptional digital solutions that elevate our clients' brands and the experiences they create for their customers. Through technology-driven creativity, we enable meaningful business transformation. Our team is made up of capable, confident, and motivated individuals who collaborate, challenge ideas, and grow together.

About the job

We are looking for a passionate and motivated Junior Full Stack Developer to join our growing technology team. You will assist in building modern web applications using backend technologies such as Node.js and/or .NET, and frontend frameworks like React, Angular, or Vue.js. This is an excellent opportunity to grow your skills in full-stack development and cloud technologies in a collaborative, agile environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support the development and maintenance of backend services using Node.js and/or .NET.
  • Assist in building interactive and responsive front-end interfaces using modern JavaScript frameworks (React, Angular, or Vue.js).
  • Help integrate backend services with the frontend using RESTful APIs.
  • Participate in deploying and maintaining applications in cloud platforms like AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud Platform.
  • Collaborate with senior developers, designers, and other stakeholders to define and implement new features.
  • Contribute to code reviews and documentation.
  • Learn and apply industry best practices, coding standards, and agile methodologies.
  • Troubleshoot and resolve bugs or issues in applications.

Requirement and Qualification

  • 1-3 years of experience in full-stack or web development. Fresh graduates are encouraged to apply.
  • Basic understanding of Node.js and/or .NET development.
  • Exposure to one or more modern frontend frameworks (React, Angular, Vue.js, or Svelte).
  • Familiarity with REST APIs and client-server integration.
  • Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) is a plus.
  • Experience with version control systems like Git.
  • Understanding of relational or NoSQL databases (e.g., MySQL, PostgreSQL, MongoDB).
  • Strong eagerness to learn, adapt, and take ownership of tasks.
  • Good communication and teamwork skills.
  • A bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field is preferred.
  • Singapore Citizen or Permanent Resident only.

Security Requirement (Mandatory)

This role involves work on Singapore Government projects. Candidates must have the legal right to work in Singapore and must be eligible for the required security clearance. Appointment to this role is subject to successful completion of all security vetting and clearance requirements.

Benefits

  • ﻿﻿Competitive pay
  • Medical benefits
  • International and multicultural work environment
  • Continuous training and development opportunities
  • Birthday leave to celebrate your special day
  • Career growth in a dynamic, fast-growing company

Our Hiring Process

After submitting your application, our Talent Acquisition team will review your profile. If your skills and experience align with the role, we'll get in touch to discuss the next steps. Please be cautious of recruitment fraud, only engage with emails from our official team that end with @vertis.digital domain.

Vertis is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We believe that diversity of thought, background, and experience drives innovation and success. That's why we are committed to building and nurturing a team that reflects a wide range of perspectives, abilities, identities, and experiences. At Vertis, every voice matters, and together, we achieve more. We appreciate all applications; however, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Thank you for your interest.

The use of artificial intelligence tools, including language models such as ChatGPT, during the interview process is strictly prohibited and will result in the disqualification of your application.

Singapore Citizens are welcome to apply. This position is supported by IMDA's Company-Led Training Programme.

title: FULL TIME ASSISTANT ART TEACHER
raw_description:
FULL TIME ASSISTANT ART TEACHER @ LyttleSpace (Bedok South)
FULL-TIME POSITION STARTING IN FEB

(5 days work week, must work on 1 weekends)
$2500-$3000 depending on experience

Do you enjoy nurturing young minds and supporting their creative growth? Are you someone who loves being hands-on — contributing to lesson planning, creative prototyping, and working in a thoughtful, dynamic environment where ideas come to life? If you’re passionate about guiding children through their artistic journeys, LyttleSpace would love to meet you.

At LyttleSpace, we celebrate the individuality of every child and believe in building a nurturing community that supports creative expression. As we enter an exciting phase of growth, we’re expanding and looking for enthusiastic and committed individuals to join our team. If the following qualities resonate with you, we’d be delighted to invite you on this meaningful and fulfilling journey with us.

QUALITIES WE VALUE:

  • Child-Led Approach— Our ideal candidate understands the importance of genuine care when working with young children. Respect, patience, and a positive attitude are not just desirable, but essential qualities for fostering a nurturing environment. Embracing our child-led teaching philosophy to inspire creativity and imagination.
  • Growth Mindset/Adapable to Change— We appreciate individuals who are open to learning and growing. A willingness to embrace new teaching methodologies, along with a growth mindset and the ability to accept constructive feedback, is vital in our collaborative space.
  • Strong Communication Skills — Clear communication is key. Proficiency in English, both written and spoken, enables effective interaction with both our young artists and their families.
  • A Collaborative, Hands On Environment — Experience the joy of working in a warm and collaborative atmosphere where creativity thrives. There is alot of hands-on involvement with prototype making, fostering a dynamic and interactive learning environment
  • Self Artistic Exploration — Explore your own artistic expressions while encouraging children to explore theirs. We believe that an essential aspect of being a good teacher is not only guiding children on their creative journey but also embarking on an art practice of your own. It will be a source of inspiration and understanding of your very own creative process.

POSITION OVERVIEW:

As an Assistant Teacher, you will play a pivotal role in the growth and management of LyttleSpace Pte. Ltd. Your responsibilities will encompass various aspects, including teaching, preparatory work, social media content management, and administrative tasks.

Full time assistant teacher will be supported and trained to teach and lead a class. Your role goes beyond instruction. You will engage in the learning and development of the students, encouraging them to be creative and imaginative. We adopt a child led approach and practice respectful teaching.

Daily tasks involves lesson planning, preparation and facilitation of classes. There will be content creation where strong graphic and illustration skills comes into play. Your hands-on involvement in designing lesson plans and engaging in prototype creation, will contribute to our dynamic learning environment. Research, ideation and innovation process is key at LyttleSpace.

For social media management, you will help assist, create and manage captivating social media content to showcase our class processes. This includes preparing stories, simple video editing, photo editing and organising materials.

Full time assistant teacher will assist in admin duties such as lesson documentation. We hope that all teachers here at LyttleSpace will make a meaningful impact in the creative heart work we do (:

As an Assistant Teacher grows into a Lead Teacher role, the pay will be adjusted to reflect the expanded responsibilities and experience.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Genuine love working with young children is a must.
- Adaptable to change
- Respectful
- Patient with children
- Positive attitude
- Willingness to learn
- Have a growth mindset and open to feedback.
- Good command of English
- Good social skills
- Organised

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS:
While prior experience in early childhood education and art classes is advantageous, it is not a strict requirement. We encourage applications from a diverse pool of candidates, including recent graduates with a Diploma or Degree in Fine Arts, Design, Art Education, or related disciplines.

If you are excited about embarking on this merry and meaning making work with us, join the LyttleSpace family and help us shape a world where every child's creativity is celebrated and nurtured. We invite you to submit your application to lyttlespace@gmail.com along with you CV and cover letter in the below format:

Email subject: Application for the position of Assistant Teacher at LyttleSpace

Please be informed that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

OUR LOCATION:
Blk 158 Bedok South Ave 3 #01-589, Singapore 460158

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: $2,500.00 - $3,000.00 per month

Work Location: In person

title: PART TIME ASSISTANT ART TEACHER
raw_description:
ART TEACHING ASSISTANT @ LyttleSpace (Bedok South)
PART TIME POSITION STARTING FEB/MARCH
(2-3 days work week. At least 1 weekday and 1 weekend)
(Must be able to commit at least 6 months.)
Weekday hours: Tuesday-Friday 2-6.30pm
Weekend hours: Sat-Sun 9am-6.30pm

Looking to hire a part time merry maker to join our cosy team!

At LyttleSpace, every child is celebrated as a unique individual, and we create a supportive community to elevate their creative expressions. As an assistant teacher, you will dive into the fun of class preparations, guiding little ones in their artistic adventures, and fostering a community of creative makers. If you love hands-on involvement, to be a part of lesson planning and creative prototype making and be in a dynamic environment full of magic making, this is the place for you. Working with kids is exhausting but can be very rewarding! Part time position with fixed hours. Being able to commit to the hours is important to keep the sense of familiarity for the kids too.

POSITION OVERVIEW:
Assistant teacher will help support in preparation and facilitation of classes. You will engage in the learning and development of the students, encouraging them to be creative and imaginative. We adopt a child led approach and practice respectful teaching.

There may be extra sessions during school holidays and birthday parties. You will help to plan, prepare and assist. You will also assist in setting up and clean up before and after classes.

QUALITIES WE VALUE:

  • Child-Led Approach— Our ideal candidate understands the importance of genuine care when working with young children. Respect, patience, and a positive attitude are not just desirable, but essential qualities for fostering a nurturing environment. Embracing our child-led teaching philosophy to inspire creativity and imagination.
  • Growth Mindset — We appreciate individuals who are open to learning and growing. A willingness to embrace new teaching methodologies, along with a growth mindset and the ability to accept constructive feedback, is vital in our collaborative space.
  • Strong Communication Skills — Clear communication is key. Proficiency in English, both written and spoken, enables effective interaction with both our young artists and their families.
  • A Collaborative, Hands On Environment — Experience the joy of working in a warm and collaborative atmosphere where creativity thrives. There is alot of hands-on involvement with prototype making, fostering a dynamic and interactive learning environment
  • Self Artistic Exploration — Explore your own artistic expressions while encouraging children to explore theirs. We believe that an essential aspect of being a good teacher is not only guiding children on their creative journey but also embarking on an art practice of your own. It will be a source of inspiration and understanding of your very own creative process.

REQUIREMENTS:
- Genuine love working with young children is a must.
- Adaptable to change in a quick working environment.
- Respectful
- Patient with children
- Positive attitude
- Willingness to learn
- Have a growth mindset and open to feedback.
- Good command of English
- Good social skills
- Organised
-Able to commit at least 6 months

QUALIFICATIONS:
Current students or fresh grads with Diploma in Fine Arts/Design/Art Education, pursuing degree or Pre-U/A level/College art stream or related discipline. Experience in early childhood and conducting art classes preferred but not a must.

SALARY:
$11 per hour
1 month probation
Salary will be adjusted on a yearly basis, or based on performance.

ADDRESS:
Blk 158 Bedok South Ave 3
Singapore 460158

Interested candidates who can commit to the hours above.
Kindly email your portfolio and resume to lyttlespace@gmail.com with the subject title
APPLYING FOR PART TIME POSITION - ART TEACHING ASSISTANT
Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Job Types: Part-time, Student job

Pay: $11.00 per hour

Education:

  • Local Polytechnic Diploma (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

title: Quality Assurance Engineer ( Junior )
raw_description:
About Us

Innovate. Create. Elevate.

At Vertis, we believe innovation starts with people. We design and deliver exceptional digital solutions that elevate our clients' brands and the experiences they create for their customers. Through technology-driven creativity, we enable meaningful business transformation. Our team is made up of capable, confident, and motivated individuals who collaborate, challenge ideas, and grow together.

About the Job

Join our dynamic team and play a crucial role in ensuring software quality. As a Junior QA Engineer, you will be introduced to the fundamentals of manual and automated testing, working under the guidance of experienced professionals to ensure product quality and performance.

Key Responsibilities

  • Learn and assist in the development, implementation, and maintenance of test scripts to ensure test coverage.
  • Collaborate with development and product teams to understand requirements and assist in designing test strategies.
  • Assist in identifying, documenting, and tracking defects, working closely with developers for resolution.
  • Perform basic functional and regression testing, with opportunities to learn automation tools.
  • Participate in continuous improvement of the testing process and framework.
  • Receive mentorship in conducting code reviews and understanding high-quality code standards.
  • Analyze test results with guidance and contribute to reports on product quality.
  • Engage in learning opportunities related to industry trends and advancements in testing tools and methodologies.
  • Assist in ensuring all deliverables meet quality standards and project timelines.

Requirements and Qualifications

  • Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
  • Basic understanding of software testing concepts and methodologies.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Excellent attention to detail and ability to follow instructions.
  • Good communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Ability to work effectively in a team environment.
  • Willingness to learn and grow in the field of software quality assurance.

Security Requirement (Mandatory)

This role involves work on Singapore Government projects. Candidates must have the legal right to work in Singapore and must be eligible for the required security clearance. Appointment to this role is subject to successful completion of all security vetting and clearance requirements.

Benefits

  • Best in class compensation
  • Medical Benefits
  • International and multicultural environment
  • Training & Development
  • Career opportunities in a successful, fast-growing company

Our Hiring Process

After submitting your application, our Talent Acquisition team will review your profile. If your skills and experience align with the role, we'll get in touch to discuss the next steps. Please be cautious of recruitment fraud; only engage with emails from our official team that end with @vertis.digital domain.

Vertis is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We believe that diversity of thought, background, and experience drives innovation and success. That's why we are committed to building and nurturing a team that reflects a wide range of perspectives, abilities, identities, and experiences. At Vertis, every voice matters, and together, we achieve more. We appreciate all applications; however, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Thank you for your interest.

The use of artificial intelligence tools, including language models such as ChatGPT, during the interview process is strictly prohibited and will result in the disqualification of your application.

Singapore Citizens are welcome to apply. This position is supported by IMDA's Company-Led Training Programme.

title: 25928660 Transaction Manager - Officer
raw_description:
At Citi, we get to connect millions of people across hundreds of cities and countries every day.  And we've been doing it for more than 200 years.  We do this through our unparalleled global network.  We provide a broad range of financial services and products to our clients – whether they be consumers, corporations, governments or institutions – to help them meet their biggest opportunities and face the world's toughest challenges.

Shape your Career with Citi
Transaction Management will be responsible for supporting multiple aspects of the lending lifecycle including but not limited to loan closing and servicing coordination, facilitating complex client onboarding, data management, and quality control.  The team will be accountable and responsible for operational oversight of the closing and servicing of all loan transactions of the business division being supported to ensure all internal and external requirements are met.

Responsibilities:

  • Contribute to the build and development team to support loan origination and lifecycle management which includes large complex syndicated and bilateral loans covering but not limited to corporate, commercial, real estate, structured lending and trade.
  • Assist with the creation of processes, standards, procedures, controls, training, and implementation of transaction management.
  • Aid in the design and implementation of technology including testing and rollout.
  • Provide training and support to transaction management staff and business constituents.
  • Partner with each of the originating and coverage business lines, credit risk, operations, legal, etc.
  • Ensure that quality of data, controls and processes performed meet internal policies and regulatory requirements/expectations.
  • Work with lines of business, transaction management, and loan operations to define and implement operational metrics and risk and control reviews to ensure all stakeholders have end to end transparency.
  • Constantly improve processes, controls and efficiencies within the group and front to back across loan lifecycle
  • Participate in projects to enhance performance and efficiency.
  • Take part in loan remediation and loan system testing / migrations.
  • Actively manage and maintain a deal closing pipeline of loan transactions.
  • Coordinate and close loan transactions/lifecycle events, capture data, store documentation, troubleshoot issues/roadblocks, perform quality control
  • Appropriately assess risk when business decisions are made, demonstrating particular consideration for the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients and assets, by driving compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behavior, conduct and business practices, and escalating, managing and reporting control issues with transparency.

Qualifications:

  • Minimum 1-2 years of experience
  • Individual must have excellent communication, organizational and interpersonal skills.
  • Individual must work well under tight deadlines, manage priorities, and complete assigned tasks with minimal management follow up. 
  • Candidate has quantitative skills with a high proficiency with Excel and other Microsoft based programs.
  • Tableau or visualization tool familiarity
  • Experience with Loan IQ, transaction capture tools, etc.
  • Keen attention to detail
  • Developed analytical and problem-solving abilities
  • Client relationship and solution skills

Additional non-core qualifications which will be considered are:

  • Experience within a large global team supporting wholesale lending at large financial Institution
  • Knowledge of wholesale loan (syndicated / bilateral) product and business, including an understanding of legal loan documentation, loan / funding mechanics, deal structure, funds flows, etc.
  • Experience working with clients under time constraints to facilitate a transaction or process.
  • Proven ability to collaborate with team members, Senior management, and other business constituents
  • Critical decision-making capabilities including the ability to identify, escalate, and propose solutions to problems
  • Ability to manage multiple concurrent deadlines through the practice of strong organizational and time management skills with experience in continuously following-up with various stakeholders
  • Understanding and awareness of appropriate corporate and regulatory policies in relation to Lending
  • Deep understanding of operational processes supporting Wholesale lending
  • Focus on achieving/exceeding key operating standards within a KPI/metrics driven culture
  • Ability to work in an environment where priorities are frequently changing

Education:

  • Bachelor's/University degree or equivalent experience

Take the next step in your career, apply for this role at Citi today

https://jobs.citi.com
