title: Associate Engineer (Fresh Grad)
raw_description:
Rockwell Automation recruits Engineering Graduates (Freshers or with less than 12 months of experience) with the title of "Engineer in Trainee (EIT)". In this role you will undergo structured 1-year training program which includes classroom trainings as well as job rotations in multiple functions, as part of this program you are entitled to choose your career path by choosing one of the functions you have undergone in the job rotations.

You will initially report to the Regional Training Business Manager, AP and have a hybrid schedule working in Singapore, and after completion of training program, you will report to your functional manager depending on your career path selection in the rotation program.

Your Responsibilities:

  • Actively engage and demonstrate learning attitude in the first 1-year training program

  • Leverage trainings and job rotation opportunities, by developing and demonstrating your capabilities in the rotation program

  • After the 1-year training program, you will choose any of the following functions as your career path & gets placed in that function

    • Automation Project Engineering

    • Automation Field Services Engineering

    • Cyber & Network Project Engineer

    • Manufacturing Operations Management/Information Solutions Engineer

    • Technical Proposal Engineer

  • After choosing the specific function, your roles & responsibilities may vary based on the function you chose as your career path, some of those major responsibilities may include the following…

    • Support manufacturing industries in their innovate, Design, Operate & Maintain lifecycle phases.

    • Visit to manufacturing industries to delivery projects or services

    • Develop & Delivery Digital Transformation solution to manufacturing industries

    • Cybersecurity & Network solutions to industries for secure & sustainable operations

    • Automation products & software project programming and commissioning

    • Automation products & software services including troubleshooting and maintenance

    • Automation / MES Project Design & Development

The Essentials - You Will Have:

  • Bachelor Engineering Degree in any of the following functions like Electrical / Electronics / Instrumentation / Electronics & Communication.

  • Fresh graduate / Less than 12 months experience in the field

  • Adequate knowledge on Core Electrical, Electronics, Computer Science and Programming Languages

  • The ability to travel 75% of the time.

The Preferred - You Might Also Have:

  • Analytical mindset towards programming & developing automation solutions

  • Certifications in core subjects, industry internship, innovative projects

  • Experimental mindset, creativity and deliver innovative solutions

What We Offer:

Our benefits package includes …

  • 16 weeks classroom training in Singapore in the first year of employment

  • Comprehensive mindfulness programs with a premium membership to Calm

  • Volunteer Paid Time off available after 6 months of employment for eligible employees.

  • Company volunteer and donation matching program – Your volunteer hours or personal cash donations to an eligible charity can be matched with a charitable donation.

  • Employee Assistance Program

  • Personalized wellbeing programs through our OnTrack program

  • On-demand digital course library for professional development

... and other local benefits!

At Rockwell Automation we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive and authentic workplace, so if you're excited about this role but your experience doesn't align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right person for this or other roles.

#LI-Hybrid #LI-JT1

title: R&D Software Engineer (Java/Python)
raw_description:
Please submit your application via Thermo Fisher’s job portal. https://jobs.thermofisher.com/global/en/job/R-01336006/Software-Engineer-II

Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc. is the world leader in serving science, with annual revenue exceeding $40 billion and extensive investment in R&D. Our Mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. The customers we serve fall within pharmaceutical and biotech companies, hospitals and clinical diagnostic labs, universities, research institutions and government agencies. Our innovations drive scientific breakthroughs, from pioneering research to routine testing and real-world applications.

How will you make an impact?

You will be a cornerstone in developing and ensuring reliable and innovative applications that ultimately drive excellence in our product offerings. You will collaborate with and learn from a dedicated team of software developers, revolutionizing healthcare through low-cost and high throughput diagnostic systems.

What will you do?

  • Design, prototype, and deploy innovative applications — including chatbots, copilots, and autonomous agents — optimized for safety, performance, and cost-efficiency.
  • Integrate tools via Model Context Protocol (MCP) and other agent frameworks.
  • Design and implement robust, multi-turn conversational agents with escalation paths, safety guardrails, and seamless integration into web/mobile/internal platforms.
  • Using AI to quickly conduct feasibility study and POCs on new architecture designs.
  • Involved in all phases of software development including design, implementation, testing, integration, and maintenance of the software.
  • Development and support of GUI, analysis, communications, and instrument control software
  • Integration of algorithms & mathematical functions for identification and quantification of data collected by instruments
  • Understand the business needs, analyze the workflows, and carry out integrations across different sub-components.

How will you get here?

Education

  • Bachelors Degree in Computer Science or Bioengineering (with software focus) or equivalent

Experience and skills Required

  • 2+ year experience in software development experience in the software industry preferably in AI application development and evaluation experience, fresh postgraduates with relevant research background are welcome
  • Hands-on experience with major LLMs/APIs or frameworks (Open AI, LangChain or Anthropic, Hugging Face etc)
  • Strong knowledge in using AI tools when performing development tasks.
  • Knowledge of latest software development methodologies
  • Experience in source control (i.e., GitHub) and CI/CD pipeline.
  • Proven background in core Java or Python.
  • Good understanding of data structures, design patterns, object-oriented design, and algorithms used in programming.
  • Excellent teamwork and collaboration skills. Ability to manage high priority tasks and adapt to changes in priorities. Effectively communicates and illustrates ideas; contributes critically and positively to a team.

Preferred experience and skills

  • Previous experience of developing web applications using front-end technologies like Angular, React, VueJS and Electron is a plus.
  • Familiar with RAG frameworks with vector search (eg. FAISS/pgvector/Pinecone), hybrid search, rerankers, citation/grounding.
  • Experience with MLOps tools (MLflow, Kubeflow, Docker, CI/CD) and cloud services
  • Hands-on experience of building agent or chatbot
  • Evaluation: RAGAS/G-Eval or similar; offline/online metrics and A/B frameworks.
  • Data systems: SQL/NoSQL, message queues, object storage; schema design for documents & metadata.
  • Understanding AI safety principles, hallucination mitigation, and factuality evaluation techniques.
  • Experience in using AWS Sagemaker, AWS Bedrock and AWS Step Functions to streamline the AI agents into production
  • Experience in biotechnology industry is a plus

raw_description:

https://jobs.thermofisher.com/global/en/job/R-01336522/Engineer-II-Software

Do you have a passion for innovative ideas and groundbreaking discoveries? With over $1 billion invested annually in R&D, at Thermo Fisher Scientific you’ll help solve some of the world’s toughest challenges, from giving cancer patients hope, ensuring safe drinking water and helping law enforcement tackle cases through forensics. We empower our teams to put science into meaningful action and give our R&D colleagues the autonomy, resources and tools they need to take science a step beyond.

Location/Division Specific Information

Marsiling, SG / Genetic Sciences, qPCR/dPCR instrument

How you will make an impact

You will be part of an innovative software team that develops instrument software that control an instrument and allows scientists to interact with instrument features intuitively. You will work closely with product managers and engineering teams around the world to develop the instrument. You will work on sophisticated solutions using full stack technology and reusable UI components.

What you will do

  • Design and implement features based on requirements and specifications.
  • Well verse in object-oriented programming and functional code design.
  • Develop a novel UX, implementing the design provided by an internal team

  • Good understanding of data structures, design patterns, object-oriented design, and algorithms used in programming.
  • Excellent teamwork and collaboration skills. Ability to manage high priority tasks and adapt to changes in priorities. Effectively communicates and illustrates ideas; contributes critically and positively to a team.

title: QC Reviewer - BioAnalytical
raw_description:
Job Level & compensation offered may vary depending on candidates’ experiences and competencies

Responsibilities:

  • Assists co-workers and internal customers with interpretation of regulatory requirements (e.g., GLP/GCP), SOP requirements, and other guidance documents, as applicable.

  • Reviews data to ensure quality standards are met and to verify compliance with applicable SOP and regulatory requirements.

  • Maintains working knowledge of client SOPs and requirements as applicable.  Ensures that data reviewed meets applicable client SOPs and requirements.

  • Performs regularly scheduled audits of departmental systems and supporting data, as applicable, to ensure proper documentation and maintenance (e.g. training files, client SOP reading lists, logbooks, etc.).  

  • Collect error tracking data for items reviewed and reports trends as feedback to the department.

  • Monitors citation trends and performs follow-up actions with respondents, management, or others, if needed, to prevent recurring citations, problems, or errors.

  • Promotes quality and regulatory compliance within the department by working with operations and scientific staff to resolve service/quality issues, serving on a committee, assisting with the development of training programs related to documentation and quality, or other related involvement.

  • Notifies management of observed quality/compliance issues and service failures.

  • Participates in quality improvement opportunities and harmonization efforts to promote best practices. 

  • Participates in project debrief meetings as needed.

  • Facilitates changes to SOPs, policies, training materials, and other documents as applicable. 

  • Complete self-development opportunities.

  • Performs other related duties as assigned.

Requirements:

  • Bachelor of Science/Arts (BS/BA) degree.

  • Ability to efficiently use basic software (e.g., Word, Excel, PowerPoint).

  • Preferably with good knowledge of ligand binding assay and GxP experience.

  • Experience may be substituted for education.  For example, a two-year Associate degree plus 2 years applicable experience may be substituted for a BS/BA degree

title: Business Development Executive
raw_description:
We are looking for a proactive and motivated Business Development Executive to support sales activities and client engagement for our Industry business unit. This role suits someone with basic technical knowledge and strong interest in business development.

Key Responsibilities

Sales Support & Lead Generation


    •    Identify new leads, develop prospect lists, and conduct outreach to potential clients.
    •    Support the preparation of quotations, proposals, pre-qualifications, and tender submissions.
    •    Maintain and update CRM systems, sales reports, and activity logs.

Client Engagement


    •    Build positive relationships with relevant stakeholders.
    •    Coordinate client meetings, presentations, and follow-ups.
    •    Assist with resolving client issues in coordination with operations team. 

Market & Competitor Research


    •    Monitor opportunities of upcoming projects and shutdown/turnaround activities.
    •    Collect and report market intelligence, trends, and competitor activity.

Internal Coordination


    •    Liaise with operation team to ensure accurate proposals and timely service delivery.
    •    Support marketing initiatives such as digital campaigns, brochures, and events.

Requirements


    •    Diploma/Degree in Engineering, Business, or related fields.
    •    1–3 years of experience in sales, customer service, or technical support (preferably O&G or engineering).
    •    Basic understanding of inspection services is an advantage.
    •    Strong communication skills, customer-focused attitude, and willingness to learn.
    •    Fresh graduates with strong interest in O&G business development are welcome.
    •    Ability to multitask and manage deadlines.

title: Intern, Strategic Communications
raw_description:
COMPANY DESCRIPTION

The National Volunteer & Philanthropy Centre (NVPC) is the steward of the City of Good vision for Singapore, where individuals, organisations, and leaders come together to give their best for others. Through our brands, programmes, and initiatives, we facilitate partnerships with non-profits, organisations, public sector bodies, and individuals to enliven the giving ecosystem within Singapore. title: Admin Associate / Executive (Social Service)
raw_description:
Monday to Friday

8:00 AM – 5:30 PM

Location: Toa Payoh/ Elizabeth Drive

Job Responsibilities

  • Gather social and financial background information of residents and/or their families prior to the application of financial assistance schemes.
  • Update and maintain accurate records in the referral and enquiries database and information system.
  • Ensure timely submission of financial assistance applications such as Medifund, MFEC (Medical Fee Exemption Card), IDAPE (Interim Disability Assistance Programme for the Elderly), PGDAS (Pioneer Generation Disability Assistance Scheme), and others.
  • Act as the main point of contact and liaison between residents, families, and healthcare institutions to coordinate referrals and process applications for potential residents.
  • Ensure proper documentation of case files, referrals, and other relevant documents for effective case management and audit purposes.
  • Perform data entry and regularly update information to maintain accuracy of databases.
  • Handle administrative tasks including enquiries, referrals, and admissions.
  • Facilitate residents’ transfers to other facilities or healthcare institutions, as well as coordinate discharge processes.
  • Undertake any other duties or projects as assigned by the supervisor.
  • May required to be stationed in the office to handle incoming phone calls, walk-in enquiries, and other front-desk administrative duties as and when required

Requirements

  • Minimum 1 year of relevant working experience in social service, community service, healthcare, or related sectors.
  • Prior exposure to financial assistance schemes (e.g. Medifund, MFEC, IDAPE, PGDAS) will be an advantage
  • Diploma or Degree in Social Work, Social Sciences, Community Services, Healthcare Administration, or related disciplines preferred.
  • Comfortable liaising with residents, families, healthcare institutions, and external agencies.
  • Strong administrative and documentation skills, with attention to detail for case files and audit purposes.

Candidates pursuing a part-time diploma or degree in a relevant field (e.g. Social Work or Psychology) are welcome to apply


Interested candidates please WhatsApp me at 8779 6100 or send in your resume to willie.goh@swiftsearchglobal.com

We regret to inform you that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Account Executive (Client Services)
raw_description:
Are you a go-getter? BookMyShow SEA is a regional ticketing start up, and we are looking for big personalities to handle our projects, big or small! We work closely with event promoters and venue owners, and we empower them by providing them with the right tools to successfully manage their end-to-end ticketing journey. Our team is a multi-disciplinary, tight-knit, meticulous, hardworking, and talented bunch. We work closely together to ensure the success of every event, concert, theatre performance, outdoor festival, and major sporting event that we ticket for.


Do you have what it takes to:

· Step up and be the main liaison officer with our numerous promoters?

· Do what needs to be done to ensure the success of every event?

· Collaborate with business teams to better understand clients' needs and wants, and to separate what is really needed, vs. what is really wanted?


We’re looking for someone who has:

· A go-getter attitude who never turns down a challenge

· Passion for creative problem-solving, and who can think out of the box

· Effective crisis and time management skills

· Strong written and communication skills

· A passion for the Arts & Entertainment world (added advantage)


This role would be a good fit if you have:

· A Level, Diploma or Degree in any related field, or equivalent practical experience

· Will be required to present during events day (Sat, Sun or Public Holiday)

· No experience or Fresh graduate are welcome to apply

· Worked in a start-up (added advantage)

title: Partnership Executive
raw_description:
Our Client

The Talent People is representing a fast‑growing organization in Singapore that partners with leading brands to deliver high‑impact digital and social‑led marketing solutions. With a strong focus on creativity, data‑driven execution, and community‑centric storytelling, our client works across diverse sectors to help brands engage modern audiences in meaningful ways.

About The Role

As the Business Development Executive, you will be part of the partnerships function responsible for building new client pipelines, supporting commercial activations, and ensuring strong client satisfaction that drives repeat business. You will work closely with senior partnership leaders to convert opportunities into active projects and ensure smooth collaboration across internal teams.

Key Responsibilities:

Pipeline Development

  • Research and identify potential clients across multiple industries.

  • Map key decision‑makers and organizational structures.

  • Drive outbound prospecting through digital outreach, referrals, and internal databases.

  • Maintain accurate CRM records and track lead progression.

  • Support senior team members with early‑stage outreach, proposal preparation, and coordination.

Sales Activation Support

  • Co‑develop proposals and pitch materials with the partnerships team.

  • Coordinate with strategy and creator‑related teams to align with client briefs.

  • Prepare timelines, talent recommendations, and supporting documents for presentations.

  • Participate in partnership calls, document key actions, and ensure timely follow‑through.

Campaign & Client Servicing Support

  • Work with internal operations teams to ensure deliverables remain on schedule.

  • Maintain regular client touchpoints, manage minor updates, and monitor satisfaction.

  • Gather post‑campaign results and assist in developing case studies.

Internal Process & Reporting

  • Provide weekly updates on pipeline status and lead progression.

  • Track individual performance metrics through CRM dashboards.

Experience Required:

  • Strong understanding of social platforms, digital behaviour, and online trends.
  • Minimum 2 years in a client‑facing or commercial role.
  • Experience in Sales Development Representative (SDR) roles or similar business development functions.
  • Background in media, digital, or technology‑related industries is preferred.
  • Strong communication skills, close attention to detail, and comfort working in a fast‑paced environment
  • Come from a creative marketing agency is highly desirable

Education/Qualification

  • A relevant tertiary qualification in Business, Marketing, Communications, or a related field.

  • Additional training in sales, digital marketing, or CRM systems will be advantageous.

Why This Role?

This is an exciting opportunity to join a dynamic and expanding organization where you will play a key role in driving commercial growth and shaping client relationships. If you are proactive, relationship‑driven, and passionate about the digital landscape, we’d love to hear from you!

The Talent People 24C2589

title: Logistics Coordinator/Senior Logistics Coordinator
raw_description:
Summary of position:

PSA BDP, a member of the PSA Group, is a leading provider of globally integrated and port-centric supply chain, transportation, and logistics solutions. The company is headquartered in Philadelphia, PA, and employs more than 5,500 people worldwide.

We are a team that celebrates our unique diversity and close-knit community atmosphere. Our core values begin at the very top and span the broad reach of our global community.

We offer dynamic careers for those individuals looking to be a part of something bigger and provide unequivocal opportunities for growth within the organization.

PSA BDP serves over 5,000 customers, including some of the world's leading multinational companies within the chemical, retail & consumer, life sciences & pharmaceuticals, and electric vehicle & industrial verticals.

The Logistics Coordinator is responsible for the timely daily shipment order execution and management. The role involves booking and shipment coordination, liaising with various stake holders for warehousing and transportation, shipping document preparation, and regulatory custom and trade compliance.


Scope of work & Responsibilities

Export/Import

· Receive and process shipment orders triggered by Customers.

· Book freight with nominated carriers based on customer's carrier routine guide.

· Provide quotation on other services such as warehousing and transportation and documentation services.

· Liaise with the truckers to collect and truck empty containers from depot to customer premise for stuffing, and to launch the laden container to port thereafter.

· Prepare Permits and other relevant Applications/Declaration.

· Prepare Letter of Credit & final shipping documents

· Monitor shipping documents for shipment clearance, and other regulatory compliance.


Experience

· 1 to 2 years relevant working experience in Sea operations.

· Fresh “Logistics and Supply Chain Management” graduates or equivalent from Polytechnics and Universities are welcome to apply.

Qualification

Diploma or Degree holders in “Logistics and Supply Chain Management” or equivalent.

Knowledge/Skills

MS Office, especially Excel

Good understanding of IT System, such as SAP, will be an advantage.

Personal Traits

Commitment to Work Excellence

Strong interpersonal communication skill

Good Customer Service

title: Administrative Assistant
raw_description:
Summary

Supports teams using a wide range of developing professional skills such as scheduling meetings and appointments, coordinating office needs, welcoming visitors and providing general administrative support to our employees. Highly organized, personable and detail-oriented to support company operations and teams.

Responsibilities

  • Prepares meeting agendas for assigned staff, assists with meeting set-up/clean-up which includes ordering lunch and takes minutes as required
  • Schedules and organizes activities such as meetings, travel, conferences and interviews
  • Answers phones, distributes mail and processes expense reimbursement for assigned staff
  • Under minimal supervision, performs desktop publishing activities of both routine and advanced nature, for reports, correspondence and presentations
  • May support other teams, such as Marketing, with various administrative tasks
  • Assist with event setup and coordination
  • Manage office supplies, stationery, and pantry inventory
  • Oversee office equipment maintenance and coordinate office cleanliness with cleaning staff

Cultural

  • Demonstrates professionalism by consistently maintaining positive and collaborative attitude with all staff, visitor and clients
  • Performs work in a manner that demonstrates an awareness of and an appreciation for the firm’s cultural values, including a commitment to quality, innovation, learning and growth
  • Becomes skilled in fostering and maintaining a collaborative professional working relationship with the project team
  • Takes personal responsibility for fostering a green workplace through sustainable work practices

Financial

  • Understands the financial implications of overhead and chargeable time, including accountability to project plan and planned utilization rate

Revenue or Opportunity Generation

  • May assist in production of marketing presentations

Qualifications

  • High school education; college level preferred
  • Typically with 2+ years of related experience
  • Experience in MS Office Suite, including Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint and Teams
  • Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to interact with all levels of staff
  • Ability to maintain appropriate degree of confidentiality and adherence to the practice of business ethics
  • Ability to communicate in a clear, concise and professional manner both verbally and in writing
  • Ability to proactively problem solve and collaborate on innovative solutions
  • Ability to work in team environment
  • Ability to prioritize tasks to meet multiple deadlines and respond to changes at short notice
  • Ability to work on multiple projects at the same time
  • Ability to effectively meet deadlines at expected quality
  • 6-month contract, with possibility of extension
  • Open to Singaporeans and Singapore PRs only.

title: Application Developer Intern (Jun-Dec'26)
raw_description:
About ITLink Business Solutions

ITLink Business Solutions is an award-winning Singapore-based tech consultancy, renowned for its innovative approach to transforming businesses with tailored, enterprise-grade data and financial performance solutions, specifically leveraging IBM Planning Analytics and Microsoft Power BI. We empower our clients to make informed business decisions based on dependable data insights, driving tangible business outcomes. Embark on a dynamic internship with us and gain invaluable hands-on experience in the exciting journey of tech-driven business transformation.

Role Overview

As an Application Developer Intern at ITLink, you will contribute directly to the development and implementation of Business Intelligence and Corporate Performance Management solutions for our clients. This internship offers a unique opportunity to gain practical experience with leading enterprise technologies (IBM Planning Analytics and Microsoft Power BI) and contribute to real-world projects. You'll work alongside our experienced team, receive direct mentorship, and play a supporting role in delivering data-driven insights that help businesses succeed.

What You'll Learn & Do

  • Support Solution Development: Assist Solution Architects and Developers in designing, coding, and implementing components of IBM TM1 and Microsoft Power BI solutions.
  • Data Integration Assistance: Support efforts in connecting to various data sources and transforming data for use in BI dashboards and financial models.
  • Problem-Solving & Testing: Participate in identifying and troubleshooting technical issues within applications, and assist in conducting system testing to ensure solution quality and reliability.
  • Requirements Support: Help gather, document, and analyze business requirements for new features or enhancements.
  • Documentation & Training Aid: Support the creation of technical documentation and user guides, and assist in preparing training materials for end-users.
  • Project Administration: Assist Project Managers with tracking project progress, preparing reports, and other administrative tasks to ensure smooth project execution.
  • Technology Exploration: Gain exposure to our proprietary tools like Omni and TMVGate, and learn about the strategic shift towards productized services and future SaaS concepts.
  • Continuous Learning: Actively engage in learning new technical skills and concepts related to data analytics, financial planning, and specific software solutions.

What We're Looking For (Requirements)

  • Education: Currently pursuing a Bachelor's Degree in Software Engineering, Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Analytics, or a closely related quantitative discipline.
  • Analytical Aptitude: Strong analytical abilities with a keen interest in problem-solving and understanding complex data.
  • Foundational Technical Interest: Basic understanding of programming concepts or database principles (e.g., SQL) is a plus. A genuine curiosity about Business Intelligence tools, data modeling, or financial software is highly valued.
  • Eagerness to Learn: A strong willingness and enthusiasm to learn new, specialized technologies rapidly, particularly IBM Planning Analytics and Microsoft Power BI.
  • Proactive & Organized: Self-motivated, with good organizational skills and the ability to manage tasks effectively under guidance.
  • Team Player: Able to collaborate effectively within a team, taking direction and contributing positively to group efforts.

Why Intern with ITLink?

  • Specialized Hands-on Experience: Gain practical, sought-after skills in enterprise-grade technologies (IBM Planning Analytics and Microsoft Power BI) that are highly valued in the industry.
  • Direct Mentorship: Learn directly from experienced Solution Architects and our Managing Director, benefiting from their expertise and guidance on real-world projects.
  • Real-World Impact: Contribute to projects that directly help businesses make better decisions through data, offering a unique opportunity to see the impact of your work.
  • Dynamic Learning Environment: Work in a supportive and collaborative setting where your contributions are valued, and continuous learning is encouraged.
  • Pathway to Career: A successful internship can serve as a strong foundation for future career opportunities in data analytics, BI consulting, or software development.

Ready to Make an Impact?

If you are a motivated university student eager to gain practical experience, learn cutting-edge technologies, and contribute to meaningful projects, we encourage you to apply.

title: Operations and Admin Assistant
raw_description:
Overview

You will be responsible for providing daily Operations Admin support to the team while working closely with the sales and service teams.

Duties and responsibilities:

  • Responsible for the daily operations activities such as purchasing, inventory and delivery etc.
  • Responding to clients' queries and calls.
  • Process and maintain purchase order to suppliers.
  • Monitor stock status with the suppliers to ensure on-time delivery date.
  • Perform timely inventory planning and stocking to ensure order fulfilment on customers’ orders.
  • Co-ordinate with freight forwarders for incoming and outgoing shipments delivery.
  • Perform inventory control and tracking demo unit and monitor stock movement and ensure store in compliance with regulatory.
  • Support sales team in generate paperwork (invoices, DOL and update database to ERP etc.
  • Assist Product Registration (HSA & NEA), liaise and work with local authorities, compliance with SOP and renewal of relevant license/s.
  • Support internal and external audits (GDPMDS)
  • Assist in administrative work such as processing order for office stationery etc and schedule office maintenance/facility/visitors management etc
  • All other ad hoc duties as assigned by the Reporting Managers

other Information:

  • Working hours : Mon – Thurs 9am to 6pm, Fri 9am to 5.30pm
  • Salary commensurate according to relevance of work experience and qualifications.

Education and skill requirement:

  • Diploma graduate with no experience are welcome
  • GCE ‘O’/ ‘A’/Diploma/Degree (Business, Logistics & Shipping, purchasing) or any other relevant educations.
  • Min 1-2 years hands-on Operations Admin/Purchasing/Logistic experience.
  • Able to work independently and under pressure, multi-tasked.
  • Customer orientated approach, highly well organized, meticulous, accountability, team player, good in monitoring & follow up skills and has good interpersonal & communication skills.

B2B | Fun Team | Mentorship Provided

Your next chapter starts here — and it’s going to be a fun ride.
📩 Apply now and let’s explore your future together!

raw_title: Senior Security Officer - Frasers House, a Luxury Collection Hotel, Singapore
title: Senior Security Officer - Frasers House, a Luxury Collection Hotel, Singapore
raw_description:
POSITION SUMMARY

Perform hotel security duties and procedures to ensure the safety and security of guests, employees and the property as well as the property’s assets and also part of the Company Emergency Response Team (CERT) assisting Fire Emergencies.

Senior Security Officer is responsible for providing guidance and leadership to Security Officers through the course of their duties; performing duties as Security Duty Shift Supervisor when needed and assisting with training and development of newly assigned personnel. SSOs are required to carry out any and all investigations that requires CCTV viewing and reporting.
 

PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

Must possess PLRD license with relevant employment grading;
Minimum tertiary education with at least 2 years of working experience in a similar capacity, in the hotel industry;
 

CORE WORK ACTIVITIES

  • Remain vigilant during duty post deployment and hotel patrol at all times and identify fire and security risks;
  • Provide advice to staff and guests on security and safety issues;
  • Liaise with external organisations including emergency services regarding security and safety issues;
  • Log security incidents and accidents for guests and colleagues in accordance with hotel requirements; to review and report on CCTV investigations.
  • Ensure effective measure against trespassers and pilferage with no undesirables loiter in or around the hotel premises;
  • Provide assistance for events or functions as required for VIPs, High Profile guests, Government, Owners, Royals, etc;
  • Maintain daily occurrence duty logs, FCC orderly with proper handover duties;
  • Ensure that the Fire Command Centre is never left unattended;
  • Be thoroughly familiar of the property layout, design, entry and exit points in order to lead and direct guest whenever possible;
  • Attend to all alarms, medical & fire emergencies, and other similar matters;
  • Evacuate premises and contact appropriate emergency services if required;
  • In the event of fire, be thoroughly familiar with and guided by the hotel’s emergency plan and be familiar with location of fire alarms and firefighting equipment;
  • Ensure checks are conducted on fire panel, fire Hosereel, fire barriers, fire exit doors and all safety equipment to be in good working order;  
  • Comply with all the Hotel Policies, Procedures and Work place safety and health;
  • Monitor CCTV surveillance and conduct regular patrols of the hotel and promptly report any suspicious or unusual activities to the Supervisor;
  • Maintain a professional demeanour, handling incidents discreetly and confidently;
  • Perform security functions at assigned posts and areas within the hotel;
  • Ensure hotel function rooms remain secure and locked when not in use;
  • To perform any other duties assigned by Security Supervisor/Security Manager.

title: Wealth Management Trainee
raw_description:
Why Mizuho

At Mizuho Securities Singapore, our greatest asset is our people, and we are committed to promoting a vibrant and supportive environment full of opportunities. Just like our global client and customer base, our people come from diverse backgrounds and our success is supported by our creativity, teamwork, and collective abilities as individuals to exceed the dynamic needs of our customers.

We provide the stability of an international industry leader with the career trajectory of a growing business. Our strategic growth gives our people at all levels rewarding degrees of responsibility and a richer work experience than a boutique firm or an established giant could offer alone. With a focus on growing and expanding our platform across Asia, we are committed to attracting, hiring, retaining, and rewarding the best-in-class talent.


Job Description

As a trainee of the wealth management team, you will be work well with other team members and assist them to pursue the team goals in terms of client acquisition, client maintenance, client documentation, and any other supporting roles.


Responsibilities

  • Support all tasks/activities needed within the wealth management team
  • Partner with the team to achieve ultimate goal of the team
  • Help to create documents in English, Japanese, and possibly Chinese for client and for internal meetings
  • Coordinate with affiliate entities of Mizuho group globally
  • To attend client meetings with team members


Role Prerequisites

  • Minimum 2 years of experience in private banking
  • Ability to complete necessary analysis and to create documentations related to clients’ account
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office products with advanced knowledge of financial analysis required
  • Ability to foster and maintain an initial level of wealth management relationship with high level of competency
  • Able to communicate clearly with the team and to work as a team
  • Speaking/writing fluency in English and Japanese is mandatory due to liaison with Japanese clients and internal stakeholders in Tokyo head office


Company Overview

As a leading Asia-focused investment bank, we provide corporate and institutional clients with unparalleled access and expertise in Japan and Asia. We are the partner of choice to many in the region, offering a wide range of investment banking and global markets solutions for the increasingly-sophisticated and technological needs of our large client base – primarily corporations, financial institutions, pensions, asset managers, and public sector entities.

Our parent company – Mizuho Financial Group – came together in late 2002, merging three of the largest banks in Japan and is today one of the world’s largest financial institutions. With our global presence and affiliates across Asia, Americas and EMEA, we’re able to meet local market needs with a combination of unique market insights, knowledge, and capabilities.

For more information about the Mizuho Financial Group, please visit: https://www.mizuhogroup.com

For more information about Mizuho Securities, please visit: https://www.mizuhogroup.com/securities

title: General Ledger Accountant
raw_description:
【Roles & Responsibilities】

1. Being responsible for general ledger accounting and intercompany reconciliations of the Group’s overseas subsidiaries.

2. Being responsible for regularly general ledger accounts review to ensure accurate and timely bookkeeping, clearance, and compliance with supporting documents.

3. Being responsible for preparing financial statements, financial footnotes, and annual final financial statements for the Group’s overseas subsidiaries.

4. Being responsible for tax filing for the Group’s Singapore subsidiary.

5. Revising general ledger and accounting standards to promote standardization in accounting practices.

6. Driving tax sharing and financial analysis initiatives, including shared tax filing and BI financial analysis projects.

7. Being responsible for improving the financial information quality of served business units.


【Key Requirements:】

1. Bachelor’s degree or above in financial accounting or related fields education, with at least four years of experience in general ledger accounting or accounting firms. Experience with consolidated financial statements is preferred.

2. Familiarity with International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) and Singaporean financial and tax regulations.

3. Proficiency in SAP systems.

4. Strong logical skills, stress resistance, teamwork, and organizational skills.

5. Able to work overtime under high-pressure during peak seasons.

6. Strong written and verbal communication skills in English and Mandarin.

7. Strong self-drive and learning ability

8. Knowledge of Chinese Accounting Standards and relevant tax regulations is a plus.


Others:

1. Specific salary details will be discussed during the interview. Salary is negotiable for candidates with excellent work experience.

2. Kindly include the following in your resume: Availability, Current salary, and Expected salary.

title: Business Intelligent Engineer
raw_description:
Key Responsibilities:

  • Develop, maintain, and optimize Tableau dashboards and reports based on business requirements.          
  • Assist in data preparation, blending, and transformation for visualization.          
  • Work with stakeholders to gather requirements and translate them into effective visualizations.  
  • Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to dashboards, data sources, and performance.        
  • Ensure dashboards follow best practices for usability, performance, and design.          
  • Collaborate with data engineers and analysts to integrate data from various sources (SQL, Excel, APIs, etc.).     
  • Document dashboard designs, data flows, and development processes.        
  • Stay updated with the latest Tableau features and BI trends.  

Qualifications & Skills:

  • Education: Polytechnic Diploma or bachelor’s degree in computer science, Data Analytics, Business, or a related field (or equivalent experience).  

Experience:        

  • 0–2 years of experience with Tableau Desktop (certification is a plus).      
  • Basic understanding of data visualization principles and dashboard design.         
  • Familiarity with SQL for data querying and manipulation.         
  • Exposure to data modelling, ETL processes, or BI tools is a bonus.  

Technical Skills:        

  • Proficiency in Tableau (calculations, parameters, LOD expressions).        
  • Basic knowledge of data warehousing concepts.     
  • Experience with Excel or other data analysis tools.  

Soft Skills:          

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.           
  • Good communication skills to collaborate with non-technical teams.          
  • Eagerness to learn and adapt in a fast-paced environment.

title: Business Development Executive
raw_description:
The Role

As a Business Development Executive, you’ll be on the front lines of our growth. Your mission is clear: generate new business, close deals, and earn strong commissions. This role is ideal for someone who is driven, confident, and excited to work in a fast-paced startup where individual performance makes a real impact.

What You’ll Be Doing

  • Prospecting and engaging potential clients through phone calls, personalised emails, video conferencing, trade shows, and other outbound channels

  • Running discovery calls, pitching solutions, and conducting product demos

  • Managing the full sales cycle from first contact to close

  • Consistently achieving and exceeding sales targets

  • Working closely with founders, marketing, and product teams to drive growth

  • Sharing market feedback to help refine messaging, positioning, and product direction

What We’re Looking For

  • Diploma / Bachelor degree in any field

  • 1–3 years of experience in sales, business development, or customer acquisition (B2B preferred)

  • Open to candidates from non-sales backgrounds who are driven, coachable, and excited to break into sales

  • Strong communication, persuasion, and negotiation skills

  • Experience with cold calling and telemarketing is highly preferred

  • Self-starter mindset, comfortable working independently without heavy supervision

  • Naturally curious with a strong willingness to learn and improve

  • A true hunter who can consistently generate pipeline through outbound efforts

  • Comfortable selling tech, SaaS, or AI solutions (or motivated to ramp up quickly)

  • Competitive, resilient, and motivated by results and commissions

What We Offer

  • High commissions with strong earning potential 💰

  • Hybrid work arrangement

  • Direct access to leadership and involvement in decision-making

  • A fun, collaborative, no-corporate-BS culture

  • Clear opportunities for career progression as the company scales

Why Join Us?

  • Be early in a fast-growing AI company with real momentum

  • Sell a product that solves a genuine business problem

  • Work alongside ambitious teammates who celebrate wins together

  • Learn fast, grow your skills, and make a visible impact

Your resume should clearly outline:

  • Your current role and relevant work history aligned with the criteria above

  • Your sales / business development experience

  • Your attainment or performance results

title: Administration & Operations Executive - WCAN
raw_description:
Responsibilities:

  • Manage confidential student and assessment data in the student and learning management systems.
  • Prepare and coordinate all assessments, including quizzes, class tests, and exams, ensuring proper planning and documentation.
  • Liaise with faculty and module leaders to review and finalize assessment materials before administration.
  • Plan and allocate resources, including booking venues and assigning staff for assessment support.
  • Maintain accurate assessment schedules and ensure all materials are prepared for on-campus or online assessments.
  • Brief and coordinate staff to ensure smooth administration of assessments.
  • Oversee the conduct of assessments, ensuring procedures are followed and addressing any issues that arise.
  • Manage post-assessment processes, including tracking marking timelines, liaising with faculty for moderation, and updating student records.
  • Prepare and coordinate meetings with relevant boards or committees, ensuring all required documentation is complete.
  • Handle assessment-related administrative tasks, procurement, and finance matters according to timelines.
  • Support the launch of new programs by planning initial assessments and drafting operational workflows.
  • Liaise with internal and external stakeholders to establish clear processes and timelines for assessments.
  • Coordinate with outsourced partners to monitor and track operational processes.
  • Assign and deploy staff for online and on-campus assessment support.
  • Perform any other duties as assigned by the reporting officer.


Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in any field.
  • 1–2 years of relevant experience is a plus; entry-level candidates are welcome to apply.
  • Experience in examination management, academic operations, or a similar administrative environment is preferred.
  • Excellent organizational and multitasking abilities.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills to liaise effectively with various stakeholders.
  • Detail-oriented with the ability to maintain accurate records.
  • Capable of prioritizing workload and meeting deadlines.
  • Able to work under pressure during peak assessment periods.
  • Willing to work 1–2 hours beyond regular working hours on weekdays or Saturdays when requested.


We regret only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. All applications will be handled confidentially. By submitting your application, you agree to the collection, use, retention, and disclosure of your personal information to prospective employers.

raw_title: Commodity Trader, Gasoline/Distillates - based in SG
title: Commodity Trader, Gasoline/Distillates - based in SG
raw_description:
Location:

Singapore

Experience Level:

Entry-level

Role Summary:

This role will consist of physical trading and paper trading within oil markets. The ideal candidate is a clear communicator and self-starter who demonstrates great ability to work both independently and within a team setting, especially while attempting to solve challenges in a fast-paced environment.

Responsibilities:

· Conduct thorough research and analysis on assigned commodities, including gathering market data, tracking trends, and assessing supply and demand dynamics.

· Coordinate and execute buying and selling activities for assigned commodities, ensuring efficient and profitable transactions.

· Develop and expand upstream and downstream resources, while establishing reliable procurement and sales channels; maintain regular communication with clients, addressing inquiries, resolving issues, and fostering strong business relationships.

· Formulate and implement hedging strategies for commodity futures and financial derivatives, managing positions to mitigate risk and maximize returns.

· Collaborate with team members to generate comprehensive reports and insights, contributing to informed decision-making processes.

· Uphold the company's reputation through ethical and transparent dealings with customers and suppliers.

· Undertake additional tasks and projects as assigned, demonstrating flexibility and adaptability in a dynamic trading environment.

Requirements:

· Bachelor's degree or above in a relevant field.

· Strong logical thinking, language and writing abilities, excellent communication and teamwork skills.

· Demonstrated practicality, diligence, and self-motivation; capable of making quick decisions under pressure.

· Willing to learn with a proactive attitude.

· Fluent in English and Chinese to facilitate communication with stakeholders in China.

· Fresh graduates from top-tier institutions are encouraged to apply, 0-3 years of working experience preferred.

Apply Now:

If you're excited about the prospect of joining our team and contributing to our success, please send your resume to grace.wu@gentcommodity.com and indicate the position you are applying for in the email title. Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

In Chinese version：

【大宗商品交易员】

岗位职责：

l 收集、整理相关商品信息，建立和维护基础信息数据库；

l 负责相关品种的研究、分析，并撰写报告；

l 开拓上下游资源，建立完善的采购和销售渠道，负责相关品种的采购和销售工作；

l 负责制定相关商品期货及金融衍生品对冲策略和头寸管理；

l 负责上级交办的其它事项。

岗位要求：

l 全日制大学本科及以上学历，专业不限；

l 逻辑思维清晰，语言、文字表达能力强，良好的沟通及团队协作能力；

l 踏实、勤奋、有很强的我驱动力、能吃苦；

l 永久居民，公民，符合新加坡工作签申请条件者欢迎投递；

l 能够使用中英文作为工作语言；

l 三年以内工作经验，应届生欢迎投递投。

title: Trading Analyst Intern
raw_description:
Location:

Singapore

Internship Duration: 

  • 3-6 months
  • Convertible to a full-time role

Type: 

Full-time / Part-time （at least 4 days per week)

Role Summary:

This internship offers a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in commodity trading and market analysis. The intern will work closely with traders to monitor global commodity markets, analyze price movements, and support trade execution. Through data-driven insights and real-time market tracking, the intern will contribute to identifying trading opportunities and optimizing strategies. The ideal candidate is a clear communicator and self-starter, with the ability to work effectively both independently and as part of a team. Candidates should be comfortable operating in a fast-paced trading environment and demonstrate strong problem-solving skills when addressing market and operational challenges.

Responsibilities:

· Conduct thorough research and analysis on assigned commodities, including gathering market data, tracking trends, and assessing supply and demand dynamics.

· Assist traders in developing and executing hedging strategies for commodity futures and financial derivatives.

· Collaborate with team to generate comprehensive reports and insights, contributing to informed decision-making processes.

· Uphold the company's reputation through ethical and transparent dealings with customers and suppliers.

· Undertake additional tasks and projects as assigned, demonstrating flexibility and adaptability in a dynamic trading environment.

Requirements:

· Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Finance, Economics, Engineering, Mathematics, Data Science, or a related field.

· Strong logical thinking, language and writing abilities, excellent communication and teamwork skills.

· Demonstrated practicality, diligence, and self-motivation; capable of making quick decisions under pressure.

· Fluent in English and Chinese to facilitate communication with stakeholders in China.

· Interest in global commodity markets.

· Willing to learn with a proactive attitude.

Apply Now:

If you're excited about the prospect of joining our team and contributing to our success, please send your resume to grace.wu@gentcommodity.com and indicate the position you are applying for in the email title. Please note that only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

交易分析实习生

工作地点： 新加坡
实习时长： 3-6 个月，有转正机会
工作类型： 全职 / 兼职（每周至少 4 天）

岗位介绍：

本次实习提供了一个深入了解大宗商品交易和市场分析的宝贵机会。实习生将与交易员密切合作，监测全球大宗商品市场，分析价格走势。通过数据驱动的分析和实时市场跟踪，实习生将为识别交易机会和优化交易策略提供支持。

我们正在寻找具有清晰沟通能力和主动性的候选人。应具备独立工作和团队协作的能力，并能在快节奏的环境中解决问题、迎接挑战。

岗位职责：

  • 对指定的大宗商品进行深入研究和分析，包括收集市场数据、跟踪行业趋势、评估供需动态；
  • 协助交易员制定和执行大宗商品期货及金融衍生品的对冲策略；
  • 与团队协作，撰写市场分析报告，为交易决策提供数据支持；
  • 职业道德感强，维护公司在客户和供应商中的良好声誉；
  • 根据业务需求，完成其他指派的任务和项目，适应能力强。

岗位要求：

  • 全日制大学本科及以上学历在读；
  • 逻辑思维清晰，语言、文字表达能力强，良好的沟通及团队协作能力；
  • 踏实、勤奋、有很强的我驱动力、能吃苦；
  • 能够使用中英文作为工作语言；
  • 对全球大宗商品市场有浓厚兴趣；
  • 具有积极主动学习的态度。

简历可投递至：grace.wu@gentcommodity.com，麻烦备注申请岗位， 谢谢!

title: Civil, Structural and Geotechnical Design Engineer
raw_description:
The Role

The role reports directly to the team lead. He or She is responsible for supporting the Professional Engineers on the design and supervision of new erections, addition and alteration (A&A) work to existing buildings, earth retaining and stabilising systems, specialist designs for the facade, glass, demolition works etc, as well as temporary works. The role may also be required to conduct site inspections.

Responsibilities:

  • Carry out detailed analysis and design of civil, structural, and geotechnical works;
  • Prepare design calculation reports and structural drawings for authorities' submissions;
  • Coordinate with various stakeholders such as clients, consultants, and contractors for projects; and
  • Supervise and inspect construction works;

Requirements:

  • Degree in Civil/Structural Engineering from a recognized Institution;
  • Strong engineering fundamentals and analytical skills in geotechnical and structural analysis and design work would be an advantage;
  • A good knowledge of the application of design and drafting software such as Autocad, Staad Pro, Plaxis, Prota, and Tedds would be an advantage;
  • Fresh graduates and experience candidates are welcome to apply. Experienced candidates may be considered for a more senior position.
  • Passing FEE 1 and FEE 2 are an advantage for senior hires.

About VEngineers

VEngineers Consultancy Pte Ltd is a leading engineering consultancy, founded in Singapore, with design offices in both Singapore and Malaysia. The company was originally founded in 2018 as a sole proprietorship under the name VEngineers. To support its growth and broaden its geographical footprint, the company transitioned toVEngineers Consultancy Pte Ltd in 2020. Our projects now span from Singapore to China, Bhutan, and Cambodia, showcasing our ability to adapt to various design codes and seismic conditions. In 2023, we expanded further by establishing VNM Engineering Sdn Bhd in Malaysia, aiming to collaborate with local consultants on landmark projects across the region. Over the years, we have successfullydelivered a diverse range of civil, structural, and geotechnical engineering projects both locally and abroad.

At VEngineers, innovation is at the core of everything we do. We constantly seek out innovative solutions that create value throughout the building life cycle for all stakeholders in the construction industry. Backed by a team of experienced and dedicated engineers, we are trusted by clients for delivering quality services and value-added solutions over the years. Our commitment to innovation has led to industry collaborations, including the commercialization of the Ultra-High Performance Concrete (UHPC) Prefabricated Bathroom Unit System across four residential projects. We also hold two patents in UHPC Prefabricated Prefinished Volumetric Construction System in Singapore. As we stand at the forefront of the industry, VEngineers continues to explore new ways to enhance productivity and sustainability within the built environment.

Vision:

To be a Civil & Structural (C&S) Consultancy firm recognised for innovative engineering solutions that are created in the course of work.

Mission:

To engineer construction solutions that create new values for all building stakeholders.

Why Join Us?

We offer attractive remuneration packages that commensurate with performance and work experience;

  • Flexibenefits, Medical, rand Dental
  • 13th AWS and Performance Bonus
  • Group Insurance
  • Training Grant and Education support,
  • Miscellaneous allowance,
  • Blue Sky Friday
  • Bonding Events

title: Application / Software Support Executive
raw_description:
Job Summary

We are seeking an Application / Software Support Executive to provide operational support for internal systems and business applications. The role involves incident handling, basic troubleshooting, and coordination with internal and external stakeholders to ensure applications operate reliably and efficiently. Training will be provided for entry-level candidates with a relevant educational background.

Key Responsibilities

  • Provide Level 1 application support for internal and customer-facing systems
  • Receive, log, track, and resolve incidents and service requests through ticketing systems
  • Perform basic troubleshooting of application, system, and access-related issues
  • Escalate complex or unresolved issues to senior support or development teams
  • Support system testing, user acceptance testing (UAT), and deployment activities
  • Conduct basic database checks (read-only) and application log reviews
  • Prepare and maintain system documentation, SOPs, and user guides
  • Coordinate with vendors and internal teams to resolve technical issues
  • Adhere to established support processes and service level agreements (SLAs)

Requirements

  • Diploma or Bachelor’s Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Information Systems, or related discipline
  • Entry-level candidates and fresh graduates are welcome to apply
  • Basic understanding of web and/or mobile applications
  • Basic knowledge of Windows and/or Linux operating systems
  • Basic understanding of networking fundamentals (e.g. IP addressing, DNS)
  • Basic knowledge of SQL (e.g. simple SELECT queries)
  • Ability to communicate clearly in English, both written and verbal
  • Willingness to learn, adaptable, and able to work effectively in a team environment


Preferred Skills (Advantageous)

  • Internship, project, or practical experience in application support or IT helpdesk
  • Familiarity with ticketing systems (e.g. Jira, Freshdesk, ServiceNow)
  • Exposure to cloud platforms (e.g. AWS, Azure)
  • Basic understanding of IT service management processes (e.g. Incident and Problem Management)

Additional Information

  • On-the-job training and guidance will be provided
  • Shift work or on-call support may be required based on operational needs
  • Appointment and remuneration will be commensurate with qualifications and experience
  • Employment is subject to applicable work pass approval (where required)

title: PLC Control Engineer (Marine Automation)
raw_description:
Our client is a Marine and Offshore Industry. Due to business needs, they are now recruiting a PLC Control Engineer to be part of their Team for ongoing transformation projects. They are located in the West - easily accessible.

Responsibilities:

  • Design and develop green marine solutions aligned with the company’s sustainability and business objectives
  • Set up and manage test laboratories to facilitate development, experimentation, and validation of electrical propulsion systems.
  • Develop PLC software and algorithms for advanced marine electrical propulsion solutions.
  • Produce comprehensive documentation, including software architecture, logic flow, test procedures, quality control protocols, and other supporting materials.
  • Participate in and support vessel commissioning activities for systems with similar configurations.

Requirements:

  • Min engineering degree in relevant discipline
  • Basic experience in software development skills particularly in equipment controls
  • Knowledge in power electronics, motor drives, marine propulsion systems is a plus

Job Responsibilities

General Ledger & Financial Maintenance
· Transaction Management: Accurately record all daily financial transactions in QuickBooks, including sales, expenses, and ledger entries.
· Reconciliation: Perform regular reconciliations of bank statements and financial accounts to ensure data integrity and accuracy

Tax Compliance
· Tax Compliance: Assist in the timely preparation and accurate filing of tax returns.

Accounts Payable & Receivable (AP/AR)
· Invoicing & Payments: Process incoming invoices, manage accounts payable, and ensure timely payments to vendors.
· Collections: Monitor accounts receivable and follow up on outstanding balances to ensure timely customer payments.
· Stakeholder Communication: Serve as the primary point of contact for vendor payment inquiries and assist customers with billing resolution.

Reporting & Analysis
· Financial Reporting: Generate monthly, quarterly, and annual financial reports and statements via QuickBooks to provide insights into the company's financial performance.
· Data Analysis: Analyse financial data to identify opportunities for cost reduction and operational efficiency, providing actionable recommendations to management.

Budgeting
· Budgeting & Forecasting: Collaborate with the Head of Finance to manage departmental budgets and assist in forecasting financial trends and variances.

Inventory Management
· Stock Monitoring: Monitor and reconcile inventory levels within QuickBooks.
· Auditing: Assist in conducting periodic physical inventory audits to ensure system accuracy.

Other Ad-hoc Duties
· Monthly Inter-company reconciliation in between parent company and all subsidiaries.
· Assist Manager and other team members of the department as assigned.

Job Requirements

Education and Additional Requirements
· Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or a related field
· Proficient in using QuickBooks or other accounting software.

Experience
· Minimum 2-3 years of experience in accounting and finance role (experience in e-commerce/fashion/apparel are preferable

Additional Requirements
• Knowledgeable in accounting principles and financial regulations to perform the role.
• Strong analytic and problem-solving abilities.
• Effective communication and interpersonal skills, and ability to work collaboratively in a team and meet deadlines.
• Strong organizational skills and ability to manage multiple priorities.

raw_title: Global Facilities and Construction Staff Architect
title: Global Facilities and Construction Staff Architect
raw_description:
Job Description

As the Global Facilities & Construction (South Asia)– Senior Architect, you provide high‑quality architectural leadership and technical authority for the planning, design, construction, operation, and maintenance of facilities systems supporting Micron’s global semiconductor manufacturing network. 

You will act as a subject matter expert throughout all project phases – from concept design and construction to installation, on-going operations, and maintenance – ensuring environmental, safety, regulatory, and code compliance.  

Key Responsibilities 

Architectural Design & Leadership 

  • Lead and define architectural design strategies for semiconductor facilities, including FABs, CUPs, Probe, Admin Building, and Miscellaneous Buildings. 

  • Develop conceptual designs, detailed plans, and specifications in compliance with Micron’s global standards and local/international codes.  

  • Provide technical support during construction, including submittal reviews, evaluating GC-initiated changes, developing project modifications, and conducting QA/QC reviews of design documents (e.g., architectural plans, specifications, and calculations).  

  • Perform technical reviews of structural design work by A&E firms, GC design teams, and sub-consultants to ensure quality and compliance.  

  • Prepare architectural design, RFPs, design documentation reports, and develop architectural layout plans, specifications, and cost estimates for projects.  

  • Oversee layout, interior design, and space planning for FABs, CUPs, Probe, Admin, and Miscellaneous Buildings. 

  • Conduct field inspections during construction to verify compliance with scope, schedule, and budget.  

  • Apply International Building Code (IBC) for exiting, fire compartmentation, LEED, Green Mark, sustainability, occupancy, and building classifications.  

  • Design specialized cleanroom components, including raised floors, demountable walls, and ceiling grid systems.  

  • Ensure constructability in complex environments, minimizing disruptions near operating buildings.  

  • Lead alignment of facility design standards, construction project management practices, and O&M best-known methods (BKM).  

  • Perform regular field inspections and monitor projects to ensure adherence to Micron specifications, codes, regulations, and agreed scope, schedule, and budget.  

  • Validate cost estimates and construction schedules for architectural aspects of projects.  

  • Review and approve design drawings and specifications for completeness and accuracy.  

  • Evaluate architectural drawing submissions and supporting documentation to ensure design integrity.  

  • Review Autodesk and BIM 3D models for functionality, egress, maintenance accessibility, constructability, and clash analyses coordination with other engineering disciplines.  

  • Inspect construction and installation progress for conformance to established drawings, specifications, and schedules. 

  • Support all sites, including on-site presence for construction projects and resolution of critical architectural issues 

  • Collaborate with consultants, contractors, and multidisciplinary teams to ensure timely, cost-effective project execution. 

Site Master Planning (SMP) & Space Optimization 

  • Develop, propose, and collaborate with global and site teams to generate preliminary site master plan (SMP) concept designs for greenfield and brownfield projects aligned with company strategic goals. 

  • Collaborate with multi-functional facilities teams to identify opportunities for space planning optimization and establish standards for critical facilities, rooms and areas. 

  • Standardize and continuously improve facilities space management for critical systems, leveraging industry best practices and lessons learned. 

  • Assess and mitigate risks related to space constraints in brownfield projects by conducting clash checks during preliminary stages and providing support for project continuity. 

Project Leadership & Strategic Alignment 

  • Lead design and construction of regional greenfield and brownfield projects in alignment with global standards. 

  • Collaborate with central sustainability, cost, construction, and line planning teams to ensure strategic alignment. 

  • Provide technical support for site projects, Alpha to Beta technology transfer, and sustainability initiatives. 

  • Review and approve site capacity expansion and sustainability CAPEX proposals. 

  • Evaluate priorities to enable business-driven decisions on Facilities Reliability Improvement project CAPEX. 

Reliability & Strategic Program Support 

  • Provide architectural support for site improvement projects, including System Reliability Improvements, Quality Enhancements, and Equipment End-of-Life (EOL) replacements. 

  • Oversee regional efforts and identify opportunities to align strategic programs (e.g., BCP, Critical Specifications, Quality Defense Line). 

  • Support major Site Infrastructure Planning reviews and ensure architectural compliance. 

Sustainability & Certifications 

  • Champion LEED and Green Mark certification processes for new and existing projects. 

  • Integrate sustainable design principles and energy-efficient solutions into all architectural plans. 

  • Support the network effort to implement, monitor and drive continuous improvement for success of Micron’s Sustainability programs in alignment with the long-term Sustainability strategy. 

  • In depth, review of site sustainability projects and sustainability data monitoring to support sites and company’s sustainability targets. 

  • Support sustainability projects achievement data acquisition and validation in central tracking depository. 

Interior Design & Space Optimization 

  • Oversee interior design for cleanrooms, office spaces, and collaborative environments. 

  • Ensure ergonomic, functional, and aesthetic excellence in space planning. 

Project Oversight & Coordination 

  • Manage architectural deliverables throughout design and construction phases. 

  • Collaborate with consultants, contractors, and multidisciplinary teams to ensure timely, cost-effective project execution. 

Compliance & Standards 

  • Ensure adherence to local and international building codes, safety regulations, and Micron’s internal standards. 

  • Manage permitting and regulatory approval processes. 

Qualifications 

  • Education: Master’s or bachelor’s degree in architecture. 

  • Experience:  

  • Minimum 10 years of architectural experience in industrial construction projects & minimum 5 year in Semiconductor and/or facilities related industry. 

  • Technical expertise, knowledge, and experience on building design and construction of Semiconductor Wafer Fab building and CUB building. 

  • Strong background in consulting and construction for fast-track large-scale semiconductor/industrial projects. 

  • Certifications:  

  • LEED and Green Mark (or equivalent) experience required. 

  • Skills:  

  • Proficiency in CAD software and BIM tools  

  • Expertise in semiconductor cleanroom design and high-tech manufacturing environments. 

  • Good knowledge and understanding of semiconductor manufacturing plants and local construction codes (preferably with fundamental knowledge of semiconductor manufacturing processes). 

  • Excellent interpersonal skills, strong collaboration and analytical abilities, ability to work independently, and proven team leadership experience. 

  • Ability to prioritize and schedule workload and work on multiple tasks concurrently. 

  • Ability to travel 15-25% of the time on average, but up to 30% of the time during Critical Project phases or Site Activities. 

  • Preferred:  

  • Professional Architect licensure.

title: Primary School Mathematics Teacher
raw_description:
About Us 

Founded by 2 expert young teachers, Math Vigilante is a tuition centre providing tutoring services for Math. We provide regular small group classes and 1-to-1 classes for students in primary, secondary and junior college levels. 

Who We Look For  

We are looking for individuals who are passionate and committed to help students improve and cultivate their interest in teaching. Individuals who are proactive and takes initiative to inspire and nurture students to reach their academic potential.  

We have positions for teachers who are able to teach PSLE Math, or any levels between Primary 1 to Primary 6.  

Job Responsibilities  

  • Plan, prepare, and deliver lessons. 

  • Create a positive classroom culture by nurturing positive relationships with students.  

  • Drive improvement and outstanding performance for students. 

  • Assess, record and report on development and progress of students. 

  • Provide regular updates to parents on students’ performance and progress. 

  • Ability to create social media deliverables, instagram/tiktok will be a bonus 

Requirements  

  • Post O-level graduate with good math academic records. 

  • Preferably with some teaching experiences.  

  • A good understanding of the MOE syllabus. 

  • Personable and able to work with young children  

  • Good communication and interpersonal skills.  

How to Apply 

Please note that to apply for this role, you must be residing in Singapore. 

Do send an email to mathvigilante@gmail.com and chanyuxin13@gmail.com with the following documents for us to process your application.  

  • Personal Resume  

  • ‘O’ Level Certificate 

  • ‘A’ Level Certificate / Diploma Certificate and Transcript

title: Technical Service Technician (Night Shift, Up to $2,500 + Allowances)
raw_description:
Description
A manufacturing company in Singapore is hiring a full-time Technical Service Technician. You will support production by operating equipment, performing preventive maintenance, and troubleshooting to meet output and quality targets. Training will be provided, fresh graduates are welcome.

Key Responsibilities
• Operate manufacturing equipment to meet production schedule and output targets
• Troubleshoot equipment issues to minimise downtime and meet quality standards
• Perform daily and monthly preventive maintenance to improve equipment reliability
• Support workplace safety practices and participate in quality improvement projects
• Maintain basic records for maintenance activities and equipment performance

Requirements
• Minimum NITEC in an Engineering discipline (Mechanical, Mechatronics, Robotics, or related)
• Able to read and understand electrical and mechanical diagrams
• Comfortable with 12-hour shift work and willing to work overtime when required
• Able to lift and carry 10–15kg
• Willing to work in a controlled manufacturing environment
• Open to fresh graduates
• Eligible to work in Singapore without employer sponsorship

Shift Pattern
• 12-hour shift, 4-3-3-4 work/rest cycle
Week 1: Work 4 days, rest 3 days
Week 2: Work 3 days, rest 4 days
Repeat cycle
• Assigned shift: Sunday Night Shift, 7:45pm to 7:55am

Salary & Allowances
• Basic Salary: Up to $2,500 per Month
• Allownaces: Up to $400++ per Month

Location
• Jurong East

Interested?
Kindly WhatsApp 8839 0096 and mention “Technical Service Technician”.

Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Epiphanius Tan
Outsourcing Team

raw_title: Product Engineer (Advance Data Analytics),  Heterogeneous Integration Group (HIG), High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) Product Engineering
title: Product Engineer (Advance Data Analytics), Heterogeneous Integration Group (HIG), High Bandwidth Memory (HBM) Product Engineering
raw_description:
Our vision is to transform how the world uses information to enrich life for all. Micron Technology is a world leader in innovating memory and storage solutions that accelerate the transformation of information into intelligence, inspiring the world to learn, communicate and advance faster than ever.

As a key team member within the HIG HBM Product Engineering Advanced Data Analytics team, you will be spearhead and develop a high-performing team to drive initiatives that significantly enhance yield and quality through advanced data analysis, machine learning, and AI. Your responsibilities will include developing cutting-edge algorithms, creating robust product disposition solutions, and establishing a proactive deviation alert system for yield and quality, enhancing data infrastructure, mentor team members, and fostering innovation to maintain a competitive edge. You will also collaborate with cross-functional teams within and outside of HBM to achieve both strategic and tactical objectives, continually maximizing the effectiveness of the HIG HBM PE organization, focusing on critical KPIs such as quality, cost, cycle time, and scale.

Key Responsibilities

  • Cumulative Yield Ownership: Be a key member of cross-functional teams, to lead impactful initiatives that significantly enhance overall yield and quality improvement, driving substantial benefits for the organization.

  • Data Analysis for Yield Improvement and Reliability: Utilize in-house statistical tools, machine learning, and AI for engineering data analysis to enhance yields and reliability, integrating these improvements within the Dispo workflow.

  • Models/Algorithm Development: Develop state-of-the-art algorithms, including Machine Learning and Deep Learning models, to advance data mining and pattern recognition for quality enhancement, yield improvement, wafer/die level screening and efficiency enhancement.

  • Data Infrastructure Development: Collaborate with IT, Manufacturing, and Operations teams to develop and enhance the data infrastructure pipeline using cloud technologies to support reliability and engineering assessments.

  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with various cross-functional teams, including Fab, HBM Technology Development, HBM Design, System Development, and Quality/Reliability teams, to ensure the holistic development and successful shipping of end products.

  • Promotion of Innovation: Promote innovation and drive changes that provide a technical advantage over competitors, maintaining the company's competitive edge in the market.

  • AI/ML Advocate: Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop, deploy, and validate AI/ML models aimed at enhancing key performance indicators (KPIs) such as Quality, Cost, Cycle Time, and Scale.

Requirements

  • Bachelor's or Master's degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Statistics, Artificial Intelligence, or related fields.

  • Experience building full-stack systems powered by GenAI, including RAG pipelines, vector databases, and LLM integration.

  • Proven ability to orchestrate data engineering pipelines, including SQL query optimization, RESTful API development, and Docker containerization.

  • Strong proficiency in machine learning, especially regression and classification models in Python.

  • Experience with BI tools like Power BI and Tableau

  • Required to work onsite in Singapore with international travel to Taiwan.
