title: Engineer, Process (Lithography) (Fresh Graduate)
raw_description:
Position Summary

The Process Engineer (Lithography) is responsible for the development and establishment of production process.


Responsibilities

  • Review and accept machine buyoff, setup pilot line process and transfer from pilot line to production line.
    Establish, review and update specifications, workflow, quality and safety related documentation for production line. 

  • Troubleshoot process issue and improve process capabilities, yield and cycle time from customer NPI stage to LVM/HVM stage.  
  • Process sustaining and control through SPC and periodic defect Pareto Analysis.  
  • Lead and drive process control measures such as SPC, FDC/KPC and RCA towards zero defects.  
  • Lead and drive continuous improvement activities in area of quality, productivity, cycle time, cost and process control automation. 
  • Review and update FMEA, Control plan OCAP with cross-functional teams. 
  • Coordinate and liaise with vendor/suppliers on process issue or quality issue. 
  • Support customer NPI activities and internal engineering activities. 
  • New material, process & machine development and qualification. 
  • Handle and manage customer audits and 8D reports.  
  • Provide disposition for engineering and production lots. 
  • Conduct process characterization and qualification to establish process baseline. 
  • Any other ad-hoc duties as assigned. 

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Chemical, Materials Science, Microelectronics, Mechanical Engineering or relevant engineering degrees.  
  • At least 3-years hands-on process experience in area of in the Wafer-level Fanout packaging production line or LCD/FPD production line in either Wet, PVD, Lithography or AOI. 
  • Knowledge in process engineering, process control engineering and yield enhancement. 
  • Experience in SPC, DOE, FEMA, and 8D reports

title: Delivery Rider (Long Term Part Timers)
raw_description:
Flex Logistics is a fast-growing Healthcare Supply Chain as a Service startup in Singapore.


For more details, please visit our website https://www.flexlogistics.sg/.


Job Description: A Logistics Specialist (Rider) is responsible for efficiently transporting goods and packages from one location to another using motorcycle. This role requires strong time management skills, able to work in a structured team environment, able to work under pressure and the ability to navigate through traffic and various terrains. Most importantly, strong customer service, there will be high degree of engagement with customers and what we deliver is quality.


Key Responsibilities:

1) Island wide collection and delivery of spectacles and lens (very light items)

2) Two waves per day, 12.30pm and 4.30pm

3) Start and return to Bendemeer daily

3) Our areas are clustered


Requirements

1) Possess Class 2B valid driving license.

2) Possess own motorbike with box.

3) Valid commercial motorcycle insurance.

4) Familiar with Singapore road.

5) Familiar with using mobile application for job assignment, POD etc.

6) Customer service oriented, good working attitude, interpersonal and communication skills.

7) Collaborative mindset that can excel in a structured team environment.


Part Time Rider :

1) Monday to Friday(11.30am to 7pm) - Supplier will be paid a daily fee of $80.00 per day for two waves of collection and delivery runs


2) Saturday (12.30pm to 6pm) - Supplier will be paid a daily fee of $100.00 per day for one waves of collection and delivery runs


3) Sunday and Public Holiday (12pm onwards, OTOT) - Supplier will be paid a daily fee of $100.00 per day for one wave of collection and delivery run.



Additional incentives for commitment - $20.00 per day, for completion of full week without fail. These additional incentives will not apply for any lapses in any working day of the week. It is not considered a lapse if there is a Public Holiday within the week,



**As we recieved many applicants for the role, we will priortize riders that can commit to Mon-FRI schedule.**



ONLY APPLY THROUGH CAREERFUTURE OR EMAIL yiting.liew@flexlogistics.sg or apply via https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1nRoqwPZAx6z5CStmsRQX2j3J-J-erR6S3MN084_XR_E/edit?ts=64abb4b0

title: Conference Producer / Conference Marketing Specialist
raw_description:
The Conference Producer / Conference Marketing Specialist is a full-time position based in Singapore. As a Conference Producer / Conference Marketing Specialist, you will be involved in understanding and providing support in the smooth running of conferences from concept to execution, ensuring timelines are met, and that each event exceeds expectations. For virtual events, you will be responsible for both production and marketing; for in-person events, you will be responsible for either production or marketing. You can let us know your preference during the interview for Conference Producer or Conference Marketing Specialist role.

You will be responsible for the following areas:

  • Market Research: Assist in conducting preliminary research on market trends, competitor landscapes, and industry challenges;
  • Conference agenda curation: Assist in scheduling sessions with the chair and session chairs, coordinating with speakers and session chairs, and ensuring timely updates to the event agenda;
  • Stakeholder Engagement: Assist in maintaining communication with key stakeholders, including chair, session chairs, speakers to create advocacy for our events
  • Website Management: Assist in managing event websites, ensuring it is updated timely with the latest information, speakers, partners, and sessions;
  • Customer Service: Provide exceptional service to partners, speakers, and stakeholders, resolving issues promptly;
  • Team Coordination: Coordinate with editorial team, marketing team, live session teams;
  • Editorial progress monitor: Work closely with the editorial team on abstract processing and with production teams on abstract book preparation;
  • Live streaming of virtual conferences: Assist in coordinating and supporting the live streaming of virtual conferences, ensuring seamless technical operation, real-time troubleshooting, and smooth engagement throughout the session
  • Assist in onsite delivery: Assist in the creation and delivery of event briefs for on-site delivery, ensuring exceptional on-site execution of the conferences to meet customer expectations;
  • Plan and execute virtual conferences, ensuring seamless coordination and delivery of all sessions and activities

Others

  • Recognize work processes and/or outcomes that are negatively impacting the project; owns the issue and takes action to address deficiencies by identifying resolutions and notifying the appropriate manager

Requirements:

  • A Bachelor or Master's degree in any subject
  • Fresh graduates are welcome to apply
  • Experience in conference industry is preferred
  • Are curious about people and love to speak, build and nurture relations
  • Capable of handling multiple projects, and articulate common objectives for the teams to follow.
  • Excellent online call etiquette and written communication skills
  • International travel and confidence in face-to-face networking
  • Proven ability to work well in a team but also initiative to work on your own

What We Offer:

  • Professional and personal development
  • International work environment
  • Flexible working hours in the limits of a full-time job
  • Trips to the other MDPI offices
  • Attending Scientific Conferences
  • Competitive salary, benefits

title: Administrative Executive (Take A Break Programme, TAB)
raw_description:
To provide administrative support, feedback, data collection and evaluation of Take-a-Break programme.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES AND ACCOUNTABILITIES

Administration & Operations

  • Responsible to programme head and division director in all matters pertaining to the administration and streamlining of TAB.
  • Handle all Information & Enquiries and conduct assessment to recommend outcome of a referral to TAB as a core responsibility
  • Update and manage dashboards
  • Suggest practices for the efficient and effective operation and management of services and assist in implementing those policies and practices.
  • Responsible for achieving key performance indicators in the client reach, outcomes and impact. This includes completion of progress reports and assisting in data collation, or analysis for planning and reporting purposes in a timely manner.
  • Collaborate with and respond to funder on any queries on TAB programme cases.
  • Provide administrative support and coverage to other TAB colleagues.

Service Management

  • Triage, manage and deploy respite service providers, as relevant to the needs of the clients and caregivers / families.
  • Manage working relationship with key stakeholders etc such as mediating between social workers (applying on behalf of clients / caregivers) and service providers
  • Find out the needs of the clients/caregivers in the area and collate the data
  • Collaborate with funder and appointed service providers to identify service pain points and work towards solutioning.

Networking & Professional Matters

  • Network, participate and contribute professionally through work with relevant professionals and stakeholders that involves TAB programme.
  • Represent TAB to undertake outreach efforts to referral agencies, community organisations and any other relevant agencies
  • Supervise TAB colleagues that may include social worker, programme assistant and social work associate and work with caregivers/clients to offer services to the clients.

Other Duties

  • Any other duties as may be assigned from time to time.

JOB HOLDER REQUIREMENT

  • A diploma or degree in any discipline related to social sciences or related disciplines
  • At least 3 years of relevant working experience in administrative work or managing programmes for people with disabilities. Experience in social service sector preferred.
  • Analytical, flexible, resilient and ability to see different perspectives and draw connections
  • Confident as an independent worker and a team player
  • Good interpersonal skills

Job Description

  • Industry/ Organization Type: Healthcare
  • Position Title: Resident Doctor/ Anchor General Practitioner
  • Working Location: Clementi
  • Working Hours: Weekdays: 8:30am–3:00pm (evening sessions 6:00pm–9:00pm on Mon/Wed/Thu), Saturday: 8:30am–1:00pm
  • Salary Package: Basic + Progressive Profit Share component
  • Duration: Permanent

Key Responsibilities

  • Provide safe, high-quality, patient-centred primary care in an HSG/CHAS/MediSave-accredited clinic with good patient footfall from the surrounding neighbourhood.
  • Consult, assess, diagnose, and treat patients.
  • Manage common acute conditions (e.g., URTI, fever, minor injuries).

title: Research Intern
raw_description:
We are looking for a meticulous, diligent, and resourceful individual to evaluate the impact of sustainability projects. The intern will analyse case studies according to the Steward Leadership framework and identify scalable, sustainable projects that address environmental and social challenges.

Responsibilities:

  • Review case studies: Thoroughly evaluate all materials and present findings to the reviewing team.
  • Conduct desktop research on impact metrics, existing regulations, and industry standards to perform robust analysis of case studies.
  • Collaborate with manager and teammates: Work closely with the process manager and teammates to ensure smooth communication and adherence to timelines.
  • Assist in other administrative and research development duties as needed.

Requirements:

  • Postgraduate students from a relevant field such as Social Science, Business Administration, Corporate Sustainability, or Sustainable Development are preferred.
  • Attention to detail: Keen eye for spotting outstanding qualities in case studies and ensuring compliance with guidelines.
  • Time management skills: Ability to meet deadlines and manage the review process efficiently.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Good team player yet able to work well independently.
  • Meticulous, analytical, highly organised and intellectually inquisitive.
  • Prior experience in evaluating case studies is a bonus.

Application deadline: 30 April 2026

Internship Period: 18 May – 25 Jun 26, full time.

Location: Singapore; Hybrid (you are required to attend meetings in-person when necessary)

To apply: Please submit your resume and a brief cover letter explaining your relevant experience to adelinefoo@stewardshipasia.com.sg.

We look forward to your application!

title: Graduate Trainee (Corporate Services)
raw_description:
Nexcore Consulting Group is a renowned consultancy firm dedicated to offering comprehensive solutions for businesses and individuals aiming for overseas operations and cross-border expansions. Our footprint spans globally, including pivotal regions like Singapore, Hong Kong, Japan, the US, Dubai, and China. Whether it's setting up company structures, accounting, or tax and legal compliance issues, our expert team is equipped to handle every challenge and ensure smooth business transitions.

Role Overview


As a Graduate Trainee (Corporate Services), you will receive structured on-the-job training and hands-on exposure to corporate secretarial and compliance matters. This role is suitable for candidates who are detail-oriented, eager to learn, and looking to develop professional skills in the corporate services industry.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in company incorporation and post-incorporation matters;
  • Support preparation and filing of corporate secretarial documents (e.g. resolutions, annual returns, changes in company information);
  • Assist with KYC / AML documentation and client due diligence checks;
  • Coordinate and prepare documents for bank account opening and work pass applications;
  • Maintain accurate client records and statutory registers;
  • Communicate with clients and external parties via email in a professional manner;
  • Support the team in day-to-day corporate compliance and administrative tasks;

Requirements

  • Diploma or Degree in Business, Accounting, Finance, Law, or related fields;
  • Fresh graduates are welcome to apply; candidates with up to 1 year of relevant experience will be considered;
  • Good command of written and spoken English;
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to follow checklists and procedures;
  • Willingness to learn, responsible, and able to work in a fast-paced environment;
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Word and Excel;

What We Offer

  • Structured training and mentorship;
  • Clear career progression path in corporate services;
  • Exposure to local and international clients;

title: Social Media Marketing Executive (Content & On-Camera)
raw_description:
Roles & Responsibilities

  • Plan, create, and manage engaging content across social media platforms (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, etc.).
  • Act as the on-camera talent for product promotion videos, lifestyle shoots, and brand campaigns.
  • Work with the team to script, shoot, and edit short-form videos and creative content.
  • Monitor and analyze social media performance to improve engagement and reach.
  • Research and stay updated on current social media trends, especially in video content.
  • Support marketing campaigns, events, and collaborations with influencers.

Job Offers & Benefits

  • Attractive basic salary with performance incentives.
  • Career growth opportunities in digital marketing & branding.
  • Transport / mobile allowance may be provided.
  • Fun and creative working environment with hands-on content projects.
  • Training in video production and social media tools will be provided.

Candidate Role

  • Be both a social media marketer and brand ambassador.
  • Take part in video shoots and bring energy/personality to the brand.
  • Build strong brand presence through creative storytelling.

Job Scope

  • Manage daily posting, scheduling, and interaction on social media accounts.
  • Participate in video shoots (product features, lifestyle content, creative skits).
  • Work with content creator/video editor to produce engaging campaigns.
  • Track analytics, prepare reports, and propose improvements.

Job Requirements

  • Diploma in Marketing, Mass Communication, Media, or related field (or equivalent experience).
  • Comfortable appearing on camera and able to act/present naturally in videos.
  • Familiar with TikTok, Instagram Reels, and short-form video trends.
  • Creative, energetic, and passionate about digital marketing.
  • Knowledge of basic video editing or Canva/Photoshop will be an advantage.
  • Fresh graduates or entry-level candidates with strong interest in social media are welcome.

title: Graduate Trainee (Accounting Services)
raw_description:
Nexcore is a renowned consultancy firm dedicated to offering comprehensive solutions for businesses and individuals aiming for overseas operations and cross-border expansions. Our footprint spans globally, including pivotal regions like Singapore, Hong Kong, Japan, the US, Dubai, and China. Whether it's setting up company structures, accounting, or tax and legal compliance issues, our expert team is equipped to handle every challenge and ensure smooth business transitions.

Role Overview

As a Graduate Trainee (Accounting Services), you will receive hands-on training in bookkeeping and accounting support functions. This role is ideal for candidates who are looking to build a strong foundation in accounting and compliance, even without prior working experience.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in bookkeeping and maintenance of accounting records
  • Support preparation of management accounts and financial schedules
  • Assist with bank reconciliations, invoices, and expense tracking
  • Prepare supporting documents for audit and tax filing purposes
  • Maintain proper documentation and filing of accounting records
  • Communicate with clients to follow up on accounting-related information
  • Support the accounting team in day-to-day operational tasks

Requirements

  • Diploma or Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, or related fields
  • Fresh graduates are welcome to apply; candidates with up to 1 year of accounting experience will be considered
  • Basic understanding of accounting principles is preferred (training will be provided)
  • Comfortable working with numbers and Excel
  • Good written and spoken English
  • Responsible, detail-oriented, and willing to learn

What We Offer

  • Structured training in accounting and compliance work
  • Exposure to SME and international client portfolios
  • Career development opportunities in professional services
  • Supportive team and learning environment
  • CPF contributions in accordance with local regulations

title: Derivatives Trading Executive (Entry-Level)
raw_description:
Derivatives Trading Executive -

Entry-level

  • Execute derivatives and forward/future contracts and/or other financial products trading instructions.
  • Collect and organize information related to assigned product and establish database to evaluate the market fundamentally and technically by following up supply, demand, global/regional geo-political developments and market dynamics.
  • Conduct research, analysis, and prepare regular reports on assigned products on daily and weekly basis to develop position-taking strategies on key markets.
  • Maintain real-time trading portfolio position to facilitate contracts pricing and portfolio valuation.
  • Reconcile executed derivatives trades with broker statements and confirmations on daily basis.
  • Maintain accurate reporting of derivative transactions and results.
  • Monitor margin calls/excess cash related to derivatives and work with finance team to optimize positions across brokers to minimize cash utilization.

title: Supply Chain & Inventory Administration Intern
raw_description:
We are looking for a Supply Chain & Inventory Administration Intern to support the daily operations of our bakery. This role is primarily administrative in nature, focusing on inventory tracking, purchasing coordination, and supplier documentation, ensuring smooth and timely supply of ingredients and packaging materials for production.

This internship offers hands-on exposure to supply chain processes in a food production environment, while building strong foundations in operations administration.

Key Responsibilities

Inventory & Stock Administration

  • Assist in maintaining accurate inventory records for ingredients, packaging, and supplies

  • Track stock movement and usage through spreadsheets or inventory systems

  • Flag low-stock items and support replenishment planning

  • Support periodic stock counts and reconciliation

Purchasing & Supplier Coordination

  • Assist with purchase order preparation and documentation

  • Liaise with suppliers on order confirmations, delivery schedules, and basic follow-ups

  • Track purchase orders and delivery status

  • Organise supplier invoices, delivery orders, and related records

Administrative & Operations Support

  • Maintain organised records for supply chain and operations documentation

  • Support data entry, filing, and report preparation

  • Assist with process documentation and simple workflow improvements

  • Provide general administrative support to the operations team as needed

Requirements

  • Currently pursuing or recently completed a diploma/degree in Business, Supply Chain, Operations, Logistics, or a related field

  • Comfortable with administrative work and data handling

  • Proficient in Microsoft Excel / Google Sheets

  • Detail-oriented and organised

  • Able to follow procedures accurately and consistently

  • Interest in operations, supply chain, or food production is a plus

What You’ll Learn

  • End-to-end inventory and purchasing workflows

  • Supply chain administration in a bakery/food production setting

  • Practical exposure to supplier coordination and stock control

  • Real-world operations support and documentation practices

Internship Allowance: $800-$1000

title: Management Trainee Program (Fresh Graduate))
raw_description:
Job Description & Requirements


As a Management Trainee, your main goal will be to learn and master various aspects of the company's operations, including but not limited to operations management, human resources management, team leadership, decision-making, and problem-solving. Through this training program, you will acquire the necessary skills and knowledge to prepare for future senior management positions.


Key Accountabilities:

  1. Continuously learn and understand the specific business operations and management strategies of the company.
  2. Participate in and lead team projects, enhancing teamwork and leadership.
  3. Effectively manage and execute projects in a multitasking and high-pressure environment, with a results-oriented approach.
  4. Solve problems encountered in work, formulate and implement solutions, emphasizing replicable processes.
  5. Embrace change, continuously improve your skills and knowledge, and break through your own capability boundaries.

Main Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Participate in the company's daily operations and management activities, learning the company's business processes and strategies.
  • Assume a leadership role in projects, effectively distribute tasks and manage progress, ensuring smooth completion of projects.
  • Think independently when encountering problems, propose and implement solutions, and conduct a review after problem resolution.
  • Accept feedback from superiors and colleagues, continuously improve your skills and knowledge, and maintain positive energy with an open mindset to change.
  • Enhance your professional knowledge and skills by participating in the company's training and learning activities and continuously learning.

Job Specification:

  • Education: Bachelor's or master's degree in business management, education, psychology, or other related fields.
  • Skills: Leadership and teamwork abilities, problem-solving and decision-making capabilities, project management skills.
  • Personal Characteristics: High self-drive, leadership, adaptability, resilience.
  • Career Goals: Passionate about education and training, looking forward to long-term development in the company, willing to take up a management position after completing the training.
  • Others: Deep understanding of the education and training market in Singapore, familiar with basic information technology and software applications.

Working Hours:

  • Monday: Off
  • Tuesday to Friday: 1:30 PM to 7:30 PM
  • Saturday and Sunday: 9:00 AM to 7:30 PM

title: Asset & Wealth Management, AWM Public Operations, Client Services - Operations, Analyst, Singapore
raw_description:
Asset Management Operations 
We provide customized service to our clients and enable our business to grow the Asset Management franchise across traditional and alternative products. Our teams execute within a front to back operating model, delivering support across the client, product, and investment lifecycle. We also conduct risk oversight, provide change management, business intelligence, and market solutions for the division.

The Global Shareholder Services has teams in Chicago, London, Tokyo, Hong Kong and Singapore. We are responsible for the ongoing Client Services and Transfer Agent vendor management covering over 1,000 Goldman Sachs offshore mutual funds across a wide range of client types including Third Party Distributors, Banks, Institutions and High Net Worth individuals.  

Role Summary & Responsibilities
As a Shareholder Services Asia Client Services Manager, you will work closely with Shareholder Services and relevant stakeholders across the region, managing investors / client facing teams to ensure service delivery is maintained at a highest quality. Additionally, you will support other Shareholder Services functions in onboarding new clients, protecting and maintaining existing clients. In addition, you will be collaborating with internal teams and service providers (e.g. transfer agents) to offer guidance and manage Services related solutions, and continuously enhancing our process in line with best market practice. 

The Client Services role contributes to asset gathering, enhancing client engagement, identifies operational efficiencies opportunity, and differentiates through technology and, ultimately improving the client experience.  You will also execute the Operations’ strategy and modernize the client experience.

You are expected to provide clear direction and leadership, whilst driving your team forward in promoting staff development and process improvement whilst also providing efficient service-level reporting and performance metrics. 

Keys to this strategic role include a proactive and self-motivated attitude, strong communication skills, a thorough understanding of mutual fund Shareholder Servicing/Transfer Agent operations, and experience in selling/implementing technology and service solutions.
 

HOW YOU WILL FULFILL YOUR POTENTIAL
Plan and lead the end-to-end process of implementation & transition events including client onboarding and change, asset take-on / take-off activities, and account terminations.

  • Project-management approach to client onboarding & change events including complex portfolio structures across the asset classes.
  • Partner with Shareholder Services team colleagues and in some instances sales organization to ensure a seamless client onboarding from front to back and liaise with multiple parties to bring about execution of a transaction
  • Communicate with internal & external parties and teams, provide advice and consulting with all participant parties on all aspects of onboarding and change events.
  • Ensure due diligence is performed on new clients to comply with “Know Your Customer” regulations
  • Develop expertise in the Firm's Anti Money Laundering/Customer Identification Program due diligence policies involving country specific requirements
  • Satisfy the responsibility for completing detailed reviews of documentation for existing clients, including legal agreements and organizational documents
  • Becoming commercially aware and understand the nature of the business you participate in while at the same time ensuring consistent adherence to all Goldman Sachs CIP standards
  • Displaying a strong ability to influence the Asset management business by serving as a liaison between Anti-Money Laundering Compliance and Sales teams
  • Engage in opportunities to review processes, analyze information, make recommendations, and implement and monitor procedure enhancements
  • Proactively review indicators of operational issues/concerns with the goal of resolving such issues before client impact
  • Proven analytical and problem solving skills with meticulous attention to detail to enable the ability to communicate complex items to a wide array of internal counterparties
  • Self-motivated and proactive team player who takes ownership and accountability of projects, and has strong organizational skills to effectively manage competing priorities
  • Flexible and able to work well under pressure in a team environment 

BASIC QUALIFICATIONS

  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent
  • 2-3 years financial industry experience (AML / KYC or other Client Onboarding Regulatory experience a plus)
  • Highly motivated and results orientated
  • Have a high level of self-initiative to challenge and improve processes
  • Strong understanding of risk and control framework in operations
  • Has strong interpersonal skills to establish Business relationships
  • Able to manage multiple tasks or projects and manage deadlines effectively
  • Strong team player

 PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

  • Business level in both written and spoken Mandarin to cover different markets and deal with existing clients to comply with AML/KYC regulations and interact with our external stakeholders

ABOUT GOLDMAN SACHS
At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world.  

We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs. Learn more about our culture, benefits, and people at GS.com/careers.  

We’re committed to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates with special needs or disabilities during our recruiting process. Learn more: https://www.goldmansachs.com/careers/footer/disability-statement.html

©The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc., 2026. All rights reserved.
Goldman Sachs is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, veterans status, disability, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

title: Assistant Project Engineer
raw_description:
An EPC Solar PV System Integrator providing technologies solutions in Engineering, Design, Procurement, Construction, Testing and Commissioning, Operations and Maintenance of Solar Energy Systems from concept to completion. 

Responsibilities:

  • The candidate has to lead the execution solar projects including procurement, supervision of subcontractor with the following tasks:
  • Conduct detailed site survey and modify solar PV system designs that meet client requirements, local codes, and industry standards.
  • Optimise system layout for maximum energy production and cost-effectiveness.
  • Conduct shading analysis and assess site suitability for solar installations.
  • Develop bill of materials (BOM) for projects.
  • Use modelling software to predict system performance and energy output.
  • Prepare and submit permit applications, ensuring compliance with local regulations and utility requirements.
  • Coordinate with regulatory authorities to secure necessary approvals.
  • Develop and implement quality control procedures for solar installations.
  • Conduct inspections to ensure system integrity and safety standards.
  • Provide technical guidance and support to installation teams during system setup.
  • Troubleshoot and resolve technical issues as they arise.
  • Create and maintain accurate and detailed project documentation, including schematics, drawings, and technical manuals.
  • Manage manpower and ensure project run smoothly.
  • Monitor and ensure systems are operating optimally before handing over to clients.
  • Promote and adhere to safety protocols and ensure that all installations meet safety requirements.
  • Provide technical support for sales, proposals, and during potential client meetings.

Requirements:

  • Diploma in Engineering
  •  Fresh graduates are welcome
  • Candidates will undergo structured hands-on On The Job training under experienced PV Engineers 
  • Candidate must be willing to work in the Engineering and Construction industry.
  • Passionate in renewable energy is a plus
  • Consistent and dedicated personality in improving projects including applications of better ideas or improving productivity with management/technical skills

Others:

  • Work location: Central region, Singapore (Near MRT)
  • 5 days’ work week

title: Settlement & Operation Officer
raw_description:
Company Overview
Pureheart Capital Pte. Ltd. (“Pureheart”) is a Singapore-based, MAS-regulated licensed fund management company managing over USD 1.3 billion in assets across two global macro hedge funds.

We offer a dynamic and collaborative environment where operations professionals play a critical role in supporting daily fund activities, including trade settlements, reconciliations, reporting, and regulatory compliance. At Pureheart, you will gain hands-on experience across middle- and back-office operations, work closely with senior management, and contribute to improving operational efficiency in a fast-paced hedge fund setting.

Job Description – Settlement and Operations Officer
We are seeking a detail-oriented Settlement and Operations Officer to support the firm’s day-to-day middle- and back-office operations. This role is key in ensuring accurate trade settlements, reconciliations, and smooth coordination with internal and external stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support daily operations, including trade settlement, trade reconciliations, fund payments, and fund report preparation
  • Perform monthly NAV reconciliations
  • Liaise with auditors, fund administrators, custodians, brokers, counterparties, and legal consultants
  • Communicate with brokers to ensure timely trade settlements
  • Support day-to-day middle- and back-office operations, including broker account setup
  • Ensure compliance with all relevant regulatory requirements, including reporting and filing
  • Participate in the development and implementation of operational processes and procedures to improve efficiency
  • Perform ad-hoc operational duties as required

Requirements

  • Minimum 3 years of relevant work experience, including at least 1 year in back-office or fund operations at an asset manager, fund administrator, or buy/sell-side financial institution
  • Strong quantitative and analytical skills; excellent knowledge of Microsoft Office
  • Familiarity with hedge fund valuation (NAV), financial statements, and accounting practices
  • Meticulous, detail-oriented, and organized
  • Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills in English; fluency in Mandarin is an advantage
  • Proficiency with Bloomberg Terminal
  • Relevant degree from an internationally recognized university

What We Offer

  • Attractive salary and bonus
  • Exposure to a fast-paced global macro hedge fund environment
  • Opportunity to work closely with senior management and gain broad operational experience

Interested candidates, please send your latest resume. All information will be treated in strict confidence and used solely for employment-related purposes.

Our team of passionate social workers supports children and youth with disabilities and their families in leading meaningful lives—where they are safe, connected, healthy, and heard.

Job Responsibilities:

The Administration Operations Executive is responsible for ensuring the smooth and effective daily operations of RC’s services. The role provides reliable administrative, data, and operational support to service leaders and teams, enabling them to focus on high-quality service delivery to clients and families. This role supports a broad range of administrative and operational functions, including financial and staff administration, client records management, procurement support, data integrity, reporting, and customer service.

Job Requirements:

title: Associate Executive, Finance (1 Year Contract)
raw_description:
Job Summary

You will be working on a hybrid role which encompasses managing backend administration of patient billing operations, driving accuracy and efficiency in financial processes for both Business Office (BO) and Accounts Receiveable (AR) functions, while supporting audit readiness and automation initiatives to enhance operational effectiveness.

Responsibilities

  • Process patient bills and administer financial assistance schemes such as Medifund to ensure accurate billing and compliance
  • Manage corporate billing and receipting activities
  • Process payments and receipt transactions into subledger systems, generating relevant financial reports
  • Follow up with corporate and individual payors to resolve outstanding payments
  • Prepare cashflow reports and conduct cash projection analyses
  • Create journal entries in SAP and prepare audit schedules to facilitate internal and external audit processes
  • Organize and compile supporting documentation to ensure audit readiness and compliance
  • Support the setup and deployment of Robotic Process Automation (RPA) tools such as UiPath or Power Automate to streamline repetitive tasks
  • Execute ad hoc duties assigned by supervisors to support team objectives and business needs

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Minimum diploma in finance or accounting
  • Fresh graduates are welcome
  • Candidates pursuing part-time degrees who can commit to required work hours are welcome to apply
  • Proficient in Microsoft Excel for data analysis and reporting
  • Experience with SAP, RPA tools (UiPath, Power Automate), or VBA programming is advantageous
  • Strong communication skills to confidently engage with diverse stakeholders
  • Meticulous and organized approach to managing numerical data with high accuracy
  • Proactive learner with the ability to work both independently and in a team

title: Internship Interior Designer
raw_description:
Launch Your Interior Design Career: A Career-Focused Internship

About Us:
At HG Works Pte Ltd, we are passionate about crafting inspiring and functional spaces. As a dynamic interior design firm, we believe in nurturing the next generation of talent. When you join our collaborative team, you’re not just completing tasks; you’re building the foundation for your professional future.

Your Career Opportunity:
This full-time internship is designed as a launchpad for your design career. Go beyond academic theory and immerse yourself in the complete lifecycle of live projects. Under the direct mentorship of our senior designers, you will develop the practical skills and professional mindset essential for success in the industry. The internship begins with a 3-month introductory period to ensure a valuable mutual fit, with the opportunity to extend for deeper, long-term career development.

Build Your Professional Skills & Portfolio:
In this role, you will actively contribute while building critical career assets:

  • Develop Design Solutions: Assist in creating tangible design concepts, including space planning, material palettes, and colour schemes that solve real client needs.

  • Master Project Coordination: Gain hands-on experience supporting projects from concept to installation, learning to liaise effectively with clients, suppliers, and contractors.

  • Enhance Client Communication: Participate in client meetings and contribute to presentation materials, building confidence and professional communication skills.

  • Execute Collaborative Workflow: Collaborate with our internal team to see how design intent is translated into actionable project plans.

  • Strengthen Industry Knowledge: Conduct research on trends, materials, and innovations, keeping your design thinking current and informed.

Your Foundational Profile:
We are looking for a future colleague who is building their professional toolkit:

  • Currently pursuing or a recent graduate of a Diploma/Degree in Interior Design or a related field.

  • Building Technical Proficiency: Foundational skills in AutoCAD, SketchUp, and Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, InDesign) are essential.

  • Demonstrating Core Competencies: A keen visual eye, clear communication, and strong organizational abilities.

  • Possessing a Professional Mindset: A proactive, eager-to-learn attitude and the collaborative spirit needed in a studio environment.

  • Showcasing Your Potential: A portfolio that reflects your design thinking and passion, even if it's academic or personal work.

Invest in Your Career Future – What You Will Gain:
This internship is an investment in your long-term success. Here’s how it will propel your career forward:

  • Professional Mentorship: Receive direct, ongoing guidance from experienced designers who are invested in your growth.

  • A Robust Professional Portfolio: Contribute to real-world projects, giving you compelling, client-based work to showcase to future employers.

  • Inside Industry Insight: Understand the full professional workflow, from initial briefing and client management to project execution and delivery.

  • Practical Experience: Translate classroom knowledge into applied skills, making you a confident and job-ready candidate.

  • Competitive Monthly Stipend: Your contribution is valued, and you will be supported with a stipend for the duration of your internship.

Ready to build your future in design? Submit your portfolio and resume to start the conversation.

Key Changes and Benefits:

  • Strong Career-Oriented Framing: The headline and descriptions consistently frame the internship as a strategic career step, not just a temporary role.

  • Focus on Skill Acquisition: Responsibilities are phrased to highlight the transferable professional skills the intern will develop and master.

  • Reframed Requirements: Changed to "Your Foundational Profile," positioning requirements as the starting point for growth rather than just a checklist.

  • Enhanced Value Proposition: The "What You Will Gain" section is expanded to clearly articulate the long-term career benefits, including job readiness and industry insight.

  • Professional Tone: Uses more assertive and professional language ("Launch Your Career," "Master Project Coordination," "Invest in Your Career Future") to attract serious, ambitious candidates.

  • Clear Call to Action: The closing line invites candidates to take a proactive step for their career

title: Digital Marketing Strategist
raw_description:
Job Description

We are looking for a Digital Marketing Strategist to support the company’s digital marketing initiatives and brand presence for our central kitchen and food production solutions.

The role focuses on planning and executing digital marketing activities, improving online visibility, and supporting business growth through digital channels.

Key Responsibilities

·      Plan and execute digital marketing campaigns across online platforms

·      Manage and optimise company website, social media and digital content

·      Support brand positioning and online communication for central kitchen and food-related solutions

·      Assist in developing marketing strategies to reach B2B customers, partners and distributors

·      Monitor digital campaign performance and prepare basic reports and insights

·      Coordinate marketing materials to ensure consistency with brand guidelines

·      Support lead generation and online customer engagement activities

Requirements

·      Bachelor’s degree or above in Marketing, Digital Marketing, Communications, Business or related fields

·      Strong understanding of digital marketing fundamentals

·      Familiarity with social media platforms, content management and online marketing tools

·      Ability to analyse basic digital performance metrics

·      Good communication and organisational skills

·      Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks

Preferred / Advantageous

·      Experience or exposure to B2B marketing, F&B or food-related industries

·      Knowledge of digital marketing trends and content planning

·      Ability to support structured content and online engagement

·      Exposure to regional or overseas markets is an advantage

title: Research Assistant - Mid Size Molecule (Chemistry)
raw_description:
Chugai Pharmabody Research Pte. Ltd. (“CPR”) was established in Singapore as a wholly-owned subsidiary of Chugai Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd (Chugai), a member of Roche group, in January 2012 and started operations on July 2, 2012. Located at Biopolis in Singapore, it aims to generate new antibody drugs based on Chugai’s proprietary antibody engineering technologies, such as recycling antibody®, sweeping antibody®, bispecific antibody, various Fc engineered antibodies, and other new antibody engineering technologies that are under development.


Research Assistant – Mid-size Molecule Unit (Chemistry)


Job Scope:

  • Peptide synthesis – solid phase, liquid phase synthesis
  • Compound synthesis – amino acid, nucleotide derivative, tool compound
  • Peptide purification - preparative LC
  • Peptide/small molecule analysis - LCMS, UPLC, NMR, etc.
  • Lab management

Requirements

The applicant is recommended to have experience in at least one of the following areas:

  • Organic synthesis of small molecules, mid-size molecules or DNA/RNA.
  • Medicinal chemistry.
  • Analysis for small molecules or mid-size molecules.
  • Familiar with basic laboratory procedures.
  • Skilled in solid phase synthesis will be an added advantage.
  • Fresh graduates are welcome to apply.
  • A relevant degree in Organic Chemistry/Medicinal Chemistry/Biochemistry/Chemical Biology with 1-2 years of working experience in a related field.

Interested applicants are invited to apply now with their detailed resume, qualifications, availability, current and expected salary.


For more information regarding our Company, you may refer to our website at:

http://chugai-pharmabody.com/


We regret that only short-listed candidates will be notified

title: Shift Trader
raw_description:
Working Hours: This role requires working on shifts to support 24/7 coverage across regions
Team: Central Trading & Monitoring

Squarepoint is a global investment management firm that utilizes a diversified portfolio of systematic and quantitative strategies across financial markets that seeks to achieve high quality, uncorrelated returns for our clients. We have deep expertise in trading, technology and operations and attribute our success to rigorous scientific research. As a technology and data-driven firm, we design and build our own cutting-edge systems, from high performance trading platforms to large scale data analysis and compute farms. With offices around the globe, we emphasize true, global collaboration by aligning our investment, technology, and operations teams functionally around the world.  

Role Overview

Squarepoint is building a Central Trading & Monitoring team to ensure around-the-clock supervision of trading activities and operational processes. As a Shift Trader, you will play a critical role in risk management, trade oversight, operational lifecycle management, and incident response. You will also actively contribute to the automation and efficiency of the team’s workflow by developing tools and scripts to streamline monitoring and trading operations.

Responsibilities

Day-to-day remit:
- Active Risk Monitoring:

  • Monitor real-time exposures across Squarepoint’s trading activities
  • Proactively raise alerts and escalate incidents as required
  • Execute hedging actions following established procedures
  • Monitor profit and loss, and risk metrics, ensuring continuity during market holidays

- Production Monitoring:

  • Oversee systematic trading strategies, including restarting as needed
  • Respond to alerts and ensure the smooth operation of trading processes
  • Support internal teams with issue escalation and resolution

- Ad-hoc Trading & Operations Tasks:

  • Perform ad-hoc hedging and booking tasks as they arise
  • Support pilots and special initiatives
  • Respond to diverse requests from internal and external stakeholders


Additional oversight areas (as activity scales):

  • Monitor market-making activities and exposures, taking action in case of issues
  • Use and improve tools to run post-execution analysis and achieve best execution
  • Support trading of new products as they are launched (e.g., power and gas markets)
  • Oversee lifecycle management tasks, including monitoring of position rolls, option expirations, and corporate actions
  • Respond rapidly to risk limit breaches and serve as a contact point for market counterparties
  • Support near round-the-clock trading, including special coverage for weekend or holiday markets


Process Improvement:

  • Dedicate time to streamlining monitoring and booking workflows using scripting or automation
  • Engage with stakeholders to identify new requirements and extend team responsibilities

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative or related field
  • Programming proficiency in Python. KDB is a plus.
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills with the ability to collaborate with teammates globally
  • Strong sense of urgency with the ability to work well in a fast-paced environment

description: Working Hours: This role requires working on shifts to support 24/7 coverage across regions Team: Central Trading & Monitoring Squarepoint is a global investment management firm that utilizes a diversified portfolio of systematic and quantitative strategies across financial markets that seeks to achieve high quality, uncorrelated returns for our clients. We have deep expertise in trading, technology and operations and attribute our success to rigorous scientific research. As a technology and data-driven firm, we design and build our own cutting-edge systems, from high performance trading platforms to large scale data analysis and compute farms. With offices around the globe, we emphasize true, global collaboration by aligning our investment, technology, and operations teams functionally around the world. Role Overview Squarepoint is building a Central Trading & Monitoring team to ensure around-the-clock supervision of trading activities and operational processes. As a Shift Trader, you will play a critical role in risk management, trade oversight, operational lifecycle management, and incident response. You will also actively contribute to the automation and efficiency of the team’s workflow by developing tools and scripts to streamline monitoring and trading operations. Responsibilities Day-to-day remit: - Active Risk Monitoring: Monitor real-time exposures across Squarepoint’s trading activities Proactively raise alerts and escalate incidents as required Execute hedging actions following established procedures Monitor profit and loss, and risk metrics, ensuring continuity during market holidays - Production Monitoring: Oversee systematic trading strategies, including restarting as needed Respond to alerts and ensure the smooth operation of trading processes Support internal teams with issue escalation and resolution - Ad-hoc Trading & Operations Tasks: Perform ad-hoc hedging and booking tasks as they arise Support pilots and special initiatives Respond to diverse requests from internal and external stakeholders Additional oversight areas (as activity scales): Monitor market-making activities and exposures, taking action in case of issues Use and improve tools to run post-execution analysis and achieve best execution Support trading of new products as they are launched (e.g., power and gas markets) Oversee lifecycle management tasks, including monitoring of position rolls, option expirations, and corporate actions Respond rapidly to risk limit breaches and serve as a contact point for market counterparties Support near round-the-clock trading, including special coverage for weekend or holiday markets Process Improvement: Dedicate time to streamlining monitoring and booking workflows using scripting or automation Engage with stakeholders to identify new requirements and extend team responsibilities Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative or related field Programming proficiency in Python. KDB is a plus. Strong verbal and written communication skills with the ability to collaborate with teammates globally Strong sense of urgency with the ability to work well in a fast-paced environment

title: Curriculum Development and Teaching Intern
raw_description:
Academia is seeking enthusiastic Curriculum Development and Teaching Interns. This is an opportunity for undergraduates and post-JC students who are interested in teaching and education. Interns will work with the team to develop comprehensive and structured instructional materials, teaching and learning plans, and assessments to evaluate student progress. Additionally, interns will have opportunities to develop classroom management and teaching skills.

Academia's mission is to make learning intuitive in order to maximize educational outcomes for every student. We blend the best of the local education system with strong instructional scaffolding, research-based pedagogy, and elements from liberal-arts colleges and the humanities to empower our students to be smart, confident, and in control of their educational futures.

We offer competitive compensation and benefits, career development opportunities, and a supportive work environment where your creativity and passion for teaching will be valued and appreciated.

Job Description

  • Work with the curriculum team at Academia, to develop and update educational materials for students of varying levels
  • Assist teachers with their classes, including student management and differentiated instruction
  • Mark and review students’ work
  • Positions are available for Primary English, Secondary O-Level and IP English, and JC General Paper
  • Credit-based internship applications are welcome.

Profile and background

  • Strong proficiency in the English language is essential. English majors and undergraduates majoring in the humanities and social sciences are typically a good fit for this role, but we have had excellent interns from a range of different academic backgrounds.
  • Curriculum development will require the ability to work independently and synthesise content and research materials from various sources.
  • We do expect that candidates possess effective communication skills and a good work ethic.
  • We accept both full-time and part-time interns. Candidates who are able to work at least 1 weekend will be prioritised.
  • Interns should deploy creativity, meticulousness and rigor when developing educational content, in line with the high standards of the organisation

title: SUPERVISOR
raw_description:
The Site Supervisor oversees the day-to-day operations on construction sites, ensuring that the work is completed according to project specifications, on time, within budget, and in compliance with safety regulations. They manage construction teams, subcontractors, and suppliers while coordinating tasks and addressing issues as they arise. The Site Supervisor also ensures quality control and adheres to company policies and industry standards.

Site Management: Supervise all on-site activities, ensuring the construction process runs smoothly from start to finish.

Team Coordination: Manage, direct, and motivate construction workers, subcontractors, and other personnel to meet deadlines and work efficiently.

Safety Oversight: Ensure that all construction activities follow health and safety regulations and that the site is a safe working environment.

Quality Control: Monitor the quality of work, conduct inspections, and ensure compliance with project specifications and local building codes.

Resource Management: Oversee the delivery, storage, and usage of materials, tools, and equipment on-site, ensuring optimal usage.

Scheduling: Help develop and maintain construction schedules, ensuring projects stay on track and within deadlines.

Problem Solving: Address and resolve issues on-site, including delays, material shortages, or unforeseen challenges.

Communication: Serve as the primary point of contact for project managers, architects, engineers, subcontractors, and clients, ensuring effective communication and reporting.

Documentation: Maintain daily reports on project progress, work completed, and any issues encountered. Document any incidents or safety violations.

Compliance: Ensure all work is performed in compliance with legal and regulatory standards.

Skills and Abilities:

Strong leadership and supervisory skills to manage teams effectively.

Excellent knowledge of construction processes, materials, and safety standards.

Ability to read and interpret blueprints, plans, and technical drawings.

Strong problem-solving and decision-making capabilities.

Good organizational skills and attention to detail.

Proficiency with construction management software (e.g., Procore, Buildertrend) is often preferred.

Good communication skills to interact with various stakeholders.

Job Requirements

Education and Qualifications:

High school diploma or equivalent (required).

A degree or diploma in construction management, civil engineering, or a related field is preferred.

Certification in construction safety (e.g., OSHA) is often required.

title: Systems Engineer
raw_description:
Company Description

Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.

At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world. Join Visa and do work that matters — to you, to your community, and to the world.

Progress starts with you.

Job Description

The Workload Automation & Scheduling group is responsible for engineering and support of the IT infrastructure for Visa Inc. and VisaNet via Vendor Tools and Homegrown Utilities. The team is responsible for all technical aspects of the Workload Automation lifecycle from project initialization, system installation and maintenance, operational support to application and system decommissioning. This role is a basic SME individual contributor role responsible for identifying and resolving issues of moderate to high complexities to do with Visa’s Workload Automation Scheduling, VFT/MFT. This position is at a professional level and requires strong execution and analytical abilities to diagnose, mitigate and resolute small to highly complex events. This role works independently and receives minimal guidance providing out of the box solutioning, innovation and keeping up with the latest industry innovative workload automation processes.
Responsibilities:  

  • Support of Workload Automation Tools (BMC Control-M & Broadcom ESP)
  • Excellent Problem Determination skills to ensure mitigation time is kept to a minimum in meeting SLA and uptime expectations.
  • Basic understanding to write and integrate into WA tools scripts for automation in the following languages NodeJS, MVS JCL & REXX, HTTPs API, JSON, Python, HTML, XML, Shell and JavaScript.
  • Proactively work with Ops PRE, PD and or vendors to provide a resilient, flexible and fit for purpose solution around Workload Automation for Visa’s & M&A’s internal and external customer resolving issues and system problems.
  • Provide 24X7 production support of Visa operations. Requires on-call rotational support for geographically dispersed data centers.
  • Proactively work with leaders to implement improvement and help push the team to achieve better efficiency and advancing in technical knowledge in the Workload Automation area.
  • Ensure that all server deployment follow Visa security guidelines and report any non-compliance for the Workload Automation Platform.
  • Communicate and work cordially in global team with teammate from different culture and in time zone.
  • Manage workload and escalate to appropriate Engineers and manager, as needed.
  • Work with other support, development, and user groups to manage requests from the perspective of automation and BRP failover design and functionality.
  • Work with vendors on software issues and problems as needed.
  • Basic use of documentation tools, such as Microsoft Project, Microsoft Excel, and Microsoft Power Point.
  • Ability to work with management and users from all levels of knowledge and expertise.
  • Act as Coordinator for centralize change and managing the request and coordinating the work between various internal Ops, PRE & PD groups
  • Act as Change Coordinator to Process, Assign and Report Enterprise tooling changes assigned to Gsched & Workload Automation

This is a hybrid position. Expectation of days in office will be confirmed by your hiring manager.

Qualifications

Basic Qualifications:

  • 2 - 5 years of work experience with a Bachelor’s Degree or at least 2 years of work experience with an Advanced degree (e.g. Masters, MBA, JD, MD) or 0 years of work experience with a PhD degree
  • 2+ years of Unix/Linux experience


Preferred Qualifications:

  • Project Management Skills is a must
  • Basic working knowledge of Linux, Windows, Mainframe & Tandem operating systems (Need to have prior experience on all Visa Operating Systems)
  • Basic working knowledge of Workload Automation Tools (Control-M & ESP) (Need have prior scheduling experience with Visa’s Workload Automation Tools)
  • Basic Ability to write scripts for automation. Windows/Unix/NodeJS/MVS JCL & REXX/API/JSON, Python, HTML, XML, Shell and JavaScript. (Ability to code simple scripts to automate workload)
  • Workload Automation Scheduling experience is required
  • Ability to solicit Workload Automation options to end users
  • Strong problem determination skills.
  • Basic working knowledge of Databases, Java, JavaScript & Hadoop. (Basic DB queries, Ability to review and understand Java, JavaScript & Hadoop on Scheduling Definitions)
  • Basic knowledge of documentation tools, such as Microsoft Project, Microsoft Excel, and Microsoft Power Point.
  • Ability to work with management and users from all levels of knowledge and expertise.
  • Ability to work independently.
  • Basic – Advanced systems engineering or programming experience.
  • Team player with strong communication skills.

title: Data Vizualization Engineer
raw_description:
About GlobalFoundries

GlobalFoundries is a leading full-service semiconductor foundry providing a unique combination of design, development, and fabrication services to some of the world’s most inspired technology companies. With a global manufacturing footprint spanning three continents, GlobalFoundries makes possible the technologies and systems that transform industries and give customers the power to shape their markets. For more information, visit www.gf.com.

Introduction:

As a Visualization Engineer, you will serve a crucial function in our Digital Manufacturing initiatives. Your primary mission will be to develop and deploy interactive data visualizations and digital applications that directly optimize and improve our manufacturing processes. You will be key to turning complex data into the actionable insights required to drive our digital transformation success.

Your Job:

  • Visualization Design & Development: Design, develop, and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau) to provide key business insights and support data-driven decision-making.
  • Data Analysis & Insight Generation: Perform statistical analysis and data plotting to solve complex business problems, derive actionable insights, and create clear correlation models for functional teams.
  • Project & Stakeholder Management: Manage the full life cycle of data visualization projects, from requirements gathering with functional teams to final delivery, serving as the primary liaison between business stakeholders and IT/Engineering teams.
  • Data Sourcing & Modeling: Execute data discovery and cataloging to efficiently locate and understand internal data sources. Apply foundational knowledge of data models and schemas to perform source-to-target mapping, ensuring accurate, structured data for reporting.
  • Data Pipeline Collaboration: Work closely with data engineering teams, utilizing familiarity with AWS services and exposure to PySpark to understand and collaborate on the transformation scripts and data pipelines that process large volumes of manufacturing data.
  • Cross-Functional Teamwork: Work closely with the Analytics Center of Excellence Manager and other stakeholders to define project objectives, deliverables, and timelines.

Required Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in a computer science, information technology, or related fields
  • 2 years of experience and knowledge in data visualization with a strong understanding of both front-end and back-end technologies.
  • Proficiency in data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Looker).
  • Familiarity with AWS cloud environment and data transformation tools such as PySpark.
  • Proficiency in front-end and back-end technologies.
  • Experience with API development and integration.
  • Experience with version control systems and familiarity with Agile development methodologies.
  • Excellent communication and collaboration skills.
  • Ability work independently and as part of the cross-functional team.
  • Strong problem-solving and analytical skills with a passion for building high-quality digital applications.

GlobalFoundries is an equal opportunity employer, cultivating a diverse and inclusive workforce. We believe having a multicultural workplace enhances productivity, efficiency and innovation whilst our employees feel truly respected, valued and heard.

As an affirmative employer, all qualified applicants are considered for employment regardless of age, ethnicity, marital status, citizenship, race, religion, political affiliation, gender, sexual orientation and medical and/or physical abilities.

All offers of employment with GlobalFoundries are conditioned upon the successful completion of background checks, medical screenings as applicable and subject to the respective local laws and regulations.

title: Property Loan Relationship Manager (Banking & Finance)
raw_description:
Property Loan Relationship Manager (Banking & Finance)

📍 Ubi | 💰 Basic $4,000 – $6,000 + Commission | 🏦 Property & Secured Financing

About the Role

Tembusu Financial Services is a fast-growing Singapore finance firm specialising in property-backed and secured financing solutions for individuals and businesses.

We are looking for a Property Loan Relationship Manager to build long-term client relationships, structure property-based financing solutions, and grow a sustainable loan portfolio.
This role is ideal for candidates from banking, mortgage sales, relationship management, or those keen to build a professional finance career with strong earning potential.

What You Will Do

  • Build and manage relationships with clients seeking property loans and secured financing
  • Understand client needs and recommend suitable property-backed financing solutions
  • Source and engage clients through referrals, outreach, and provided leads
  • Prepare basic financing proposals and coordinate documentation (full training provided – no prior structuring experience required)
  • Work closely with internal credit and operations teams to ensure smooth execution
  • Grow and manage your own client portfolio over time

Who We Are Looking For

  • Strong interest in banking, finance, or property financing
  • Confident communicator who enjoys relationship-building and client engagement
  • Sales-driven, disciplined, and motivated by performance-based rewards
  • Prior experience in banking, mortgage sales, insurance, real estate, or finance is an advantage (but not mandatory)
  • Fresh graduates and career switchers with the right attitude are welcome

What We Offer

💰 Attractive Compensation

  • Basic salary: $4,000 – $6,000
  • Uncapped commission structure
  • High performers regularly earn $8,000 – $12,000+ per month

📈 Career Progression

  • Clear pathway: Relationship Manager → Senior RM → Team Lead / Management
  • Hands-on mentorship and structured on-the-job training
  • Exposure to real-world property financing and credit evaluation

🤝 Supportive Environment

  • Professional yet entrepreneurial culture
  • No cold-call scripts — focus on relationship quality
  • Strong operational and credit support so you can focus on clients

Why Join Tembusu Financial Services

  • Specialise in property-backed financing, not generic consumer sales
  • Learn skills transferable across banking, private credit, and finance roles
  • Fast-growing firm with opportunities to grow with the business
  • Performance and results are recognised and rewarded

raw_title: Principal/Sr Engineer, Advance Packaging Process Engineering
title: Principal/Sr Engineer, Advance Packaging Process Engineering
raw_description:
Micron Technology is a world leader in innovating memory and storage solutions that accelerate the transformation of information into intelligence, inspiring the world to learn, communicate and advance faster than ever.

Micron's Assembly Package Technology Development team is looking for a motivated and experienced individual contributor for this position in Process Development team.

Roles & Responsibilities:

  • Developing, configuring and optimizing Package Assembly processes (front end, bonding and back end) for Micron's memory from pathfinding through to NPI start up and certification side by side with Manufacturing teams.

  • Assessing processes by knowledge and skills, taking measurements and interpreting data

  • Assembly design rules development working side by side with Design teams (Globally)

  • Involved in the design approvals (Substrate and Silicon), running, testing and upgrading systems and processes, approving the materials, process and equipment changes in the CCB (change control board).

  • Path finding of innovative package technology: Deep fundamental understanding of the key risks in Bumping, Flip chip and HBM technologies. Actual experience with TCB (Thermal Compression Bonding), CMP (Chemical Mechanical Polish), WSS (Wafer Support System), photo process and Ball Mount is a plus.

  • Hands-on integration experience in Package Assembly and Wafer Level package process are needed, may need to conduct the supplier interactions/meetings and drive new NDAs with new projects, which all together requires a significant understanding of the processes, first principles and process control systems. 

Requirements

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Materials Science, Physics, or related field.

  • Minimum of 3 years of experience in semiconductor manufacturing or related fields.

  • Proven track record in yield management and driving yield improvement initiatives.

  • Flexibility to support operation including shift -based work and periodic weekend coverage based on critical business needs.

  • Deep understanding of semiconductor manufacturing processes, yield analysis, and statistical process control (SPC).

  • Hands-on experience with data analysis tools such as JMP, or equivalent.

  • Strong communication skills, and able to present complex technical data and trends to leadership and non-technical audiences.

  • Proven ability to work with multi-functional teams and influence partners to achieve strategic goals.

  • Problem-Solving Skills: Strong analytical skills with the ability to troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues in a high-pressure environment.

  • Project Management: Ability to manage numerous projects simultaneously and prioritize tasks in a fast-paced environment.

  • Cross-Cultural Communication: Experience working with teams across different geographies and cultures.

  • Possess Micron Values: People, Innovation, Tenacity, Collaboration and Customer Focus

    Performs hardware, software, semiconductor design or telecomm engineering assignments. This job mapping should be used only if the position cannot be mapped specifically to other design/development engineering jobs.

title: Fresh Graduates - Bank Operations
raw_description:
This is a back-office operations role in a bank’s Card Operations Processing Centre, specifically within the Reconciliation and Investigation Unit. Your main responsibility is to ensure that the bank’s financial records match actual money movement, particularly for credit card transactions.

Responsibilities:
- Daily reconciliation/proofing of General Ledger (GL) and Nostro (Cash accounts) on transactions for Cards Operations Processing Centre
- Escalation of outstanding items for the day, and age items for both Nostro and General Ledger (GL)
- Invoice reconciliation"


(A) Key Duties Explained:
- Daily Reconciliation of Accounts: You will compare and match bank records (General Ledger) with actual cash balances (Nostro accounts) to ensure there are no discrepancies. Think of this as daily “balancing the books” — making sure the bank’s internal system reflects the real-world financial activity.
- Escalating Outstanding Items: If any mismatch or error is found (e.g., a payment didn’t go through or an amount doesn’t match), you will flag or escalate it to the relevant team for investigation and resolution.
- Aging of Items: You'll monitor unresolved discrepancies over time. “Aging” refers to tracking how long items remain unmatched and ensuring they are not left unresolved for too long.
- Invoice Reconciliation: You will help match invoices received (e.g., from vendors or third-party processors) against the bank’s records, ensuring correctness before payment or settlement.


(B) Skills You’ll Use:
- Attention to detail (to spot mismatches), Familiarity with Excel or reconciliation tools, Ability to follow up and escalate when issues arise, Time management (to complete daily checks promptly)
- Who is This Job Suitable For: Fresh graduates in banking, finance, or business, or Candidates with 1–2 years of back-office banking experience, Someone who prefers structured, process-oriented work rather than customer-facing roles

title: Trade Support Analyst (Operations) – 6-Month Contract
raw_description:
Job Description

We are seeking a Trade Support Analyst for a 6-month contract to support the Operations function of a leading global financial institution. This role works closely with Sales, Trading desks, and internal stakeholders to ensure accurate trade processing, effective operational risk control, and continuous process improvement across Structured and Flow Credit derivatives.

The ideal candidate is proactive, detail-oriented, and thrives in a fast-paced trading environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Partner with internal clients, Sales & Trading desks, and stakeholders to resolve trade-related queries and issues

  • Monitor and control operational risk throughout the post-trade lifecycle for Structured and Flow Credit derivatives

  • Act as a trade support subject matter expert to mitigate risk and drive operational efficiencies

  • Manage real-time trade booking exceptions, coordinating closely with Trading and Sales teams

  • Collaborate with Technology and Business partners to enhance systems, workflows, and controls

  • Participate in industry and regulatory initiatives in a constantly evolving market environment

  • Continuously review and improve operational processes to ensure strong risk management

  • Deliver results that meet expectations for timeliness, accuracy, and cost effectiveness

Requirements

Basic Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree (BA or BS required)

  • Proactive, enthusiastic, and detail-oriented with strong ownership mindset

  • Ability to prioritise and multitask effectively in a fast-paced environment

  • Strong collaboration and communication skills, with the ability to engage diverse stakeholders

  • Effective problem-solving and critical thinking skills, with sound judgment and discretion

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office, especially Excel, with ability to learn new systems quickly

Preferred Qualifications

  • 2–5 years of experience in Trade Support, Operations, or Financial Markets

  • Prior exposure to trading desk support or post-trade operations

  • Understanding of credit derivatives and relevant regulatory environments is an advantage

  • Ability to remain calm and effective under pressure

  • Experience in a global financial institution or institutional trading environment

    All Curriculum Vitaes will be treated with the strictest confidentiality. By sending us your personal data, you are deemed to consent to RMA Consultants Pte Ltd and its affiliates collecting, using and disclosing your personal data for the purposes set out in our Data Protection Policy at http://bit.ly/3GHgskn and Privacy Policy at http://bit.ly/3W63rp7.

    "This is in partnership with the Employment and Employability Institute Pte Ltd (“e2i”).e2i is the empowering network for workers and employers seeking employment and employability solutions.e2i serves as a bridge between workers and employers, connecting with workers to offer job security through job-matching, career guidance and skills upgrading services, and partnering employers to address their manpower needs through recruitment, training, and job redesign solutions. e2i is a tripartite initiative of the National Trades Union Congress set up to support nation-wide manpower and skills upgrading initiatives. By applying for this role, you consent to RMA Groups’s PDPA and e2i’s PDPA."

    We regret to inform that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

    Syed Hafiz

    EA Regn No: R21103431

    RMA Consultants EA License No: 93C4403
