raw_description:
Accountant – AR & AP

5 days, Monday to Friday: 8:30am to 5:30pm
$5800 – $6800
Clementi

The Accountant – AR & AP is responsible for managing the company’s accounts payable and accounts receivable functions, ensuring accurate financial records, timely payments, and efficient collection of receivables, and compliance with company policies. This role focuses on reporting, analysis, controls, and process improvement rather than transactional processing.

Responsibilities:
AR & AP Oversight and Review:

  • Review AR, AP and Banking transactions including GST and tax compliance to ensure accuracy and adherence to accounting policies
  • Monitor payment and collection cycles to ensure timeliness and effectiveness
  • Identify errors, irregularities, and control gaps, and recommend corrective actions
  • Ensure proper approval workflows, framework and documentation are in place
  • Liaise with sales and clients to resolve invoicing and credit collection issues promptly.
  • Liaising with procurement and suppliers to resolve invoicing and payment issues promptly.

Planning & Analysis:

  • Plan and manage AR and AP cash flow cycles to support business operations
  • Plan and manage banking facilities and banker’s guarantees
  • Prepare and review AR and AP aging reports and key performance metrics
  • Support banking activities, invoice financing, LC, cash flow forecasting, and working capital management

Controls & Compliance, Process Improvement & Coordination:

  • Maintain and enhance internal controls over AR and AP processes
  • Ensure compliance with accounting standards, company policies, and audit requirements
  • Drive continuous improvement initiatives for AR and AP processes
  • Partner with procurement, sales, and operations teams to resolve issues
  • Support system enhancements and automation initiatives

Month-End & Reporting:

  • Review AR and AP balances as part of month-end and year-end close
  • Prepare AR and AP related journal as part of month-end and year-end close
  • Prepare and review account reconciliations
  • Provide AR/AP aging reports to management
  • Coordinate with external auditors on AR and AP matters

Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field
  • 3–5 years of experience in AR/AP oversight, analysis, or accounting review roles
  • Strong understanding of AR/AP processes and internal controls
  • Experience with ERP systems and advanced Excel skills
  • Experience in audit support
  • Experience in GST and WHT compliance
  • Familiarity with Trust receipts/Invoice Financing process and LC negotiation

title: Management Trainee
raw_description:
The journey: 

Nurturing future leaders is pivotal to our success. Our Regional Management Trainee (RMT) Program provides an accelerated journey for graduates to develop to their fullest potential and become our business leaders in the near future.  

​As an RMT, you will be participating in an intensive 18-month program where you will be exposed to new business environments. We'll give you real professional experience and the chance to apply your knowledge to real business challenges through job rotations, blended learning activities, management mentoring, corporate training and special projects. You'll also get guidance and coaching from an experienced mentor.  There will also be many opportunities to connect with new faces, including senior leaders, RMT alumni and fellow trainees. 

​Successful graduates of the RMT program will be offered a landing role in the company beginning their journey to becoming Managers or Senior Managers within 2 to 5 years.​ 

Functions that you will be exposed to: 

Operations – we are operations centric and thrive on thrive on delivering results. You get hands-on exposure on the warehouse floor understanding how we design solutions, implement and execute them for our global retail clients. Get behind the scenes during major online campaigns like 11.11. We guarantee a roller coaster ride.  

Who are we looking for?  

  • Bachelor’s or master’s degree (any discipline) with a good GPA 

  • Fresh graduates or have a maximum of 2-years working experience 

  • Strong analytical and problem solving skills, drive to succeed, and proactive  

  • Passionate about the Supply Chain industry 

  • Good spoken and written English  

Want to be part of our exciting growth journey? Join us as a Management Trainee! You’ll play a vital part in our success, developing, designing, and implementing innovative supply chain solutions, which bring visibility, reliability and accountability to our customer’s supply chains.

title: Marketing Operation Specialist
raw_description:
About the Role

We are looking for a highly organized regional Marketing Operation Specialist for our Global Marketing Team. In this role, you will support marketing and business initiatives across multiple markets with a focus on project coordination, campaign execution, partner and vendor collaboration, and operational support. This is a position that works cross-functionally to ensure smooth delivery of marketing programs, events, and strategic initiatives, while contributing to research, reporting, and process improvement efforts.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support the planning and execution of marketing and business initiatives across regional markets.
  • Coordinate with internal teams and external partners to deliver campaigns, events, and partnership programs.
  • Manage project timelines, deliverables, and cross-market stakeholder communications.
  • Assist in campaign rollout, content coordination, and promotional activities across regional markets.
  • Prepare reports, presentations, and internal documentation to support planning and decision-making.
  • Conduct market and competitor research to support regional strategy and program development.
  • Liaise with vendors and partners on marketing materials and related deliverables.
  • Support operational processes related to regional marketing projects and cross-team initiatives.

Qualifications & Requirements

  • Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, or related fields.
  • 0-2 years of working experience in an office environment, including internship experience. Fresh graduates are highly encouraged to apply.
  • A high degree of "teachability"—someone with excellent learning and comprehension abilities with a strong sense of responsibility and attention to detail.
  • Good communication, stakeholder management, and project coordination skills.
  • Fluency in English and Chinese (both written and spoken) is required to communicate effectively with our regional internal teams and external partners.

title: Junior Digital Marketing Executive (SEO & PPC)
raw_description:
Job Summary

We are looking for a highly motivated and enthusiastic Junior Digital Marketing Executive with 2-3 years of hands-on experience in Search Engine Optimization (SEO) and Search Engine Marketing (SEM). Based in our Singapore regional hub, this role will be instrumental in driving our digital presence and lead generation efforts across various APAC markets. You'll work closely with the regional marketing team and local country teams, and will also report directly to the Global In-Charge of SEO/SEM. This ensures alignment with global digital strategies and provides opportunities for advanced learning and collaboration. A key aspect of this role will be staying abreast of the rapidly evolving landscape of AI in SEO and adapting strategies accordingly.

Key Responsibilities

SEO Management

· Conduct keyword research to identify new opportunities and optimize existing content for relevant search terms in APAC markets.

· Perform on-page SEO optimization, including meta descriptions, title tags, header tags, and content optimization.

· Assist with technical SEO audits and work with web development teams to implement recommendations (e.g., site speed, sitemaps, robots.txt).

· Monitor and report on SEO performance using tools like Econda Analytics and Google Search Console.

· Continuously research and keep up-to-date with the latest AI trends and their impact on SEO, including AI Overviews, generative AI in search, and answer engine optimization (AEO).

· Stay up-to-date with general SEO trends, algorithm changes, and best practices.


SEM Campaign Management

· Assist in the planning of paid search campaigns on platforms like Google Ads, Microsoft Bing, and Baidu/Naver/Yahoo! Japan (depending on markets).

· Work closely with and manage local country agencies responsible for the execution and optimization of these paid search campaigns.

· Provide guidance and support to agencies on keyword research, ad copy development, and targeting strategies.

· Monitor agency performance, analyze campaign data, and provide recommendations for bid adjustments, budget allocation, and overall strategy improvements.


Content & Website Support

· Support website updates and ensure content is optimized for search engines.

· Assist in maintaining website health and user experience.


Reporting & Analysis

· Track, analyze, and report on key digital marketing metrics, including orders, revenue, and Return on Ad Spend (ROAS), across various platforms.

· Provide insights and recommendations for optimizing digital marketing performance.

Regional & Global Collaboration

· Work closely with regional and local marketing teams to ensure digital strategies align with overall business objectives.

· Collaborate with and report to the Global In-Charge of SEO/SEM to align on global strategies, share best practices, and contribute to international initiatives, particularly regarding AI's influence on search.

· Support digital marketing initiatives for specific APAC countries.


Qualifications

· Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field.

· 2-3 years of proven experience in digital marketing with a strong focus on SEO and SEM.

· Hands-on experience with Econda Analytics, Google Search Console, Google Ads, Microsoft Bing, Baidu, Naver, and Yahoo! Japan is essential.

· Familiarity with SEO tools (e.g., SEMrush, Ahrefs, Moz) is a strong plus.

· Understanding of digital marketing principles and best practices.

· Demonstrated interest in and willingness to learn about the impact of Artificial Intelligence on search engine optimization and marketing.

· Excellent analytical skills and ability to interpret data to drive actionable insights.

· Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across teams and cultures.

· Highly organized, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple projects simultaneously.

· Proactive, self-motivated, and eager to learn and grow in a fast-paced environment.

· Experience in a B2B environment or the manufacturing/industrial sector is a plus.

· Strong proficiency in English is required. Additional proficiency in APAC languages will be an advantage, as the role involves supporting regional markets.



Working hours:

Mon to Fri 9am - 6pm

title: Applied AI Associate - Ocean Transportation
raw_description:
Job Purpose and Impact

The Applied AI Associate - Ocean Transportation supports the design, development, and deployment of AI, analytics, and digital solutions that enhance commercial and operational decision-making. The role offers direct exposure to real shipping data, trading workflows, and innovative digital initiatives. It is well suited for individuals who want to apply AI and analytics in a global maritime and freight trading environment.

Key Accountabilities

AI, Analytics & Digital Development

  • Apply AI models to analyse operational, market, and voyage datasets and convert findings into actionable insights.
  • Train, refine, and evaluate AI models using domain-specific datasets, including contract clauses, voyage PnL, and market intelligence.
  • Monitor model accuracy, drift, and behaviour across vessel segments (Drybulk and Tankers).
  • Contribute to the development of digital products such as generative AI copilots, predictive models, optimisation engines, and simulation tools.
  • Build and improve analytical pipelines, dashboards, and cloud-native data solutions to support scalable AI deployment.
  • Support business teams with experimentation, prototyping, and validation of AI and data science outputs.

Rotations & Business Exposure

  • Work closely with Freight Trading, Operations, and other business functions.
  • Develop a strong understanding of commercial decision-making, operational workflows, and how data creates value across the freight value chain.
  • Translate business needs into analytical problems and collaborate with users to test and refine solutions.

Collaboration & Communication

  • Present analytical insights and model outputs to non-technical stakeholders in a clear, structured manner.
  • Participate in problem-solving sessions, innovation discussions, and digital strategy workshops.
  • Contribute new ideas in applied AI, analytics, and maritime digital innovation.

Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelors/Masters degree in Maritime Studies, Data Analytics, Data Science, or a related field, graduating in the year 2026 or recently.
  • Understanding of statistics, predictive analytics, or machine learning concepts.
  • Experience with Python, SQL, and dashboarding tools.
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
  • Interest in global trade, maritime logistics, digital products, and applied AI.
  • Self-motivated, proactive, and eager to learn in a fast-paced environment.
  • Other minimum qualifications may apply

Preferred Qualifications

  • Exposure to forecasting, optimisation, or AI-related projects.
  • Familiarity with generative AI or agentic AI concepts.
  • Interest in trading analytics, vessel performance, or sustainability topics.

raw_title: #HOT# Junior Application Analyst (L2–L3 Production Support) - 12 Months
title: #HOT# Junior Application Analyst (L2–L3 Production Support) - 12 Months
raw_description:
#HOT# Junior Application Analyst (L2–L3 Production Support) - 12 Months

Contract Type: 12-month contract
Work Schedule: Monday to Friday, with occasional weekend support (9AM–6 PM). Flexible work-from-home arrangements and time off in lieu available for weekend & PH holiday.

We are seeking a self-driven and motivated Junior Application Analyst to join our team in supporting and enhancing business-critical derivatives trading systems and shared data platforms. This role is ideal for candidates who enjoy hands-on technical work and want to leverage their experience in Python / Java (advantageous), observability tools, and cloud-based applications in a production-focused environment.

Key Responsibilities

  • Provide Level 2 / Level 3 production support for derivatives trading applications, ensuring high availability, stability, and performance.

  • Investigate and resolve application issues, bugs, and performance bottlenecks in a time-critical trading environment.

  • Apply AI-enabled tools (e.g., anomaly detection, predictive analytics, intelligent monitoring) to proactively identify and mitigate incidents.

  • Perform application recovery, verification, and monitoring, including off-office hours and weekend support as required.

  • Conduct non-functional testing, identifying breakpoints, security vulnerabilities, and application anomalies.

  • Monitor application health and performance using appropriate tools and observability platforms.

  • Automate routine production support tasks using scripting (Python, PowerShell) and AI-enhanced solutions.

  • Collaborate with engineering, infrastructure, and business teams to implement fixes and system improvements.

  • Maintain accurate documentation for support procedures, known issues, and resolutions.

  • Ensure compliance with internal controls and SOPs, including vulnerability remediation, audit evidence extraction, and system hygiene.

Essential Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent practical experience.

  • 3–5 years of experience in software development, system analysis, or application support roles (profiles with 6+ years may be considered overqualified).

  • Proficiency in Java and/or scripting languages (SQL, Python, Shell).

  • Hands-on experience supporting applications on Linux and Windows platforms.

  • Strong verbal and written English communication skills.

  • Production-first mindset with the ability to operate under pressure in a trading environment.

  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a team.

Advantageous Skills

Knowledge of derivatives products and trading operations (e.g., FX, Rates, Options, Futures).

Experience with CI/CD pipelines and DevOps practices.

Please reach out to co.co@ambition.com.sg  with your updated resume and contact details. Incomplete applications may not be considered /Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

title: Business Analyst - 12 Months Contract - SGD4.8k
raw_description:
We’re hiring a Business Analyst on behalf of our client, a global leader in technology consulting. You’ll collaborate with senior consultants and cross‑functional teams to translate business needs into actionable solutions, design workflows and functional requirements, and deliver data‑driven insights that shape strategy. 

Key Responsibilities:

  • Work alongside senior consultants to gather and evaluate client requirements, turning business needs into practical technology solutions.
  • Contribute to the design and development of solutions by preparing technical specifications, workflows, and functional requirements.  
  • Collect, analyze, and interpret data to generate insights that guide strategic decisions.
  • Support the preparation of project documentation, including plans, progress reports, and client communications.
  • Assist in leading workshops and presentations, ensuring technical concepts are clearly conveyed to stakeholders.
  • Take part in team discussions and brainstorming sessions to explore and refine solution ideas.


Requirements: 

  • Bachelor’s degree in IT or a related field.
  • Strong understanding of technical requirements at code level, with basic debugging skills. Familiarity with Java, JavaScript, SQL, and AWS Cloud.
  • Proficient in writing clear, concise technical and functional documentation.  
  • Able to collaborate with diverse stakeholders to identify solutions and piece together requirements into holistic outcomes.  
  • Experienced in legacy system migration projects and served as the primary PIC for change requests (CRs). 
  • Strong ability to break down complex problems, develop solutions and responses based on information gathered.  
  • Ability to identify areas for improvement and propose effective solutions.  
  • Basic understanding of the Software Development Life Cycle.

title: 6723 - Accountant [ERP System | Exp in Audit Support | Clementi]
raw_description:
Accountant – AR & AP

5 days, Monday to Friday: 8:30am to 5:30pm
$5800 – $6800
Clementi

The Accountant – AR & AP is responsible for managing the company’s accounts payable and accounts receivable functions, ensuring accurate financial records, timely payments, and efficient collection of receivables, and compliance with company policies. This role focuses on reporting, analysis, controls, and process improvement rather than transactional processing.

Responsibilities:
AR & AP Oversight and Review:

  • Review AR, AP and Banking transactions including GST and tax compliance to ensure accuracy and adherence to accounting policies
  • Monitor payment and collection cycles to ensure timeliness and effectiveness
  • Identify errors, irregularities, and control gaps, and recommend corrective actions
  • Ensure proper approval workflows, framework and documentation are in place
  • Liaise with sales and clients to resolve invoicing and credit collection issues promptly.
  • Liaising with procurement and suppliers to resolve invoicing and payment issues promptly.

Planning & Analysis:

  • Plan and manage AR and AP cash flow cycles to support business operations
  • Plan and manage banking facilities and banker’s guarantees
  • Prepare and review AR and AP aging reports and key performance metrics
  • Support banking activities, invoice financing, LC, cash flow forecasting, and working capital management

Controls & Compliance, Process Improvement & Coordination:

  • Maintain and enhance internal controls over AR and AP processes
  • Ensure compliance with accounting standards, company policies, and audit requirements
  • Drive continuous improvement initiatives for AR and AP processes
  • Partner with procurement, sales, and operations teams to resolve issues
  • Support system enhancements and automation initiatives

Month-End & Reporting:

  • Review AR and AP balances as part of month-end and year-end close
  • Prepare AR and AP related journal as part of month-end and year-end close
  • Prepare and review account reconciliations
  • Provide AR/AP aging reports to management
  • Coordinate with external auditors on AR and AP matters

Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field
  • 3–5 years of experience in AR/AP oversight, analysis, or accounting review roles
  • Strong understanding of AR/AP processes and internal controls
  • Experience with ERP systems and advanced Excel skills
  • Experience in audit support
  • Experience in GST and WHT compliance
  • Familiarity with Trust receipts/Invoice Financing process and LC negotiation


📲Interested candidates, please WhatsApp your resume to: +65 9642 0989 (Han) 📧 Or email your resume to: supreme.cc.han@gmail.com

raw_description:
Accountant – AR & AP

5 days, Monday to Friday: 8:30am to 5:30pm
$5800 – $6800
Clementi

The Accountant – AR & AP is responsible for managing the company’s accounts payable and accounts receivable functions, ensuring accurate financial records, timely payments, and efficient collection of receivables, and compliance with company policies. This role focuses on reporting, analysis, controls, and process improvement rather than transactional processing.

Responsibilities:
AR & AP Oversight and Review:

  • Review AR, AP and Banking transactions including GST and tax compliance to ensure accuracy and adherence to accounting policies
  • Monitor payment and collection cycles to ensure timeliness and effectiveness
  • Identify errors, irregularities, and control gaps, and recommend corrective actions
  • Ensure proper approval workflows, framework and documentation are in place
  • Liaise with sales and clients to resolve invoicing and credit collection issues promptly.
  • Liaising with procurement and suppliers to resolve invoicing and payment issues promptly.

Planning & Analysis:

  • Plan and manage AR and AP cash flow cycles to support business operations
  • Plan and manage banking facilities and banker’s guarantees
  • Prepare and review AR and AP aging reports and key performance metrics
  • Support banking activities, invoice financing, LC, cash flow forecasting, and working capital management

Controls & Compliance, Process Improvement & Coordination:

  • Maintain and enhance internal controls over AR and AP processes
  • Ensure compliance with accounting standards, company policies, and audit requirements
  • Drive continuous improvement initiatives for AR and AP processes
  • Partner with procurement, sales, and operations teams to resolve issues
  • Support system enhancements and automation initiatives

Month-End & Reporting:

  • Review AR and AP balances as part of month-end and year-end close
  • Prepare AR and AP related journal as part of month-end and year-end close
  • Prepare and review account reconciliations
  • Provide AR/AP aging reports to management
  • Coordinate with external auditors on AR and AP matters

Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field
  • 3–5 years of experience in AR/AP oversight, analysis, or accounting review roles
  • Strong understanding of AR/AP processes and internal controls
  • Experience with ERP systems and advanced Excel skills
  • Experience in audit support
  • Experience in GST and WHT compliance
  • Familiarity with Trust receipts/Invoice Financing process and LC negotiation

title: Executive Search Consultant (Entry-Level)
raw_description:
About the Role

We believe great careers are built through strong mentorship, structured learning, and long-term growth.

As an Executive Search Consultant, you’ll gain hands-on exposure to the full recruitment lifecycle while working closely with organizations to shape impactful hiring decisions. Beyond traditional recruitment, you’llprogressively take on client-facing responsibilities, sharpen your consulting and communication skills, and develop a deep understanding of how businesses build teams for success.

We welcome graduates and early-career professionals who are curious, driven, and ready to grow a sustainable career in recruitment and consulting.

Key Responsibilities

  • Lead end-to-end recruitment across various industries such as Financial Services, Consumer & Retail, Technology, Engineering, and Legal & Compliance, positioning talent solutions to meet market needs.
  • Execute targeted sourcing strategies and first-round interviews to identify candidates aligned with client branding and role requirements.
  • Drive business growth & development through strategic client acquisition, account marketing, and long-term partnership building.
  • Manage negotiations and close recruitment cases by aligning candidate value propositions with client hiring objectives.

Who we are looking for

  • Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Business, Arts & Social Sciences, or Humanities.
  • No experience needed, mentorship & training provided.
  • Genuinely curious about people, careers, and how companies grow.
  • Results-driven, organized, and self-motivated to hit goals.

Interested applicants, please send your latest resume to:
Ms Jacqueline Ng Shin Yee
HR Director (R1104502)
Jacqueline.ng@searchasia.com.sg

We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

Connect with us on LinkedIn!
https://www.linkedin.com/company/searchasiaconsulting/

title: Maintenance and Project Executive
raw_description:
The Maintenance and Project Executive will play a vital frontline role in ensuring the reliability, safety, and performance of key defence infrastructure across the island.

This is a hands-on role for individuals who enjoy problem-solving, teamwork, and making a real impact through their technical expertise.

What You’ll Do

  • Be part of a dedicated maintenance team providing scheduled and corrective maintenance for critical defence systems

  • Act as the first responder during equipment faults, attending on-site activations to restore system functionality

  • Support 24/7 operations through an on-call rotation (work outside office hours and on weekends will be compensated with time-off)

  • Handle procurement-related administrative tasks as part of project maintenance

  • Assist the Project Manager with project coordination, administration, and work scope execution


What We’re Looking For

  • Minimally ITE graduates in Engineering or Information & Digital Technologies. Other candidates with relevant technical skills and experience may also apply.

  • Willingness to provide 24/7 on-call support and work effectively in a team-based environment

  • Valid Class 3 driver’s license with at least 2 years of driving experience

  • Comfortable working at heights and in industrial environments

  • Reliable team player with a proactive and responsible mindset


At Surbana Jurong, we put talent, hard work, teamwork and a fun workplace together to approach problems and solve them creatively and collaboratively. Join us in making a positive impact on the world!

The candidate will play a leading role in engaging prospects and maintaining customer relationship, and given an opportunity to travel overseas, as well as making an impact on corporate and product marketing.

Responsibilities

  • Customer relationship management – build and maintain business relationships with semiconductor customers in Singapore.
  • Sales activities – responsible for the business contact, product promotion, order processing, and communication with customers in Singapore.

title: Marketing Executive
raw_description:
Marketing Executive

The Marketing Executive supports the planning and execution of marketing initiatives to build brand awareness, generate leads, and support sales growth. This role works closely with internal teams and external partners to deliver campaigns across digital and offline channels.

Key Responsibilities :

Campaign Execution

  • Plan and execute marketing campaigns across digital, social media, email, and offline channels
  • Coordinate and support marketing activities (seminars, exhibitions, roadshows, etc.,) on logistics, booth setup, and promotional materials
  • Manage and execute email marketing calendar and ensure alignment with business priorities

Digital and Social Media Marketing

  • Implement digital marketing activities (content planning, posting, engagement) across a broad range of digital marketing channels (Social Media [Linkedin, Facebook, Instagram, etc.], mass mailing, SEO, Google Ads, etc).
  • Manage, maintain and update the company’s website

Content & Communications

  • Prepare marketing content such as brochures, EDMs, social posts, and presentations with use of AI tools to draft, optimize, and personalize marketing content
  • Ensure brand consistency across all communications

Marketing & Performance Analysis

  • Use AI and analytics tools to track campaign performance
  • Prepare regular marketing reports

Sales Support

  • Work closely with sales teams to support lead generation activities
  • Manage marketing assets and materials inventory

 Requirements:

  • Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or related field
  • 1–3 years of marketing experience (fresh graduates with relevant internships may be considered)
  • Familiarity with digital marketing and social media platforms
  • Basic understanding of marketing analytics and reporting tools
  • Strong communication and organizational skills

All successful candidates can expect a competitive remuneration package, good career development and a comprehensive range of benefits. 

We regret only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

title: Facilities Engineer, Mechanical
raw_description:
About GlobalFoundries 

GlobalFoundries is a leading full-service semiconductor foundry providing a unique combination of design, development, and fabrication services to some of the world’s most inspired technology companies. With a global manufacturing footprint spanning three continents, GlobalFoundries makes possible the technologies and systems that transform industries and give customers the power to shape their markets. For more information, visit www.gf.com. 

As an Engineer-Facilities, you will deliver Facilities operation & engineering services, Chiller / Pump management services, and facilities equipment asset maintenance management to enable manufacturing operations. Typically performing one or more of the above tasks in the capacity of either an individual contributor:

Key Responsibilities.

  • Operates, monitors and maintains, troubleshoots, repairs and replaces facilities mechanical components and systems such as electrical equipment, HVAC (heating, ventilation and air conditioning), chemical, energy, ultra water & wastewater treatment
  • Hires and oversees outside contractors such as electricians, elevator repairmen, grounds maintenance workers, painters, plumbers and roofers
  • Maintains and secures facilities equipment asset
  • Develops and implements capital improvement projects, such as construction, remodeling, renovation, and maintenance of buildings and facilities, including preliminary concept development, detailed engineering design and bidding
  • Conducts periodic inspections of work in progress and advises construction supervisors and contractors on plans and specifications
  • Inspects existing facilities and prepares reports on the condition, deficiencies observed, designs necessary for repairs and estimated cost of repairs

Other Responsibilities

Perform all activities in a safe and responsible manner and support all Environmental, Health, Safety & Security requirements, and programs.

Required Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, or related field.
  • Around 3 years of experience in handling facilities on Mechanical Fronts.
  • Hands-on experience with Chiller and Pumps
  • Ability to handle Plumbers & Contractors onsite
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
  • Analytical mindset with ability to translate business needs into technical requirements.

GlobalFoundries is an equal opportunity employer, cultivating a diverse and inclusive workforce. We believe having a multicultural workplace enhances productivity, efficiency and innovation whilst our employees feel truly respected, valued and heard.

As an affirmative employer, all qualified applicants are considered for employment regardless of age, ethnicity, marital status, citizenship, race, religion, political affiliation, gender, sexual orientation and medical and/or physical abilities.

All offers of employment with GlobalFoundries are conditioned upon the successful completion of background checks, medical screenings as applicable and subject to the respective local laws and regulations.

Information about our benefits you can find here: https://gf.com/about-us/careers/opportunities-asia

title: Legal Associate
raw_description:
About the Role

We are expanding our Singapore legal team and are seeking a Legal Associate to support a group of Singapore-qualified lawyers advising on sophisticated and cross-border financial services and FinTech matters. This role offers hands-on exposure to FinTech advisory, licensing, and regulatory work, including matters relating to derivatives broking, payments and financial markets activities. The Singapore team is part of a broader regional and global group of legal and compliance professionals.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support Legal Counsels on a wide range of FinTech, payments and financial services legal matters.
  • Assist with licensing, regulatory advisory work, and contractual matters across multiple jurisdictions.
  • Work on legal and regulatory issues relating to derivatives broking, payments and financial markets.
  • Draft, review, and analyse legal documentation, policies, and regulatory submissions.
  • Conduct legal research and provide practical, solution-oriented support.
  • Work collaboratively with legal, compliance, and business teams as part of a wider function.

What We're Looking For

  • Bachelor's degree in Law from Singapore or other jurisdictions; bar qualification is a bonus.
  • Early-career lawyers or paralegals with approximately 2-3 years’ experience in financial services, capital markets, or regulatory practices; fresh graduates will also be considered.
  • Positive attitude, strong work ethic, and willingness to play a supportive role within a wider team.
  • Strong interest in FinTech, financial regulation, payments and derivatives.
  • Good legal research, drafting, and analytical skills.
  • Reliable, detail-oriented, and comfortable in a fast-paced environment.
  • Prior experience in a law firm or in-house financial services team is an advantage.

What We Offer

  • Join a high-calibre senior Singapore legal team within a wider regional and global legal & compliance group.
  • Exposure to complex, cross-border FinTech, payments and financial markets work.
  • Strong learning opportunities and career development.
  • Collaborative, professional team culture that values attitude and contribution.

title: Administration & Operations Executive - WCAN
raw_description:
Responsibilities:

  • Manage confidential student and assessment data in the student and learning management systems.
  • Prepare and coordinate all assessments, including quizzes, class tests, and exams, ensuring proper planning and documentation.
  • Liaise with faculty and module leaders to review and finalize assessment materials before administration.
  • Plan and allocate resources, including booking venues and assigning staff for assessment support.
  • Maintain accurate assessment schedules and ensure all materials are prepared for on-campus or online assessments.
  • Brief and coordinate staff to ensure smooth administration of assessments.
  • Oversee the conduct of assessments, ensuring procedures are followed and addressing any issues that arise.
  • Manage post-assessment processes, including tracking marking timelines, liaising with faculty for moderation, and updating student records.
  • Prepare and coordinate meetings with relevant boards or committees, ensuring all required documentation is complete.
  • Handle assessment-related administrative tasks, procurement, and finance matters according to timelines.
  • Support the launch of new programs by planning initial assessments and drafting operational workflows.
  • Liaise with internal and external stakeholders to establish clear processes and timelines for assessments.
  • Coordinate with outsourced partners to monitor and track operational processes.
  • Assign and deploy staff for online and on-campus assessment support.
  • Perform any other duties as assigned by the reporting officer.


Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in any field.
  • 1–2 years of relevant experience is a plus; entry-level candidates are welcome to apply.
  • Experience in examination management, academic operations, or a similar administrative environment is preferred.
  • Excellent organizational and multitasking abilities.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills to liaise effectively with various stakeholders.
  • Detail-oriented with the ability to maintain accurate records.
  • Capable of prioritizing workload and meeting deadlines.
  • Able to work under pressure during peak assessment periods.
  • Willing to work 1–2 hours beyond regular working hours on weekdays or Saturdays when requested.


We regret only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. All applications will be handled confidentially. By submitting your application, you agree to the collection, use, retention, and disclosure of your personal information to prospective employers.

title: Junior Investment Analyst Specialist
raw_description:
We are partnering with an Asset Management whom are seeking a Junior Alternative Investments Specialist to support the evaluation, execution, and ongoing monitoring of alternative investment opportunities. This role is ideal for candidates at an early stage of their career who are eager to gain hands-on exposure across private markets, including fund and investment lifecycle activities.

The successful candidate will work closely with senior investment professionals and management, supporting analysis, documentation, and coordination with both internal teams and external service providers.

Responsibilities:

  • Work closely with senior investment professionals on ad hoc projects and new initiatives
  • Perform cash flow modelling, IRR calculations, and financial modelling
  • Assist with the preparation of investment summaries, internal notes and presentation materials
  • Coordinate with external service providers such as fund administrators, custodians and auditors
  • Involve in the full fund cycle such as setting up, monitoring and closing of fund
  • Assist with preparation of regulatory documentation and compliance checks
  • Support documentation and follow up
  • Track follow-ups, scheduling meetings with investors

Requirements:

  • Bachelor's degree in Finance or Economics is preferred
  • Strong interest in alternative investment space such as Private Equity, Private Credit or Real Estate
  • Open to Fresh Grad with internship experience within the private alternative space
  • Prior exposure to financial modelling in a professional or internship setting is required

Simply click the 'Apply now" button to get started.

EA License No. 23S2077

EA Reg No.: R22104987

title: Accounts & Operations Assistant
raw_description:
Job Summary

The Accounts & Operations Assistant supports basic accounting tasks and daily operational activities to ensure smooth and efficient business operations. The role requires attention to detail, organizational skills, and the ability to work collaboratively across departments.

Responsibilities

  • Maintain accurate financial records, invoices, and payment documentation to support accounting processes
  • Process accounts payable and receivable transactions to ensure timely payments and collections
  • Prepare bank reconciliations and basic financial reports to support accounting accuracy
  • Organize and maintain filing systems for accounts and operational documents to ensure easy retrieval and compliance
  • Support internal and external audits by preparing necessary documentation and ensuring adherence to company policies
  • Assist with daily operational tasks and maintain related documentation to support smooth business functions
  • Communicate with vendors and follow up with internal teams to resolve operational and financial issues
  • Maintain inventory and procurement records to support supply chain and purchasing activities.

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree or diploma in Accounting, Finance, or Business Administration
  • 0–2 years of relevant experience (fresh graduates may apply)
  • Basic knowledge of accounting principles to perform core accounting tasks
  • Proficiency in MS Excel and accounting software to manage financial data efficiently
  • Strong organizational skills to manage multiple tasks and documentation
  • Effective communication skills to collaborate with team members and vendors
  • Willingness to learn and support multiple functions within the company

title: Financial Advisor (Insurance) - Flexi Work + Mentoring + Training provided
raw_description:
Flexi Work Arrangements, Essential Financial Knowledge, Attractive Remuneration

We believe in providing a holistic approach in service to our clients, ensuring their needs are met and satisfied in our care. And we believe that it starts with us!


Join our team as a Financial Advisor and enjoy a flexible work setting, comprehensive training, support structure, together with market leading remuneration. This role is ideal for both fresh graduates and those seeking a mid-career switch. Connect with us and embark on a rewarding career in financial advisory


A Day in The Life of a Financial Advisor:

  • Client Networking: Establish and grow a robust client base through effective networking and prospecting strategies.
  • Financial Strategy Development: Analyze clients' financial situations and goals to create tailored financial strategies.
  • Insurance and Financial Advisory: Provide comprehensive advisory on insurance and financial planning, covering areas such as retirement, education, and investments, supported by our extensive product suite.
  • Financial Analysis: Presenting detailed financial summaries, investment reports, and income projections for clients.
  • Client Relationship Management: Maintain and strengthen client relationships through regular reviews and adjustments to financial plans based on life changes.

Benefits & Rewards:

  • 24-months Trainee Program: Provides monthly allowance + commission, supporting you in the first 24 months of your career.
  • Extensive Training and Support Structure: Regular workshops, training & sharing sessions within the agency and individual mentorship ensuring support along the way.
  • Flexi-Hybrid Work Setting: Enjoy a hybrid work setting with flexible work arrangements.
  • Attractive Rewards: We believe in 'work hard, play hard', with incentives for top performers & fun team-bonding events.
  • Market-leading Remuneration Structure: Market-leading remuneration package along with an attractive incentive system, including commissions, performance incentives, and up to 3 incentive trips a year.

Qualifications:

  • Singaporean / Singapore PR preferred
  • Minimum 21 years old with Polytechnic Diploma, GCE 'A' level Certification or Bachelor Degree in any field, as per MAS requirements.
  • Highly motivated with strong interpersonal skills

Apply now to find out more!

title: Examination Invigilator (Undergraduate) (Contract for Services), RO
raw_description:
COMPANY DESCRIPTION

Singapore Management University is a place where high-level professionalism blends together with a healthy informality. The 'family-like' atmosphere among the SMU community fosters a culture where employees work, plan, organise and play together - building a strong collegiality and morale within the university.

Our commitment to attract and retain talent is ongoing. We offer attractive benefits and welfare, competitive compensation packages, and generous professional development opportunities - all to meet the work-life needs of our staff. No wonder, then, that SMU continues to be given numerous awards and recognition for its human resource excellence.

RESPONSIBILITIES

The role of the invigilator is to administer the examination and ensure that students abide by the rules and regulations set out for the examinations. The invigilator is required to:

  • Attend examination briefing prior to the examination period;
  • Mark attendance of students taking examinations;
  • Ensure students adhere to examination rules and the examination is in accordance with examination policy;
  • Record of any examination irregularities;
  • Distribute of examination scripts before the examination, collect and collate examination scripts for a proper handover to instructors after the examination;
  • Adhere to invigilate any examination session whenever the Registrar's Office has assigned.
  • ** Only selected candidates will be contacted. Must be available on 8 Apr (PM) for briefing and commit to work from 20 to 30 Apr 2026 (including Saturday).

Salary based on per invigilated examination.

Please include at least 1 job reference and 1 character reference.

QUALIFICATIONS
  • Edu. Qualification: min. O / A Levels, Diploma.
  • Language Proficiency: Able to speak & write good English.
  • Background: Applicants with prior invigilation experience preferred.
OTHER INFORMATION

Candidates who do not possess the stipulated qualifications but have relevant work experience may still apply. Remuneration and appointment terms shall commensurate with qualifications and experience. SMU reserves the right to modify the appointment terms where necessary.

Please note that your application will be sent to and reviewed by the direct employer - Singapore Management University

title: Project Engineer
raw_description:
Responsibilities:


The scope of Project Engineer shall include but not limited to the following.

• Manage the document review process, including collating comments from all reviewing parties and ensuring proper follow-up to the review comments;

• Manage and coordinate the design, installation, testing and commissioning activity, including coordination with all stakeholders (contractors, site owners, end users);

• Liaise with the contractors and stakeholders to monitor and update the programme and work schedule;

• Provide the assistance to the project team in terms of project delivery;

• Prepare project summary reports for progress reporting; and

• Manage the administrative activities of the project, including coordinating meetings, preparing meeting records, preparing the presentation material and drafting letters for issuance.



Qualifications & Requirements:

• Recognised degree in Engineering or related discipline;

• Relevant project management or project experience in the area of Automatic Fare Collection system in Singapore transit environment will be a plus;

• Proficient in Microsoft Office for Windows;

• Highly motivated and pro-active with the ability to work independently;

• Ability to work and collaborate with team;

• Able to cope with flexible working hour;

• Be expected to perform night works during engineering hours;

• Good analytical and problem-solving skills;

• Good written and verbal communication skills in English; and

• Be expected to travel regularly between office and sites.


EA License # 14C6941

title: Part-Time English Teacher
raw_description:
Job Summary

We are seeking a dedicated and enthusiastic Part-Time English Teacher to deliver engaging and effective lessons that support students’ academic progress and foster a positive learning environment.

Responsibilities

  • Deliver English lessons aligned with the centre’s curriculum and lesson plans to primary and/or secondary school students
  • Develop and prepare teaching materials that engage students and are appropriate for their age and learning level
  • Monitor and assess students’ learning progress, providing constructive feedback to enhance their language skills
  • Maintain classroom discipline by implementing strategies that promote a positive and focused learning environment
  • Communicate clearly and professionally with parents about students’ progress, attendance, and areas for improvement
  • Participate actively in internal meetings and training sessions to continuously improve teaching practices
  • Manage punctuality and attendance, notifying the centre in advance of any leave to ensure smooth scheduling

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Local or prominent university graduate in English teaching field or relevant fields
  • Strong communication (both spoken and written) and interpersonal skills
  • Required to work on weekends
  • Prior teaching or tutoring experience in English language instruction
  • Relevant qualifications such as English, Education, or TESOL certifications
  • Strong proficiency in spoken and written English
  • Patience, responsibility, and commitment to supporting students’ learning outcomes
  • Ability to commit reliably to a regular weekly teaching schedule

Employer questions

  • How would you rate your English language skills?
  • Describe your right to work in Singapore?
  • What's your expected monthly basic salary?
  • How many years of experience do you have as a Tutor?
  • Are you available to work on weekends or public holidays?

title: Supervisor
raw_description:
Electrical Supervisor Duties and Responsibilities

Electrical Supervisors play a pivotal role in ensuring the success and safety of electrical installations and maintenance projects.


They supervise electricians, coordinate operations, manage work schedules, and ensure compliance with safety regulations and electrical codes.

Their duties and responsibilities include:

  • Overseeing the installation, repair, and maintenance of electrical systems in buildings
  • Developing and implementing maintenance procedures, and maintaining maintenance records
  • Supervising, training, and mentoring electricians and electrical workers
  • Ensuring strict compliance with safety procedures and electrical codes
  • Planning and scheduling projects and preparing and maintaining project
  • Inspecting and testing electrical equipment, components and systems to identify hazards, defects, and the need for adjustment or repair
  • Resolving problems and ensuring that work is completed to clients’ satisfaction
  • Ordering electrical supplies and equipment
  • Preparing and presenting reports detailing the status of electrical projects and work ordersResponsibilities
  • Oversee and manage the electrical team’s operations
  • Ensure all electrical work meets quality and safety standards
  • Plan, schedule, and oversee the installation, maintenance, and repair of electrical systems
  • Develop and implement maintenance procedures and manage the emergency electrical services
  • Perform regular inspections and troubleshooting to detect faulty wiring or defective parts and arrange for necessary repairs
  • Provide training and guidance to the electrical team
  • Prepare and present reports on the status of electrical projectsnsure compliance with electrical codes and regulations
  • Coordinate with other departments to ensure smooth operations


Qualifications

  • Proven experience as an electrical supervisor or a similar role in the electrical field
  • Extensive knowledge of electrical systems, electrical repairs, components, and safety procedures
  • Familiarity with electrical system installation, troubleshooting, and maintenance
  • Strong understanding of electrical codes and regulations
  • Leadership skills and ability to manage and motivate a team
  • Excellent problem-solving skillsGood communication skills
  • Professional certification such as a diploma or a degree in Electrical Engineering or a related field

title: JUNIOR INTERIOR DESIGNER
raw_description:
We are seeking a Junior Interior Designer to join our team and contribute to the design team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in technical drafting, a keen eye for detail, and experience working on intricate designs. Fresh graduates are welcome to apply!


Job Responsibilities:

  • Ideate, conceptualize, and present precise 3D design proposals.
  • Interpret project briefs and translate ideas into accurate visual representations.
  • Develop detailed 3D models of interior spaces, including furniture, finishes, and built-in carpentry.
  • Prepare precise technical drawings for layouts, detailed plans, and construction references.
  • Collaborate with internal teams (designers, production, suppliers) to ensure high-quality project execution.
  • Attend creative review meetings to align design direction and progress.
  • Maintain and update the 3D rendering & material library for efficient workflow.
  • Keep abreast of new 3D tools, software, and interior design trends


Job requirement

  • Diploma/Degree in Interior Design, Architecture or equivalent.
  • Preferably minimum 2 years of relevant experience in an interior design or architectural firm.
  • Strong proficiency in AutoCAD is a must
  • Solid understanding of spatial planning, construction detailing, and materials.
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to deliver high-quality output consistently.
  • Excellent communication and teamwork skills.
  • Able to manage time independently and meet project deadlines.

Working arrangement:

  • 4-day work week
  • 10% profit sharing scheme on top of basic pay

title: Junior Fund Accountant
raw_description:
Company Description

Protege Fund Services is an independent Singapore based Fund Administrator. We serve a growing list of sophisticated client base ranging from individual private clients to large financial corporations.

We believe in building a long-term working relationship with our clients to create successful partnerships through customized and innovative solutions. Safeguarding our clients' privacy and confidentiality interests through robust operational processes and original solutions is our top priority.


Role Description

This is a full-time Junior Fund Accountant role located in Singapore. Some remote work is acceptable. As a Junior Fund Accountant, you will be responsible for the day-to-day fund accounting operations and delivery of Net-Asset-Value packs and reports to fund managers and investors.



Roles & Responsibilities

  • Reports to the Operations Manager
  • Responsibility includes supporting investor relations, fund accounting, and portfolio reporting activities
  • Assist with fund accounting entries of trades and cash transactions into fund accounting systems in compliance with accounting standards
  • Perform portfolio reconciliation with external parties
  • Support NAV financial closing and ensure delivery of NAV packs and reports to fund managers and investors
  • Support multiple asset classes and funds including both liquid and non-liquid strategies
  • Support fund setup process and various fund structures
  • Support in designing and implementing fund administration process automation initiatives to improve operational efficiencies and resiliency
  • Liaison with auditors to support annual audits for fund clients
  • Support fund related queries from fund managers


Qualifications

  • Good team player and possesses an excellent working attitude
  • Good knowledge of financial markets and financial products


Good knowledge of accounting and understanding of financial statements

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
  • Attention to detail and time management skills
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
  • Diploma in Accounting, Finance, or related field
  • Fresh graduate or candidates with no direct experience are welcomed to apply

title: Automation Engineer / Specialist (Python / Entry level / Banking Domain)
raw_description:
Executive Summary

Smart IMS Inc provides Digital technology & Cloud transformation services, Application & Infrastructure Management Services, Unified Communications, and Insurance implementation services to customers across the Americas, Europe, Middle East, and Asia-Pacific regions. As the trusted technology and business partner of leading MNCs, including Global Investment Banks, Smart IMS is also a Microsoft Gold Certified Partner, Oracle Platinum Partner and AWS MSP Partner.

We are looking to hire a motivated Automation Engineer / Specialist in Singapore to support our client, a top global investment bank. You will be responsible for developing, testing, and maintaining automation solutions to enhance efficiency, reduce manual efforts, and improve system performance.

Responsibilities:

  • Develop and implement automation using programming or scripting (such as Python) or other relevant technologies.
  • Collaborate with teams to identify automation opportunities in workflows, systems, and processes.
  • Design, test, and maintain automation frameworks for software, infrastructure, or operational tasks.
  • Troubleshoot and resolve automation-related issues to ensure seamless execution.
  • Document automation processes, best practices, and technical solutions for future reference.

Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Technology, or a related field.
  • Knowledge of any automation programming or scripting language (such as Python)
  • Past relevant internship or work experience in programming or scripting will be highly advantageous
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a passion for automation.
  • Ability to work independently and in a team-oriented environment.
  • Eagerness to learn and apply new technologies in automation and system optimization.

title: Accounts Assistant
raw_description:
ABOUT THE ROLE:

We are seeking a detail-oriented and motivated individual to join our Finance Department as an Accounts Assistant. Depending on your strengths and experience, you may be assigned to either the Accounts Payable team or the Bank Reconciliation team. Candidates who possess experience in both areas will be highly preferred.

In this role, you will support key financial operations such as processing invoices and payments, reconciling transactions, maintaining accurate records, and assisting in audit and month-end closing activities. You will work closely with internal departments and external partners to ensure the accuracy, timeliness, and compliance of financial data.

This position is ideal for individuals with a Diploma in Accountancy or a related field who are detail-oriented, organized, and eager to build a solid foundation in accounting. You should be able to manage multiple priorities effectively, work both independently and in a team, and demonstrate strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting systems.

ACCOUNTS ASSISTANT (ACCOUNTS PAYABLE TEAM)

Key Responsibilities:

  • Perform 3-way matching of supplier invoices against Purchase Orders (POs) and Goods Receipt Notes (GRNs) to ensure accuracy and compliance before payment processing.
  • Process supplier invoices and payments promptly according to the credit terms, ensuring that all supporting documents are complete and in accordance with company policies.
  • Communicate with suppliers and internal stakeholders to resolve discrepancies or queries related to invoices or payments.
  • Manage filing and documentation of AP records to support audit and compliance requirements.
  • Assist in the preparation of accounts payable schedules and month-end closing activities with GL accountant.
  • Support annual external audit and periodic internal reviews by providing relevant AP documentation and reconciliations.
  • Perform other ad-hoc duties as assigned by the AP Team Lead.

Key Traits and Skills:

  • Attention to detail and accuracy in data processing and document review.
  • Familiarity with 3-way matching concepts (PO, GRN, and Invoice) in a high-volume environment.
  • Strong Microsoft Excel and accounting software proficiency.
  • Working knowledge of Microsoft Business Central 365 is highly preferred.
  • ·Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a fast-paced environment.


Job Requirements:

  • Able to start immediately or within short notice period will be an added advantage.
  • Diploma in Accountancy / related- qualification.
  • Fresh graduates and entry-level candidates are welcome, especially those with relevant or strong working experience gained during their school years.
  • Good time management skills, ability to meet deadlines, and strong attention to detail. 
  • Good team player with the ability to work independently.


Note:

  • Our office will be shifted from Ang Mo Kio to Tampines in July 2026 / August 2026

title: Telemarketer (Inbound & outbound call)
raw_description:
KEY RESONSIBILITIES

  • Identify target audiences and plan marketing campaign goals.
  • Create key marketing messages, content, and materials for lead generation.
  • Support the creation of marketing collaterals, handle promo enquiries, and help design product bundles for different customer groups.
  • Set up and manage Telegram and WhatsApp communities for Group Buy and ID projects.
  • Plan and run community engagement events.
  • Make outbound and handle inbound calls to generate and follow up on leads.
  • Research competitors and analyse market trends.
  • Prepare reports and track campaign performance (basic Excel & Pivot Table skills).
  • Build strong working relationships with internal teams and external partners.
  • Monitor and respond to enquiries and feedback on social platforms (Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, Telegram, Email, Google Reviews).

QUALIFICATIONS AND REQUIREMENTS

  • Diploma in Marketing, Business, or equivalent (candidates with relevant skills and passion are welcome to apply even without formal experience).
  • Minimum 1 year of experience in digital marketing activities (fresh graduates or career switchers with strong interest in marketing and community engagement will also be considered).
  • Good working knowledge of social media platforms (Facebook, Instagram, Telegram, WhatsApp).
  • Enjoys engaging in conversations, building relationships, and proactively connecting with people.
  • Creative problem solver who loves to propose ideas and find practical solutions.
  • Experience in planning and organizing physical events is an advantage.
  • Highly organized, able to multi-task, meet deadlines, and perform under pressure.
  • Strong interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills.
  • Confident, driven, and dynamic — able to work well in a team and independently when required.


NOTE:
Working Hours:

  • Mon, Wed, Thurs, Fri, (full day in AMK office)
  • Sat & Sun (half day in Sungei Kaduk Megastore Gain City)
  • Public Holiday sometimes also need to work

title: Piano Teacher
raw_description:
Piano Teacher – Full-Time, Permanent

5-Day Work Week | 2 Days Off


Why Join LVL Music Academy?

  • Singapore’s first all-in-one music hub: music school, instrument shop, and in-house repair workshop under one roof
  • Specialising in string instruments (violin, cello & piano) for students of all ages
  • Supportive, inspiring, and professional environment where teachers and students thrive

About the Role

LVL Music Academy is seeking a passionate and experienced Piano Teacher to deliver high-quality music instruction and nurture students’ musical development. The ideal candidate will have strong organisational and teaching skills, deep product knowledge, and a love for music education, capable of promoting LVL’s services and programs effectively.


Your Role:

  • Deliver engaging piano lessons to students aged 3.5 years to adults, tailored to individual learning needs.
  • Plan, prepare, and execute structured lesson plans to ensure consistent progress
  • Follow LVL's cirriculum and guides (full training will be provided)
  • Teach music theory (ABRSM) and prepare students for examinations.
  • Organise and manage recitals and performance opportunities to showcase student achievements.
  • Maintain clear, proactive communication with students and parents to support learning outcomes.
  • Collaborate with fellow teaching staff to enhance curriculum and student experience.

What We’re Looking For:

  • Minimum Diploma in Music, with a degree preferred.
  • At least 2 years of teaching experience, with a proven track record in piano instruction.
  • Proficient in teaching both practical piano skills and music theory to students of all ages.
  • Strong organisational skills with the ability to plan and oversee recitals.
  • Collaborative, positive, and passionate about nurturing students’ musical journeys.

Perks and Packages

  • Base Salary: $4,200 – $5,000 per month
  • Medical and Insurance Coverage (Hospital & Surgical)
  • 14 Days Annual Leave & Sick Leave
  • In-House Learning, Development, and Training
  • Professional Career Development Opportunities
  • Complimentary Music Lessons
  • Vibrant and Supportive Company Culture
  • Opportunity to travel overseas with the team for annual performances
  • Performance incentives and continuous training opportunities

About LVL Music Academy

Founded in 2013, LVL is Singapore’s leading music school and instrument shop, specialising in string instruments (violin, cello & piano). We are the first music hub in Singapore to combine a music school, instrument shop, and in-house repair workshop under the same roof, providing students, parents, and musicians with a truly comprehensive music experience.

Ready to Inspire the Next Generation of Musicians?
Submit your resume and cover letter now!

title: CHEF
raw_description:
Roles & Responsibilities

You will be an integral part of the kitchen leadership team, working closely with the Head Chef or Executive Chef to ensure the highest quality of food preparation and service. You will assist in menu planning, supervising kitchen staff, and maintaining kitchen operations to uphold the restaurant's standards of excellence. we are always looking for new ideas and creations to be added into our menu to create a different environment, whereby we are able to cater to everyone's taste buds.

Key Responsibilities:

· Assist in menu planning, recipe development, and ensuring consistent food quality.

· Oversee food preparation, including cooking, plating, and presentation.

· Train, mentor, and supervise kitchen staff, ensuring they follow recipes and best practices.

· Collaborate with the Head Chef or Executive Chef to create daily specials and seasonal menus.

· Manage inventory, order supplies, and maintain stock levels to minimize waste and control costs.

· Monitor kitchen equipment for functionality and coordinate maintenance and repairs as needed.

· Maintain kitchen hygiene and cleanliness according to health and safety standards.

· Enforce food safety and sanitation practices to ensure the safety of both staff and customers.

· Assist in scheduling kitchen staff and managing labour costs.

· Participate in hiring and onboarding new kitchen team members.

· Collaborate with the front-of-house team to ensure efficient service and address customer feedback.

Qualifications:

  • Culinary degree or relevant culinary certification is preferred.
  • Previous experience as a Sous Chef or in a similar leadership role in restaurant kitchen.
  • Strong knowledge of culinary techniques, food safety and sanitation.
  • Proficiency in menu planning, recipe development and food costing.
  • Leadership and team management skills, with the ability to motivate and mentor kitchen staff.
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Able to work on weekends and Public holidays when required.

raw_description:
Our client is a Marine and Offshore Industry. Due to business needs, they are now recruiting a PLC Control Engineer to be part of their Team for ongoing transformation projects. They are located in the West - easily accessible.

Responsibilities:

  • Design and develop green marine solutions aligned with the company’s sustainability and business objectives
  • Set up and manage test laboratories to facilitate development, experimentation, and validation of electrical propulsion systems.
  • Develop PLC software and algorithms for advanced marine electrical propulsion solutions.

title: IT Operations and Business Analyst
raw_description:
IT Operation & Business analyst - Work Location: Changi

Role Overview

We are seeking an IT-savvy professional to support the day-to-day operations and continuous improvement of existing digital systems. This role involves managing assigned workstreams, working directly with stakeholders and vendors, and delivering small to medium initiatives end-to-end.

Key Responsibilities

  • Support day-to-day operations of enterprise digital and IT systems, including issue tracking, follow-ups, and resolution.

  • Manage assigned projects and change requests from requirements through delivery, including tracking timelines, actions, and dependencies.

  • Engage business users to gather and document functional requirements and translate them into clear specifications for IT teams or external vendors.

  • Coordinate with external vendors across the delivery lifecycle, including development, user acceptance testing (UAT), deployment, issue resolution, and escalation.

  • Support UAT planning and execution and coordinate rollout activities.

  • Maintain project documentation, meeting notes, status updates, and reports.

  • Support procurement activities, including quotations, vendor coordination, and contract administration.

  • Organise and support project meetings, training sessions, and workshops, including preparation of presentation materials where required.

  • Provide after-hours system support when required, based on operational needs.

Requirements

  • Diploma or degree in Information Systems, Business IT, or a related discipline.

  • Experience in IT business analysis, IT operations, or digital project delivery.

  • Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities.

  • Experience working with CRM or enterprise digital platforms; Salesforce experience is an advantage.

  • Ability to work effectively with business users, internal teams, and external vendors.

  • Strong communication, organisational, and follow-through skills.

    Next Step

    • Simply click on 'Apply here' or email to tamanna.bilandi@adecco.com to drop your resume.
    • All shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

    Tamanna Bilandi

    EA Licence No. 91C2918

    Personnel Registration No. R2096241

title: Teacher (Part / Full - Time)
raw_description:
Company Overview / Employee Value Proposition

Welcome on board the Happy Train, where the journey of learning is always fun and exciting! The Happy Train programme is a Holistic brain-training programme that enriches children’s growing minds through a variety of games, guided play and hands-on activities. The curriculum is designed to motivate the little learner’s aspiration of achievement. Our hand-made teaching materials encourage children to explore concepts and ideas through experiential learning. We believe in the importance of developing core skills and shaping children’s learning attitudes. We are the developer of the unique multi-lingual brain development programme for children aged 3 months to 6 years old. The team of developers is a team of vastly experienced teachers, with expertise in early childhood education, right-brain training, speech training, linguistics and music education. Our teachers are intensively trained to know the fundamentals of the programme and understand the developmental needs of children. The lessons are always conducted in an enjoyable manner so children can learn well in a positive environment.

Job Summary

Since 2008, Happy Train has enriched children's learning through a unique bilingual right brain training programme. As a teacher, you will deliver engaging lessons, monitor developmental progress, and foster positive relationships with children and parents, contributing to their holistic growth.

Responsibilities

  • Deliver lessons aligned with the Happy Train brain development curriculum, policies, and framework to facilitate children’s cognitive and language development
  • Prepare and organize teaching materials and learning aids to support experiential and multi-sensory learning activities
  • Monitor and evaluate children’s progress regularly to tailor instruction and provide feedback for continuous improvement
  • Establish and maintain positive rapport with children and parents to encourage a supportive learning environment and effective communication

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Demonstrate interpersonal, communication, and presentation skills to engage children and collaborate with parents effectively
  • Exhibit passion and commitment to nurturing young children’s development and learning attitudes
  • Possess proficiency in English to communicate clearly with children and parents, supporting bilingual programme delivery
  • Hold a Diploma or Degree in any major; fresh graduates are encouraged to apply
  • Availability to work a 5-day week from Wednesday to Sunday or a part-time 3-day week from Friday to Sunday, including weekends

Other Information

  • We provide specialised paid training to selected candidates to equip them with the skills needed for the programme
  • Attractive remuneration package with guaranteed bonus commensurate with experience and qualifications
  • Opportunities for career growth as we expand locally, including grooming teachers to manage their own centres
  • Interested applicants should send an email to enquiry@happytrain.com.sg with the header: Job Application
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be notified for interviews

title: Data Engineer
raw_description:
Our Company

Gentco Logistics is a leading international liquid chemical logistics provider with three core business units: chemical tankers (5th largest fleet globally), chemical tank containers (2nd largest fleet globally), and terminal storage tanks. Our global headquarters are in Shanghai, China, supported by a regional headquarters in Singapore and 40 operational offices across six continents. Gentco Logistics is wholly owned by the Junzheng Group (ticker 601216.SH).

The Opportunity

The Data Engineer position is a part of the Global IT Department, which is directly overseen by the Group’s CIO. Our team currently comprises over 40 IT, software, and data professionals primarily based in Shanghai, Moerdijk (the Netherlands), and Singapore. With our IT presence in Singapore rapidly expanding, we are excited to invite you to join our team!

Job Responsibilities:

·       Assist the senior data engineer in the implementation and maintenance of critical pieces of our Databricks ELT pipelines and data warehouse using Azure Databricks (PySpark, Spark SQL)

·       Assist in the design and architecting of ELT pipelines, data schemas/models, and data marts for business users.

·     Participate in code reviews and contribute to CI/CD pipelines for automated testing and deployment of data solutions.

·       Occasionally assist data analysts in creating new semantic models and/or dashboards in PowreBI

·       Ensure the reliability and availability of core data infrastructure with proper monitoring and alerting systems to guarantee continuous data delivery to the business.

·       Take ownership of data quality and governance. Collaborate with business and IT teams to establish and meet data standards, including developing automated data checks and data quality monitoring dashboards.

·       Promote a culture of organizational learning by facilitating knowledge sharing and implementing best practices in documentation.

·       Stay updated with current industry best practices and trends, and evaluate technical decisions at a business level.

Requirements:

·       Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science or a related engineering field from a technical university.

·       Experience in data engineering with working knowledge of Python and SQL.

·       Experience with Agile/DevOps tools (Git, Jira).

·       Deeply knowledgeable of data warehousing best practices, especially in the Azure cloud environment, with a strong commitment to staying current with industry trends and future advancements.

·       Highly motivated, with a proactive approach to project ownership and accountability for outcomes and timelines. Committed to continuous personal growth and development within the organization.

·   Fluency in both written and spoken English and Mandarin is highly desirable to effectively liaise with Chinese-speaking clients and counterparts, understanding their technical requirements.

Desirable skills 

·       Experience with public cloud infrastructure. (Azure preferred)

·       Exposure to Databricks, Snowflake, or similar big data processing platforms.

·       Background in logistics, shipping/maritime or chemicals industry.

·       Experience with Power BI or similar BI tools.
